30/04/2026, 00:40                                                                             Whitepaper Critical Analysis

            The equation of motion from varying β is
                                                                      1
                                                                  Dα + {α, α} = 0.
                                                                      2
                                                                                          ​




            So α is a flat Hamiltonian connection/deformation field. The β -field is the cotangent
            multiplier imposing flatness.
            The local observables of this closed BF theory are continuous Chevalley-Eilenberg
            cochains:
                                                           ∙                 ∨
                                            Obscl
                                               closed,H ≃ CCE,cont (g) = S (gcont [−1]).

            5. Why divergence-free polyvectors reduce to
            Hamiltonians
            The paper motivates the Hamiltonian sector as a holomorphic-symplectic analogue of
            Kodaira-Spencer/BCOV theory.
            On C2, polyvectors are
                                                     P V ∙,∙ (C2 ) ≃ Ω0,∙ (C2 )[∂z1 , ∂z2 ].                                                              ​
                                                                                                                                                                  ​

                                                                                                                                                                          ​
                                                                                                                                                                              ​




            The holomorphic volume form
                                                                              Ω = dz1 ∧ dz2                       ​                   ​




            defines a divergence operator
                                                              ∂Ω : P V i → P V i−1 .
                                                                          ​




            For a vector field
                                                                  X = a 1 ∂z 1 + a 2 ∂z 2 ,
                                                                                  ​


                                                                                                      ​
                                                                                                          ​                   ​


                                                                                                                                                  ​
                                                                                                                                                      ​




            one has
                                                             ∂Ω X = ∂z 1 a 1 + ∂z 2 a 2 .
                                                                      ​

                                                                                              ​
                                                                                                  ​                       ​

                                                                                                                                          ​
                                                                                                                                              ​               ​




            Contracting with the symplectic form gives
                                                             ιX ω = a1 dz2 − a2 dz1 .
                                                                  ​                   ​                               ​           ​                                   ​




https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa84c-a760-83ea-ae70-183a06ecd2d3                                                                                                                                   42/510
30/04/2026, 00:40                                                                                           Whitepaper Critical Analysis

            The equation
                                                                           ∂Ω X = 0         ​




            is exactly
                                                                          d(ιX ω ) = 0.             ​




            On a formal disk every closed holomorphic one-form is exact, so
                                                                          ιX ω = −df
                                                                            ​




            for some f , unique modulo constants. Therefore
                                                                           X = Xf .                                      ​




            So divergence-free vector fields are Hamiltonian vector fields, and Hamiltonians are
            defined modulo constants. This justifies replacing the divergence-free polyvector-one
            sector by
                                                                     Aˉ = C[z1 , z2 ]/C.                        ​                    ​




            6. The open brane sector from first principles
            The brane is a product of two simple local objects:
             1. an exact topological A-brane along R ⊂ T ∗R;
             2. a B -brane skyscraper sheaf O0 at 0 ∈ C2.             ​




            The open string states are
                                                Ω∙ (R) ⊗ Ext∙C[z1 ,z2 ] (O0 , O0 ) ⊗ glN [1].
                                                                                        ​               ​
                                                                                                            ​                    ​               ​           ​




            The Ext algebra is computed from the Koszul resolution of
                                                             k = C[z1 , z2 ]/(z1 , z2 ).        ​                   ​                    ​           ​




            The resolution is
                                             0 → Re12 → Re1 ⊕ Re2 → R → k → 0,
                                                             ​                      ​                                        ​




            with
                                                         d(e1 ) = z1 ,
                                                                 ​              ​                                       d(e2 ) = z2 ,        ​           ​




https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa84c-a760-83ea-ae70-183a06ecd2d3                                                                                                       43/510
30/04/2026, 00:40                                                                                             Whitepaper Critical Analysis

                                                              d(e12 ) = z2 e1 − z1 e2 .
                                                                                ​                              ​                ​                           ​           ​




            Applying HomR (−, k) kills the differentials because zi act by zero on k. Hence
                                     ​                                                                                                                                          ​




                                                             Ext∙R (k, k) ≃ Λ(ε1 , ε2 ),
                                                                        ​                                                                               ​                   ​




            with
                                                                                                ∣εi ∣ = 1.
                                                                                                      ​




            This is the exterior algebra on the two derived normal directions.
            The open BV field is
                                                     α ∈ Ω∙ (R) ⊗ Λ(ε1 , ε2 ) ⊗ glN [1].                                    ​               ​                                                   ​




            The formal Chern-Simons action is
                                                                     1           1
                                              Sopen = ∫                Tr(α dα) + Tr(α3 ).
                                                              R×C0∣2 2           3
                                                       ​                                ​        ​                                                                                          ​




            The Berezin integral over C0∣2 picks out the coefficient of
                                                                                                     ε1 ε2 .
                                                                                                          ​             ​




            The ghost-zero part of the open field is
                                                             α0 = A + ϕ 1 ε 1 + ϕ 2 ε 2 ,
                                                                ​                                                  ​                    ​                           ​                   ​




            where
                                                                A ∈ Ω1 (R) ⊗ glN ,                                                                              ​




                                                             ϕ1 , ϕ2 ∈ Ω0 (R) ⊗ glN .
                                                                    ​               ​                                                                                               ​




            The quadratic term gives, up to a boundary term,
                                                                        ∫ Tr(ϕ1 dϕ2 ).      ​                          ​                        ​

            Therefore the ghost-zero open action is

            This is a first-order constrained matrix mechanics.                                                                                                               main



            7. The Dirac interpretation of the open action
            The term
                                                                                  ∫ Tr(ϕ1 dϕ2 )                                   ​                   ​




            means that ϕ1 and ϕ2 are canonically conjugate matrix coordinates. The symplectic
            form is
                                  ​           ​




                                                                 ΩN = Tr(dϕ1 ∧ dϕ2 ).
                                                                              ​                                                       ​                                   ​




            Thus
                                                                 {(ϕ1 )ij , (ϕ2 )kl } = δli δjk ,
                                                                                  ​               ​               ​           ​                               ​                   ​




            and
                                           {(ϕ1 )ij , (ϕ1 )kl } = 0,
                                                   ​   ​         ​    ​                                                               {(ϕ2 )ij , (ϕ2 )kl } = 0.
                                                                                                                                                          ​           ​                   ​   ​




            The field A is a Lagrange multiplier. Its equation of motion is
                                                                                          [ϕ1 , ϕ2 ] = 0.
                                                                                                      ​               ​




            This is the moment-map equation for simultaneous conjugation by GLN .                                                                                                                         ​




            For ϵ ∈ glN , the moment map component is
                              ​




                                                                     μϵ = − Tr(ϵ[ϕ1 , ϕ2 ]).
                                                                          ​                                                                   ​                   ​




            Its Hamiltonian flow is
                                                                                  δϵ ϕi = [ϵ, ϕi ].
                                                                                              ​               ​                                   ​




            So the open phase space is the Dirac reduction
                                                                     {[ϕ1 , ϕ2 ] = 0}/GLN .
                                                                                      ​                   ​                                                                   ​




            In BV language, the constraint is imposed by introducing the antifield
https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa84c-a760-83ea-ae70-183a06ecd2d3                                                                                                                                                    45/510
30/04/2026, 00:40                                                                                    Whitepaper Critical Analysis

                                                                                        ψ = A∗

            with differential
                                                                             Qψ = [ϕ1 , ϕ2 ],                                ​           ​




                                                                        Qϕ1 = Qϕ2 = 0.
                                                                                ​                                                ​




            So the reduced pointwise open BV algebra is
                                                    RN = C[(ϕ1 )ij , (ϕ2 )ij ] ⊗ Λ((ψ)ij ),
                                                             ​                      ​        ​                           ​           ​                 ​




            with
                                                                             Qψ = [ϕ1 , ϕ2 ].                                ​           ​




            This is exactly Dirac reduction written homologically.

            8. Local operators and the de Rham jet contraction
            Because the brane direction is topological, positive jets along the brane line are killed in
            cohomology.
            On the jet algebra, for a coordinate t along the brane,
                                                                             [dR , ι∂t ] = ∂t .
                                                                                        ​


                                                                                                     ​
                                                                                                         ​                               ​




            This gives an Euler-type contraction of positive jet degree. Therefore every local
            cohomology class has a representative depending only on field values at the insertion
            point:
                                                                 ϕ 1 (t 0 ),
                                                                    ​    ​                  ϕ 2 (t 0 ),
                                                                                                 ​                   ​                       ψ(t0 ),
                                                                                                                                                  ​




            not on derivatives.
            That is why the local open operator calculation reduces to the finite-dimensional
            invariant BV algebra
                                                                                 GLN
                                                                               (RN   , Q).                   ​




                                                                                                                 ​




            9. Stable large-N trace invariants
https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa84c-a760-83ea-ae70-183a06ecd2d3                                                                                                 46/510
30/04/2026, 00:40                                                             Whitepaper Critical Analysis

            At finite N , invariant functions of two matrices are generated by traces of words
                                                                 Tr(w(ϕ1 , ϕ2 )),​     ​




            but there are finite-N trace identities.
            In the degreewise stable limit
                                                                          N → ∞,

            Procesi-Razmyslov invariant theory says the invariant algebra becomes freely generated
            by cyclic trace words.
            Let
                                                                 F = C⟨x, y⟩,

            and
                                                              Cyc(F ) = F /[F , F ]

            be cyclic words. Then the stable invariant algebra is
                                                   C[gl2∞ ]GL
                                                           st
                                                              ∞
                                                                ≃ S(Cyc(F )nonempty ).
                                                                  ​




                                                                      ​                                  ​




            The empty trace
                                                                          Tr(1) = N

            is not a primitive stable generator.
            When the odd matrix ψ is included, marked cyclic words obey graded cyclicity. Moving
            one ψ around the trace gives no sign if it passes even letters, but two ψ’s anticommute.

            10. Adjacent swaps become Q-exact
            The differential is
                                                             Qψ = [x, y] = xy − yx.

            Therefore
                                                    Q Tr(ψvu) = Tr((xy − yx)vu).

https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa84c-a760-83ea-ae70-183a06ecd2d3                                                   47/510
30/04/2026, 00:40                                                                          Whitepaper Critical Analysis

            By cyclicity,
                                                Q Tr(ψvu) = Tr(uxyv) − Tr(uyxv).

            So
                                                             Tr(uxyv) − Tr(uyxv)

            is Q-exact.
            Adjacent swaps of x and y are exact. Hence in cohomology, any trace word with k
            copies of x and l copies of y becomes the same class as
                                                                               Tr(xk y l ).

            Thus the ghost-zero connected stable trace classes are labelled by nonconstant
            commutative monomials
                                                             z1k z2l ,
                                                                   ​       ​               k + l > 0.

            The boundary Hamiltonian corresponding to f ∈ Aˉ is
                                                                  Of = Tr f (ϕ1 , ϕ2 ).
                                                                       ​                           ​     ​




            This is well-defined in cohomology, independent of ordering.

            11. The one-ψ descendants
            For each pair
                                                                                k, l ≥ 0,

            define the first descendant
                                                        Ψk,l = ∑ Tr(ψ w(x, y)),
                                                              ​                        ​




                                                                       w∈Wk,l      ​




            where Wk,l is the set of words with k x’s and l y’s.
                             ​




            The paper proves
                                                                               QΨk,l = 0.   ​




            Indeed,
https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa84c-a760-83ea-ae70-183a06ecd2d3                                                                48/510
30/04/2026, 00:40                                                                                  Whitepaper Critical Analysis

                                                     QΨk,l = ∑ Tr((xy − yx)w).
                                                                   ​                       ​




                                                                              w∈Wk,l   ​




            Grouping by cyclic words of multidegree (k + 1, l + 1), the number of cyclic xy-
            transitions equals the number of cyclic yx-transitions. Hence the sum cancels.
            The primitive one-ψ homology in bidegree (k, l) is one-dimensional, generated by Ψk,l .
            The proof uses a finite combinatorial complex:
                                                                                                                                  ​




                                                     C2 (k, l) → C1 (k, l) → C0 (k, l).
                                                             ​                         ​                               ​




            Here
                                                                       C1 (k, l) = C{Wk,l }
                                                                          ​                                        ​




            is spanned by one-ψ words.
            The target
                                                                                   C0 (k, l)   ​




            is spanned by cyclic words with k + 1 x’s and l + 1 y’s.
            The boundary sends
                                                                 w ↦ [xyw]cyc − [yxw]cyc .           ​                     ​




            The two-ψ source supplies square relations. The resulting cell complex is interpreted as
            a chip-moving state complex on a circle. The Abel map sends a chip configuration to the
            sum of chip positions on a circle. Its fibers are convex polytopes, so the complex has the
            homology of S 1. Therefore the first homology is one-dimensional.
            The nonconstant one-ψ descendants are
                                                                       Ψk,l ,  ​                   k + l > 0.

            The scalar descendant
                                                                              Tr(ψ) = Ψ0,0                     ​




            exists but is excluded from the Hamiltonian comparison because constants were
            removed from Aˉ.
            Thus the connected stable Hamiltonian open sector is

            The word “sector” matters. Higher-ψ primitive homology is not included in this

            14. The scalar central subtlety
            Before quotienting constants, the simplest bracket is
                                                               {Tr ϕ1 , Tr ϕ2 } = N .
                                                                              ​            ​




            But in the reduced Hamiltonian algebra,
                                                            {z1 , z2 } = 1 ≡ 0
                                                               ​      ​                                mod C.

            So the formula
                                                                   {Of , Og } = O{f ,g}Aˉ
                                                                          ​       ​


                                                                                                          ​
                                                                                                              ​




            is true only after projecting out the scalar trace class.
            This is not a minor convention. It is the scalar Hamiltonian anomaly or center-of-mass
            central term. It reappears quantum mechanically as a Capelli/normal-ordering shift.
            So the corrected statement is:
             {Of , Og }open = O{f ,g}Aˉ
                     ​     ​       ​


                                                    ​
                                                        ​   in the connected Hamiltonian quotient by scalar trace clas

            Without that quotient, the formula fails for f = z1, g = z2.                           ​              ​




            15. The CE-source obstruction
            The paper observes that the map
                                                                              f ↦ Of           ​




            is not a map from closed CE cochains in the naive way.
            The closed observable algebra is
                                                                               ∙
                                                                              CCE (g).​




https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa84c-a760-83ea-ae70-183a06ecd2d3                                                            52/510
30/04/2026, 00:40                                                                         Whitepaper Critical Analysis

            A Hamiltonian mode
                                                                                  f ∈h

            is not itself a CE cochain. The CE coordinate dual to f is a length-one cochain, but it is
            not closed.
            Indeed, Aˉ is perfect as a Lie algebra:
                                                                         Aˉ = {Aˉ, Aˉ}.

            For every monomial,
                                                                                           1 a b+1
                                                         z1a z2b = {z1 ,                     z z }
                                                                                          b+1 1 2
                                                             ​   ​                    ​            ​    ​      ​




            before quotienting constants. Hence every nonconstant Hamiltonian is a bracket.
            If ξa,b is the CE coordinate dual to z1az2b , then
                    ​                                                ​    ​




                                                                         dCE ξa,b =
                                                                                   0.
                                                                                  ​         ​




            In fact, it evaluates nontrivially on the pair whose bracket gives z1az2b . No higher CE-
            length term can cancel the length-two component of dξa,b . Therefore no CE cocycle has
                                                                                                                         ​       ​




            linear Hamiltonian part ξa,b .
                                                                                                                   ​




                                                     ​




            So the naive idea
                                      closed CE coordinate dual to f ⟼ Tr f (ϕ1 , ϕ2 )                                       ​       ​




            is wrong.
            The correction is the derived center construction.

            17. Why principal parts are necessary
            Now we come to the principal-parts note.
            The continuous dual of
                                                                       h = C[[z1 , z2 ]]/C        ​                   ​




            is not best thought of as abstract symbols δa,b . Geometrically, it is realized by principal
            parts of meromorphic top forms.
                                                                                                              ​




            Define
                                 ρa,b = z1−a−1 z2−b−1 dz1 ∧ dz2 ,
                                       ​                ​      ​           ​              ​                                           a, b ≥ 0,                            a + b > 0.

            Let
                                                                       P = ⨁ C ρa,b .                     ​                                    ​




                                                                                    a,b≥0
                                                                                    a+b>0                 ​




            The residue pairing is
                                 ⟨ρa,b , z1m z2n ⟩ = Res0 (z1m−a−1 z2n−b−1 dz1 dz2 ) = δa,m δb,n .
                                               ​    ​                  ​                      ​                                            ​           ​       ​                ​   ​




            Equivalently, integrating over a small torus ∣z1∣ = ϵ1, ∣z2∣ = ϵ2,                        ​                                ​           ​                   ​




                                                          1
                                                              2
                                                                ∫ ∫ ρa,b z1m z2n = δa,m δb,n .
                                                        (2πi)
                                                                   ​                  ​                           ​           ​                            ​       ​




https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa84c-a760-83ea-ae70-183a06ecd2d3                                                                                                                                  56/510
30/04/2026, 00:40                                                                          Whitepaper Critical Analysis

            Thus P identifies with
                                                                                h∨cont .       ​




            The exclusion
                                                                              a+b>0

            removes
                                                                   ρ0,0 = z1−1 z2−1 dz1 dz2 ,
                                                                          ​        ​                       ​                   ​       ​




            which pairs with the constant 1. Since constants were removed from h, their dual must
            also be removed.
            So:
                                                                              P ≃ h∨cont                           ​       ​




            as the correct geometric dual, realized by brane-localized residue currents.

            18. Principal parts carry the coadjoint action
            The Hamiltonian action on principal parts is
                                                                         f ⋅ ρ = πpp {f , ρ},          ​




            where πpp projects a Laurent two-form to its principal-part component.
                            ​




            For
                                                                                             p q
                                                                              f = z1 z2 ,          ​           ​




            and
                                                                 ρa,b = z1−a−1 z2−b−1 dz1 dz2 ,
                                                                     ​                 ​                               ​           ​       ​




            one computes
                                         z1p z2q ⋅ ρa,b = −(pb − qa + p − q)ρa−p+1,b−q+1 ,
                                             ​   ​           ​                                                                                 ​




            with negative-index targets set to zero.
https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa84c-a760-83ea-ae70-183a06ecd2d3                                                                                         57/510
30/04/2026, 00:40                                                                                         Whitepaper Critical Analysis

            This is exactly the coadjoint action because residue integration by parts gives
                                                                    ⟨f ⋅ ρ, g⟩ = −⟨ρ, {f , g}⟩.

            That is the defining property of the coadjoint representation.
            For the linear Hamiltonians, the corrected formulas are
                                                                    z1 ⋅ ρa,b = −(b + 1)ρa,b+1 ,
                                                                      ​                   ​                                          ​




                                                                    z2 ⋅ ρa,b = (a + 1)ρa+1,b .
                                                                          ​                   ​                                  ​




            So linear Hamiltonians act as raising operators on principal parts.
            This is important because the copied principal-parts note contains a sentence saying z1
            lowers indices on principal parts. That sentence is incorrect. The formula in the paper is
                                                                                                                                                 ​




            the correct one.

            19. Why polynomial descendants are not the coadjoint
            module
            The open one-ψ descendants Ψa,b have the stable primitive action      ​




                                               {Op,q , Ψa,b } = (pb − qa) Ψa+p−1,b+q−1 ,
                                                            ​                 ​                                                          ​




            where Ψa,b is the averaged version of Ψa,b .
                             ​                                                                        ​




            The closed coadjoint action is
                                         ad∨z1p z2q δa,b = −(pb − qa + p − q)δa−p+1,b−q+1 .
                                                ​   ​
                                                        ​       ​                                                                            ​




            These are not the same.
            For example,
                                                                                                      O1,0    ​




            acts on polynomial descendants by
                                                                                      Ψa,b ↦ b Ψa,b−1 .
                                                                                                  ​                     ​




            But z1 acts coadjointly on principal parts by
                     ​




                                                                     ρa,b ↦ −(b + 1)ρa,b+1 .
                                                                                      ​                                      ​




https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa84c-a760-83ea-ae70-183a06ecd2d3                                                                                           58/510
30/04/2026, 00:40                                                                                 Whitepaper Critical Analysis

            The direction is opposite.
            Therefore:
             Ψa,b identify with h∨ only as graded vector-space labels, not as the coadjoint modul
                    ​




            The polynomial descendants are PBW-special-fibre representatives. They are not the
            deformation-level realization of the cotangent BF module.
            The deformation-level realization is the principal-part module P .

            20. No ordinary polynomial boundary realization of
            principal parts
            The paper gives a simple obstruction.
            The linear Hamiltonians
                                                     O1,0 = Tr ϕ1 ,
                                                              ​                               ​        O0,1 = Tr ϕ2   ​               ​




            act on ordinary polynomial boundary observables by constant derivations:
                                                                          ∂ϕ 2 ,  ​
                                                                                      ​
                                                                                                     −∂ϕ1 .   ​
                                                                                                                  ​




            On any ordinary polynomial, such derivations are locally nilpotent. Apply them enough
            times and the polynomial becomes zero.
            But on principal parts,
                                                        O1,0 ⋅ ρa,b = −(b + 1)ρa,b+1 ,
                                                                  ​           ​                                                   ​




                                                             O0,1 ⋅ ρa,b = (a + 1)ρa+1,b .
                                                                      ​                   ​                                   ​




            These actions are not locally nilpotent. They raise the pole order forever.
            Local nilpotence is preserved under module isomorphism. Therefore no collection of
            ordinary polynomial boundary observables can realize the principal-part coadjoint
            module.
            That is the core mathematical reason the polynomial Ψa,b cannot be the true cotangent
            boundary field.

            22. The Dirac interpretation of principal parts
            This is the most important physical idea in the principal-parts note.
            In ordinary mechanics, position and momentum are not the same kind of object.
            Position can be multiplication by x. Momentum is
                                                                                     d
                                                                       −iℏ             .
                                                                                       ​




                                                                                    dx
            It is a derivation. It naturally acts on distributions; for example,
                                                                      d
                                                                        δ(x − x0 )
                                                                        ​                          ​




                                                                     dx
            is another distribution.
            Trying to realize momentum as an ordinary function of position is the wrong category.
            The same happens here.
            Hamiltonians
                                                                       f (z1 , z2 )
                                                                                ​          ​




            are holomorphic functions. Their duals are not another family of holomorphic functions.
            Their duals are residue currents:
                                                             ρa,b = z1−a−1 z2−b−1 dz1 dz2 .
                                                                 ​          ​                  ​       ​   ​




https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa84c-a760-83ea-ae70-183a06ecd2d3                                                         61/510
30/04/2026, 00:40                                                                    Whitepaper Critical Analysis

            They are holomorphic analogues of derivatives of delta-functions supported at the
            brane.
            That is why the brane cotangent sector must be distributional in the transverse
            holomorphic directions. A brane is a defect; defect-localized dual fields are naturally
            delta-currents normal to the defect.
            So the corrected physical slogan is:
             Hamiltonian modes are functions; cotangent modes are brane-supported residue cu



            23. The Witten/open-closed interpretation

            becomes a central operation on the whole open algebra.
            The degree-zero shadow is
                                                             f ↦ Of = Tr f (ϕ1 , ϕ2 ).

            But the full closed-open map should land in

            The closed CE algebra
                                                                  ∙

             closed Hamiltonian BF fields = polyvector central operations on the open brane ph

            The boundary Hamiltonian function Of corresponds to the cotangent coordinate. The

                                                                  ​




            This “swap” is the key to the correct closed-open interpretation.

            24. Three notions of locality
            The documents distinguish three physical meanings of locality.
            24.1 Support locality along the brane
            On the brane line, observables have compact support.
            If two observables are supported in disjoint intervals, their product is independent. The
            equal-time bracket has a delta function:
                                                       {Of (a), Og (b)} = O{f ,g} (ab).
                                                             ​            ​                     ​




            The test functions multiply because the bracket is supported where the insertions
            collide.
            This is ordinary canonical locality.
            24.2 Topological locality
            Along the topological directions,
                                                                 Lv = [Q, ιv ].
                                                                      ​                ​




            Moving insertions does not change cohomology unless insertions collide. Therefore the
            factorization algebra is locally constant in those directions.
            This is why the brane line behaves like an E1-algebra after passing to cohomology.
                                                                               ​




            24.3 Formal holomorphic locality
            In the holomorphic normal directions, the model is completed at the brane point. It is a
            theory of formal holomorphic jets, not a theory of finite-speed propagation through C2.
https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa84c-a760-83ea-ae70-183a06ecd2d3                                                   63/510
30/04/2026, 00:40                                                                    Whitepaper Critical Analysis

            Antiholomorphic translations are Q-exact:
                                                                L∂/∂ zˉi = [Q, ι∂/∂zˉi ].
                                                                         ​
                                                                             ​                      ​




            The principal parts are local in this formal/distributional sense. They are residue currents
            supported at the brane, not smooth fields spread through C2.
            So the cotangent sector is:
                                        distributionally local transverse to the brane.                             ​




            25. Unitarity
            This model is not a Lorentzian unitary quantum field theory.
            It is complex, BV, ghost-valued, topological-holomorphic, and local/formal. It has no
            positive-definite Hilbert space built into it, no ordinary time-evolution unitarity, and no
            reflection positivity statement.
            The correct consistency condition is the classical master equation
                                                                    1
                                                                QI + {I, I} = 0,
                                                                    2
                                                                                 ​




            and quantum mechanically the quantum master equation
                                                                 1
                                                             QI + {I, I} + ℏΔI = 0.
                                                                 2
                                                                     ​




            So in this paper, “physical consistency” means:
             BV master equation + factorization/OPE associativity + anomaly cancellation if q

            If this model is a protected twisted sector of a larger unitary theory, unitarity belongs to
            the parent theory, not to the local twisted sector by itself.

            27. Quantum matrix reduction and the Capelli obstruction
            On the open side, quantizing
                                                                           ∫ Tr(ϕ1 dϕ2 )                           ​           ​




            gives the Weyl algebra generated by matrix entries
                                                                 X = ϕ1 ,              ​                               Y = ϕ2 ,                ​




            with
                                                                  [Xji , Ylk ] = ℏ δli δjk .
                                                                               ​                       ​                           ​       ​




            The quantum comoment for conjugation is normal ordered:
                                                                 μϵ = − Tr([ϵ, X]Y ).
                                                                   ​   ​




            It satisfies
                                                                   ℏ−1 [μϵ , a] = ϵ ⋅ a.   ​   ​




            The quantum brane algebra is the BRST quantum Hamiltonian reduction
                                                                  ∙
                                                   Obsqbrane,N = CBRST (glN , WN , μ).
                                                                           ​                                           ​               ​           ​   ​




            Weyl-ordered traces
                                                        JN (f ) = Tr OpW (f )(X, Y )
                                                             ​                                                             ​




            are invariant and descend through quantum Hamiltonian reduction.
https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa84c-a760-83ea-ae70-183a06ecd2d3                                                                                                 66/510
30/04/2026, 00:40                                                     Whitepaper Critical Analysis

            But this does not prove the full quantum Moyal identity. The obstruction is the normal-
            ordering shift:
                                                    μϵ = Tr(ϵ(Y X − XY + ℏN I)).
                                                       ​




            So the quantum moment relation is
                                                             Y X − XY + ℏN I = 0.

            Therefore
                                                Tr(AXY ) − Tr(AY X) = ℏN Tr(A)

            modulo the quantum moment ideal.
            This is a Capelli-type shift. It can feed lower-degree traces into the connected sector. A
            full quantum theorem must either cancel, renormalize, or correctly account for this shift.
            So the quantum part of the paper gives the algebraic Weyl/Moyal target, not a
            completed quantum closed-open theorem.

            28. Costello graph problem
            Costello’s renormalized BV formalism supplies a general graph/RG framework. But the
            paper does not yet identify this Hamiltonian BF brane defect with a fully controlled
            Costello graph construction.
            The missing analytic data include:
             1. a precise elliptic closed-open parent theory or reduction;
             2. boundary/defect conditions for R × {0};
             3. gauge fixing and propagator;
             4. anomaly cancellation;
             5. the bulk-to-defect kernel;
             6. the relation between Costello’s propagator and the Weyl contraction P /2;
             7. first-order graph normalization;
             8. third-order graph normalization giving 1/24;
             9. connected trace extraction and treatment of the Capelli shift.
            The formal algebra says the boundary product should be

                                                                  m ∘ exp ( P ) .
                                                                           2
                                                                                    ​




            The graph problem is to prove that the actual renormalized BV observable product in
            this model gives exactly that product.

            29. The corrected global thesis
            The corrected paper should have two main theorems, not one overloaded theorem.
            Theorem A: PBW stable trace theorem
            The stable connected Hamiltonian open trace sector is
                                                      Obscl          ˉ ˉ
                                                         brane,H ≃ S(A ⊕ Adesc [1]).

                                                                  ∙

                                                                    U (h ⋉ h∨ [1]).

            The PBW special fibre is
                                                                    S (h ⊕ h∨ [1]).

            Its degreewise polynomial part matches the stable open Hamiltonian sector after
            identifying h∨ with the one-ψ descendant label set.
            But the polynomial descendants do not realize the coadjoint action.
            Theorem B: derived closed-open center theorem
            The closed CE algebra itself is the derived Poisson center of the stable degree-zero
            open Hamiltonian algebra:
                                                       ∙

                                                                               P = ⨁ C ρa,b ,    ​           ​

            30. Errata and corrections
            Here are the corrections I would make explicitly.
            1. Principal-part linear action
            The principal-parts note says, in one place, that acting by z1 lowers indices on principal
            parts. That is wrong.
                                                                                                                                 ​




            The correct formula is
                                                                 z1 ⋅ ρa,b = −(b + 1)ρa,b+1 ,
                                                                   ​             ​                                           ​




                                                                 z2 ⋅ ρa,b = (a + 1)ρa+1,b .
                                                                       ​             ​                               ​




https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa84c-a760-83ea-ae70-183a06ecd2d3                                                                               69/510
30/04/2026, 00:40                                                                                            Whitepaper Critical Analysis

            So linear Hamiltonians raise pole order on principal parts.
            2. Polynomial descendants do not simply “raise indices”
            The polynomial descendant action is
                                                     Op,q : Ψa,b ↦ (pb − qa)Ψa+p−1,b+q−1 .
                                                                 ​               ​                                                              ​




            Whether this raises, lowers, or preserves total degree depends on p + q:
                                                                 degtarget = a + b + p + q − 2.
                                                                                         ​




            Linear Hamiltonians lower total degree by one, quadratic Hamiltonians preserve total
            degree, and higher Hamiltonians raise it.
            The real point is not “raising versus lowering” in general. The real point is that the action
            is not the coadjoint action.
            3. The coadjoint action formula is correct in the paper
            The correct coadjoint/principal-part formula is
                                         z1p z2q ⋅ ρa,b = −(pb − qa + p − q)ρa−p+1,b−q+1 .
                                             ​       ​               ​                                                                              ​




            This should be treated as the reference formula.
            4. The smeared bracket formula needs the scalar quotient
            The formula
                                                     {I∂ (a, f ), I∂ (b, g)} = I∂ (ab, {f , g}Aˉ )
                                                             ​                               ​                         ​                    ​




            must be read in the connected Hamiltonian quotient by scalar trace classes.
            Otherwise, for f = z1, g = z2,       ​                       ​




                                                 {I∂ (a, z1 ), I∂ (b, z2 )} = N ∫ a(t)b(t) dt,
                                                         ​                   ​       ​               ​




            while
                                                                                     {z1 , z2 }Aˉ = 0.
                                                                                                 ​       ​       ​




            So the scalar central term must be projected out.

https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa84c-a760-83ea-ae70-183a06ecd2d3                                                                                              70/510
            5. “Removing constants” is not literally the same as removing all
30/04/2026, 00:40                                                      Whitepaper Critical Analysis




            U (1) center-of-mass modes
            The constant Hamiltonian corresponds to the scalar empty trace
                                                               Tr(1) = N .

            The center-of-mass pair
                                                             Tr ϕ1 ,
                                                                   ​        Tr ϕ2     ​




            still exists as linear observables, but their bracket produces the scalar N . So the precise
            statement is:
                      we project out the scalar Hamiltonian/empty-trace central term,

            not that we remove the entire U (1) center-of-mass sector.
            6. The “full closed-open map remains a problem” can now be
            sharpened
            The paper currently lists the full CE/cotangent P0 factorization-center lift as a problem.
            The corrected mathematical construction is:
                                                                               ​




                                            ∙
                                           CCE (h ⋉ h∨ [1]) ≅ PV(S(h)) ≃ Zfact
                                                  ​
                                                                          P0
                                                                               (S(h)).          ​




                                                                                                    ​




            The remaining work is not the abstract stalkwise derived-center isomorphism. The
            remaining work is to implement it inside the full unfrozen BV/factorization model, with
            compact supports, de Rham/Dolbeault fields, defect kernels, and the principal-part
            boundary realization.
            7. References and labels need cleanup
            The PDF contains malformed reference labels such as “[13]razmyslov” and “[2]tsygan,”
            and a PBW citation appears to point to the wrong bibliography number. These should be
            cleaned before circulation.
            8. The figures should either be upgraded or demoted
            The diagrams on pages 41, 50, and 51 are useful only as schematic reminders. They
            should not look like proofs. Either label them with propagators, boundary vertices,
            automorphism factors, and orientation conventions, or keep them explicitly as informal
            pictures.
