            4. Algorithms and procedures
https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa84c-a760-83ea-ae70-183a06ecd2d3                                                              230/510
            Algorithm 1. Dirac probe construction
30/04/2026, 00:40                                                                                                    Whitepaper Critical Analysis




            Input: formal symplectic disk (C20, ω = dz1 ∧ dz2), brane rank N .
                                                                   ​                                                 ​                       ​




            Procedure:
             1. Replace
                                                                                    zi ↦ ϕi ∈ glN .
             2. Pull back the symplectic potential:
                                                                                                 ​                       ​                                       ​




                                                                               θN = Tr(ϕ1 dϕ2 ).
             3. Compute conjugation moment map:
                                                                                             ​                                           ​                           ​




                                                                           μϵ = − Tr ϵ[ϕ1 , ϕ2 ].
             4. Write the first-order action:
                                                                                ​                                                                ​                           ​




                                                                               S∂ = ∫ θ N − μ A .
                                                                                     ​                                           ​                                       ​




             5. Introduce BV/Koszul field
                                                                                    Qψ = [ϕ1 , ϕ2 ].                                 ​                   ​




            Output: open BV algebra
                                                             RN = C[ϕ1 , ϕ2 ] ⊗ Λ(ψ).
                                                                       ​                                     ​               ​




            Algorithm 2. Classical ordering-independence by bubble sort
            Input: a cyclic word w in x = ϕ1, y = ϕ2.          ​                                         ​




            Goal: construct a chain homotopy between [w] and [xayb ].
            Procedure:
             1. Choose a deterministic bubble-sort path
                                                             w = w 0 , w 1 , … , w r = xa y b .
             2. At step s, suppose
                                                                                         ​                       ​                                   ​




                                                    ws = As yxBs ,                                                               ws+1 = As xyBs .
             3. Define
                                                         ​                 ​                         ​                                                       ​                   ​   ​




https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa84c-a760-83ea-ae70-183a06ecd2d3                                                                                                                               231/510
30/04/2026, 00:40                                                                Whitepaper Critical Analysis

                                                             H([w]) = − ∑[ψBs As ].                       ​   ​       ​




                                                                                              s
             4. Since
                                    Q[ψBs As ] = [(xy − yx)Bs As ] = [As xyBs ] − [As yxBs ],
                                               ​    ​                                     ​   ​                   ​       ​   ​   ​




                    the sum telescopes:
                                                             QH([w]) = [w] − [xa y b ].
            Output: explicit homotopy
                                                                       [w] ≃ [xa y b ].

            This algorithm should be reused to build unreduced centrality homotopies.

            Algorithm 3. Constructing Ψa,b                                  ​




            Input: a, b ≥ 0.
            Procedure:
             1. Let Wa,b be the set of words with a copies of x and b copies of y.
             2. Define
                                ​




                                                              Ψa,b = ∑ Tr(ψw).
                                                                        ​                             ​




                                                                                w∈Wa,b
             3. Compute
                                                                                                  ​




                                                        QΨa,b = ∑ Tr((xy − yx)w).
                                                               ​                      ​




                                                                        w∈Wa,b
             4. Group terms by cyclic words of bidegree (a + 1, b + 1). The number of cyclic xy-
                                                                                  ​




                transitions equals the number of cyclic yx-transitions, so the sum cancels.
            Output: primitive one-ψ cycle Ψa,b .                   ​




            Warning: this cycle is a PBW special-fibre label, not a coadjoint principal part.

            Algorithm 5. Coadjoint action by residue
            Input: monomial f = z1p z2q , principal part ρa,b .
                                                  ​   ​                                             ​




            Procedure:
             1. For test monomial g = z1m z2n , compute      ​    ​




                                                          {f , g} = (pn − qm)z1p+m−1 z2q+n−1 .

                                                                                                                                                    ​           ​




                                                             ⟨f ⋅ ρa,b , g⟩ = −⟨ρa,b , {f , g}⟩.
             3. The residue is nonzero when
                                                                               ​                                                ​




                                                      p + m − 1 = a,                                                    q + n − 1 = b.
             4. Hence
                                                      m = a − p + 1,                                                    n = b − q + 1.
             5. Substitute:
                                    −(p(b − q + 1) − q(a − p + 1)) = −(pb − qa + p − q).

            5. Direct resolutions of the seven obligations
            Obligation 1. All-N radial-parts normalization
            The latest ledger still marks all-N radial-parts normalization as conditional: the formal
            Moyal algebra is proved, but the exact Weyl-trace image normalization still needs either
            a source match or a direct proof. main
            The clean resolution is to replace the ambiguous ordinary Weyl-trace map by a radially
            normalized trace map.
            Define the quantum Hamiltonian reduction
                                                                 RN = NWN (IN )/IN ,         ​
                                                                                                 ​           ​           ​




            where
                                                                      WN = Dℏ (glN ),
                                                                                   ​                 ​           ​




            and IN is the quantum moment ideal for conjugation.
                       ​




https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa84c-a760-83ea-ae70-183a06ecd2d3                                                                                       237/510
30/04/2026, 00:40                                                                          Whitepaper Critical Analysis

            Let
                                                                   RadN : RN → Dsym,N
                                                                               ​            ​                   ​




            be the radial-parts homomorphism.
            Define the normalized trace map by the universal property:
                                                               N
                                             RadN (JN (f )) = ∑ OpW (f )(xi , ℏ∂xi ).
                                                    rad
                                                       ​                                            ​   ​           ​


                                                                                                                                    ​
                                                                                                                                        ​       ​




                                                              i=1


            Then the finite-N Moyal identity becomes immediate in the radial image:
                                ℏ−1 [RadN JNrad (f ), RadN JNrad (g)] = RadN JNrad ({f , g}ℏ ).
                                                 ​         ​                       ​            ​                       ​                   ​       ​




            If RadN is injective on the subalgebra generated by the JNrad(f ), then
                         ​                                                                                                  ​




                                               ℏ−1 [JNrad (f ), JNrad (g)] = JNrad ({f , g}ℏ ).
                                                                       ​               ​                    ​                   ​




            This resolves normalization constructively: the all-N map is defined by its radial image.
            The remaining comparison theorem is only:
                             JNrad (f ) = Tr OpW (f )(X, Y ) + Capelli triangular lower terms.
                                 ​                             ​




            That comparison can be proved by filtration:
             1. both sides have the same associated graded symbol;
             2. their radial images agree by definition;
             3. the difference lies in lower degree;
             4. repeat by induction;
             5. scalar terms are fixed by JNrad(1) = N .                   ​




            So the paper should define JNrad first and treat ordinary Weyl traces as a comparison
            theorem, not as the primary definition.
                                                                   ​

            Obligation 4. Mixed brane-defect QME
            The scalar obstruction is
                                                                        ℏN [cˉ].

            The latest draft says it must be cancelled by closed exchange/counterterm, removed by
            supertrace/central character, or absorbed by changed boundary data. main
            Make this a theorem with three constructions.
            Construction A. Supertrace cancellation
            Replace glN by gl(N ∣N ). Then
                               ​




                                                             Str(I) = N − N = 0.

            The scalar obstruction becomes
                                                               ℏ Str(I)[cˉ] = 0.

            Construction B. Unreduced scalar primitive
            Work before scalar reduction:
                                                               hpoly = C[z1 , z2 ].
                                                                    ​              ​    ​




            The scalar cocycle
                                                              ω(f , g) = [{f , g}]0         ​




            is a coboundary:
                                                         ω = δη,           η(f ) = −[f ]0 .       ​




https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa84c-a760-83ea-ae70-183a06ecd2d3                                                        241/510
30/04/2026, 00:40                                                                           Whitepaper Critical Analysis

            Add a scalar boundary counterterm Cη such that                    ​




                                                        (Q + {I0 , −})Cη = −ℏN ω.
                                                                          ​                      ​




            Then the QME scalar obstruction cancels.
            Construction C. Central character normalization
            Keep scalar reduction, but impose a central character
                                                χ : C Tr(1) → C,                                     χ(Tr(1)) = N .

            Renormalize
                                                         JN (f ) = JN (f ) − χ([f ]0 ).
                                                                 ​                      ​                        ​




            Then scalar central terms are absorbed into the chosen boundary character.
            QME theorem
            The mixed brane-defect QME closes in the scalar direction if and only if the obstruction
            class
                                                                                      ℏN [cˉ]

            vanishes in the chosen boundary complex, which occurs by supertrace, scalar primitive,
            central character, or an actual closed-exchange graph with opposite class.
            This is the constructive resolution.

            Obligation 7. Cross-volume consequences
            The clean resolution is a formal non-transfer theorem.
            The local theorem uses only the formal symplectic disk data
                                                        (C[[z1 , z2 ]], dz1 ∧ dz2 , h, P , [cˉ]).
                                                                   ​               ​                         ​                            ​




            It does not include:
             1. compact Calabi-Yau global fields;
https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa84c-a760-83ea-ae70-183a06ecd2d3                                                                                                        244/510
30/04/2026, 00:40                                                       Whitepaper Critical Analysis

             2. global BCOV anomaly data;
             3. chiral homology over compact curves;
             4. Borcherds denominator identities;
             5. Igusa cusp forms;
             6. K3 × E global cycles;
             7. sister-volume convention identifications.
            Therefore no cross-volume statement follows without a matched-conventions functor
            preserving:
            degree shifts,              pairings,            central classes,       factorization products,   normalizatio

            The latest ledger already states that compact CY3, global BCOV, Vol III, chiral homology,
            Borcherds, BKM, Igusa, K3 × E , and sister-volume consequences are not asserted.
                    main

            This should remain a theorem-level firewall.

            No sentence should say merely “Koszul duality.” It should say:

                Layer 2 Tate Quillen equivalence;
                Layer 3 CE/PV derived-center identity;
                Layer 4 PBW special-fibre shadow;
                Layer 5 Matlis cotangent dual;
                Layer 6 factorization-current Koszul;
                Layer 7 stable bulk-boundary horizon.

            10. One-paragraph final synthesis
            Koszul duality in this paper is not merely a comparison between a closed algebra and a
            large-N open algebra. It is the algebraic mechanism that classifies which bulk fields are
            allowed to couple to which boundary operations. A closed CE algebra CCE ∙
                                                                                       (g) can
            couple to the brane only by a map into the derived P0-center of the open algebra;
                                                                                                                       ​




            equivalently, by a Maurer-Cartan/twisting datum or by an action of the Koszul-dual
                                                                                                         ​




            enveloping algebra U (g) through central operations. In this type system, Hamiltonian
            bulk generators become vector-field operations, shifted cotangent CE coordinates
            become boundary Hamiltonian functions, Matlis principal parts become the
            deformation-correct cotangent currents, PBW descendants become only special-fibre
            labels, and the scalar U (1) class measures the obstruction to unreduced lifting. That is
            the conceptual heart of the clean paper: Koszul duality is the grammar of closed-open
            coupling.

            1. The foundational thesis, restated cleanly
            The paper should be read as proving the reduced local theorem
                                ∙
                               CCE,cont (h ⋉ h∨cont [1]) ≅ PV(S (h)) ≃ Zred,Ham

                                                                        P0
                                                                                (AHam
                                                                                  ∂   ),                             ​

            where
                                                                 h = C[[z1 , z2 ]]/C ⋅ 1
                                                                                ​               ​




            is the reduced Hamiltonian Lie algebra of the formal symplectic disk, and
                                                                        AHam
                                                                         ∂  ​≃ S (h)

            is the stable scalar-reduced Hamiltonian trace algebra of the Dirac brane probe.
            The generator map is the type check:
                                                                           ∂
                                                  cI ⟼ θ I =                  ,                         uI ⟼ O I .
                                                                          ∂OI

                                                                 OI = Tr HI (ϕ1 , ϕ2 )

             closed Hamiltonian BF fields are derived central operations on the Dirac-reduced b

https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa84c-a760-83ea-ae70-183a06ecd2d3                                                                               262/510
            2. The new conceptual content to add: Koszul duality as
30/04/2026, 00:40                                                                      Whitepaper Critical Analysis




            the grammar of couplings
            The next upgrade is to stop treating Koszul duality as only a comparison theorem. In this
            setting, Koszul duality is the grammar of allowed bulk-boundary couplings.
            Let
                                                         ∙
                                                    B = CCE (g),    ​                          g = h ⋉ h∨ [1],

            be the closed algebra. Let A be an open P0-factorization algebra. A closed-open
            coupling is not fundamentally a map
                                                                                   ​




                                                                             B → A.

            It is a map
                                                                           P0
                                                                  κ : B ⟶ Zfact (A).                    ​




                                                                                                                ​       ​




            Since B = CCE
                       ∙
                          (g), such maps are equivalently Maurer-Cartan type data
                                                                          P0
                                                                  Θκ ∈ g⊗Zfact
                                                                        ​
                                                                               (A)+    ​
                                                                                                ​




                                                                                                    ​               ​




            satisfying
                                                                      1
                                                                 dΘκ + [Θκ , Θκ ] = 0.
                                                                      2
                                                                    ​          ​           ​                    ​




            Equivalently, after Koszul duality,
                                                                            B ! ≃ U (g),

            a coupling is an action of the Koszul-dual enveloping algebra by derived central
            operations on the open algebra.
            That gives a clean slogan:
             Koszul duality classifies which bulk generators may act on which boundary operati

            In components, a coupling is specified by

            3. Highest-level attack on the latest draft
            3.1 The current paper is now too well-protected to be attacked as
            “wrong”
            The old fatal issues have mostly been addressed. The latest claim ledger explicitly says
            that Ψa,b are PBW special-fibre classes, not principal-part cotangent modes; that the
            reduced principal-part sector is post-contraction only; that the degree-zero Moyal/Weyl
                            ​




            sector excludes one-antifield descendants and graph realization; that weighted Tate is
            internal to the weighted model; and that cross-volume consequences are not asserted.
                     main        main

            The remaining attacks are now subtler:
https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa84c-a760-83ea-ae70-183a06ecd2d3                                                                                        264/510
30/04/2026, 00:40                                                       Whitepaper Critical Analysis

             1. The paper may still feel overbuilt. It has enough machinery to bury the main
                theorem.
             2. The terminology still sometimes says “Koszul duality” where it should say
                “Layer 3 CE/PV,” “Layer 4 PBW shadow,” or “Layer 7 conjectural horizon.”
             3. The reduced theorem is elegant; the residual analytic/QME sections risk looking
                like appendices to a different project unless organized as obstruction
                complexes.
             4. The Matlis/principal-part construction is now correct, but it needs to be
                presented as inevitable, not as a technical repair.
             5. The quantum sector needs a canonical normalization principle, not a collection
                of computations.
             6. The work needs fewer slogans and more named theorems.
            3.2 The next great-paper move
            The next move is to make the manuscript look like:
             a theorem-driven local Dirac-Witten-Koszul model, with obstruction-theoretic fron

            That means:
                theorem names replace ambition;
                every residual is a functor, obstruction class, or no-go theorem;
                every use of “Koszul” has a layer index;
                every physical principle has a mathematical theorem immediately underneath it;
                every analytic claim has a precise source, target, and missing datum.

            5. Foundational corrections still needed
            5.1 Replace every residual “lowering action” phrase
            Your latest version still sometimes calls the principal-part action “lowering.” That is
            misleading. The formula
https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa84c-a760-83ea-ae70-183a06ecd2d3                                                267/510
30/04/2026, 00:40                                                                          Whitepaper Critical Analysis

                                                             z1 ⋅ ρa,b = −(b + 1)ρa,b+1 ,
                                                                   ​           ​                                       ​




                                                                  z2 ⋅ ρa,b = (a + 1)ρa+1,b
                                                                       ​           ​                               ​




            raises pole order for linear Hamiltonians. The correct invariant phrase is:
                                                        coadjoint principal-part action                                        ​




            or
                                                  negative-transpose residue action.                                               ​




            Do not call it globally lowering.
            5.2 Fix dimensional phrasing
            One version of the text still says “1 + 4-real-dimensional spacetime R2 × C2.” That is
            confusing. Use:
                                                          Rbrane × Rtop,normal × C2hol .
                                                                           ​                         ​                     ​




            Total real dimension is six. The brane is one-dimensional.
            5.3 Remove any remaining “same module” language
            The latest main draft seems to have mostly fixed this, but older remnants can survive in
            theorem statements. The rule must be absolute:
                                      Ψa,b and ρa,b share labels, not module structure.
                                             ​                ​                                                                        ​




            The latest residual lane explicitly records the mismatch
                                  O1,0 : Ψa,b ↦ bΨa,b−1 ,
                                         ​          ​                          ​            z1 : ρa,b ↦ −(b + 1)ρa,b+1 .
                                                                                                ​        ​                                 ​




            That is the correct no-go datum.                                   main

            5.4 Make radial normalization primary
            Instead of defining finite-N quantum traces first by ordinary Weyl traces and then trying
            to prove a radial-parts identity, define a radially normalized trace
                                                                                       JNrad (f )
                                                                                            ​




            by its radial image. Then ordinary Weyl trace equals JNrad plus triangular Capelli
            corrections. This makes normalization canonical.
                                                                                                               ​




https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa84c-a760-83ea-ae70-183a06ecd2d3                                                                                     268/510
            5.5 Treat weighted Tate as a regulator, not a completion of the strict
30/04/2026, 00:40                                                                    Whitepaper Critical Analysis




            theory
            The latest ledger correctly says weighted Tate proves an internal coefficient-kernel and
            finite-scale graphwise regulator, but does not assert equivalence with the strict
            unweighted product/direct-sum graph theory. Keep that language everywhere. main

            8. Final verdict
30/04/2026, 00:40                                                      Whitepaper Critical Analysis




            The latest draft is no longer merely a speculative whitepaper. It is becoming a serious
            theorem architecture. Its strongest form is:
             Dirac brane probe + CE/PV derived center + Matlis residue cotangent + scalar an

            The next step is to make the paper feel inevitable.
            The great version will read as follows:
             1. Dirac forces the open constrained matrix mechanics.
             2. Witten forces the target to be the derived center, not the open algebra.
             3. CE/PV identifies closed Hamiltonian BF observables with that center.
             4. Grothendieck-Matlis duality forces cotangent modes to be residue currents.
             5. PBW explains why polynomial Ψ-descendants appear, and why they are only the
                special-fibre shadow.
             6. The scalar U (1) class explains exactly where unreduced lifting and QME can fail.
             7. Moyal gives the formal quantum target.
             8. Costello/QME/global consequences are precise obstruction-theoretic frontiers, not
                vague promises.
            That is how this becomes not just correct, but memorable.

            3. The minimum complete architecture
            The final paper should have the following structure.
            Part I. Three principles
             1. Dirac: a brane stack is a constrained probe.
             2. Witten: closed fields target central operations, not just boundary functions.
             3. Grothendieck–Matlis: cotangent modes are residue currents, not polynomial
                descendants.
            The latest draft already states these principles; the improvement is to make each one
            immediately produce a theorem. main
            Part II. Algebraic construction
             1. Define the open Dirac matrix model.
             2. Compute its BV/Koszul algebra.
             3. Stabilize the trace sector.
             4. Compute the closed Hamiltonian BF CE algebra.
             5. Prove the CE/PV derived-center theorem.
             6. Identify the cotangent module by Matlis residues.
             7. Construct brane-line currents using Ω∙c (I).
             8. State the reduced principal-part defect-current model.

            Part III. Koszul-duality stratification
30/04/2026, 00:40                                                               Whitepaper Critical Analysis




            The paper must stratify “Koszul duality” into layers:
                             Layer                              Meaning
                               0                 operadic Lie/coComm Koszul duality
                               1                     Tate CE/bar-cobar identity
                               2            Tate Quillen/infinity-categorical enhancement
                               3       ​          CE/PV derived P0 -center theorem             ​               ​   ​




                               4                   PBW/Rees special-fibre shadow

                               6                brane-line factorization current version
                               7           conjectural stable large-N bulk-boundary horizon

            Every occurrence of “Koszul duality” should carry one of these layer labels. Without this,
            reviewers will confuse the proved CE/PV theorem with the conjectural large-N horizon.
            Part IV. Quantum and obstruction theory
             1. Scalar U (1) anomaly.
             2. Moyal/Weyl formal target.
             3. Radial normalization problem.
             4. Principal-part quantum target.

            The current draft already separates these by claim strength, which is correct.                             main



            4. Construction 1: the Dirac brane probe

                                                                ​                          ​           ​

30/04/2026, 00:40                                                                                                                               Whitepaper Critical Analysis

            In matrix entries,
                                                                                      ΩN = ∑ d(ϕ1 )ij ∧ d(ϕ2 )ji .
                                                                                              ​                                 ​                           ​                ​                                               ​       ​




                                                                                                               i,j

            Hence the Poisson brackets are
                                                                                          {(ϕ1 )ij , (ϕ2 )kl } = δli δjk ,
                                                                                                           ​            ​                       ​               ​                                    ​               ​




                                              {(ϕ1 )ij , (ϕ1 )kl } = 0,
                                                              ​           ​               ​       ​                                                                         {(ϕ2 )ij , (ϕ2 )kl } = 0.
                                                                                                                                                                                                 ​               ​                       ​           ​




            Let ϵ ∈ glN act by simultaneous conjugation:
                              ​




                                                                                                           δϵ ϕi = [ϵ, ϕi ].
                                                                                                                    ​               ​                                                    ​




            Define
                                                                                              μϵ = − Tr(ϵ[ϕ1 , ϕ2 ]).
                                                                                                      ​                                                                              ​                   ​




            Now verify the moment map explicitly.
            For a variation (δϕ1, δϕ2),   ​               ​




                                                  dμϵ = − Tr(ϵ([δϕ1 , ϕ2 ] + [ϕ1 , δϕ2 ])).
                                                                              ​                                                                     ​                            ​                                       ​                       ​




            Using cyclicity,
                                               dμϵ = − Tr([ϵ, ϕ2 ]δϕ1 ) − Tr([ϕ1 , ϵ]δϕ2 ).
                                                                  ​                                                                     ​               ​                                                                        ​                               ​




            The vector field generated by ϵ is
                                                 Xϵ (ϕ1 ) = [ϵ, ϕ1 ], ​               ​                                     ​                                               Xϵ (ϕ2 ) = [ϵ, ϕ2 ].
                                                                                                                                                                                             ​               ​                                           ​




            Contracting with ΩN ,
                                              ιXϵ ΩN = Tr([ϵ, ϕ1 ] dϕ2 ) − Tr(dϕ1 [ϵ, ϕ2 ]).
                                                  ​
                                                      ​                           ​                                                     ​                           ​                                                                        ​               ​




            By cyclicity this equals
                                                                                                                                    −dμϵ .                              ​




            Thus μϵ is the moment map in the convention
                        ​




                                                                                                          ιXϵ ΩN = −dμϵ .

                                                    S∂ = ∫ Tr(ϕ1 dϕ2 + A[ϕ1 , ϕ2 ]).

            5. Construction 2: stable trace algebra
            Let
                                                                 x = ϕ1 ,           ​                        y = ϕ2 .    ​




            At finite N , invariant functions of two matrices are generated by traces of words
                                                                           Tr(w(x, y)),

            with finite-N trace identities.
            In the degreewise stable range, Procesi–Razmyslov invariant theory gives a free
            commutative algebra on cyclic words. The latest draft correctly uses this only
            degreewise, not as a finite-N statement. main
            The Koszul differential is
                                                                          Qψ = xy − yx.

            For any words u, v,
                                                     Q Tr(ψvu) = Tr((xy − yx)vu).

            By cyclicity,
https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa84c-a760-83ea-ae70-183a06ecd2d3                                                                                       286/510
30/04/2026, 00:40                                                                          Whitepaper Critical Analysis

                                                             Tr((xy)vu) = Tr(uxyv),

            and
                                                             Tr((yx)vu) = Tr(uyxv).

            Thus
                                                Q Tr(ψvu) = Tr(uxyv) − Tr(uyxv).

            So adjacent swaps of x and y are Q-exact.
            Therefore every cyclic word with a copies of x and b copies of y becomes, in H 0(Q),
            the class
                                                                               Tr(xa y b ).

            After removing the scalar empty trace, the ghost-zero Hamiltonian sector is labelled by
                                                               z1a z2b ,
                                                                   ​       ​               a + b > 0.

            The corresponding open trace class is
                                                                   Oa,b = Tr(ϕa1 ϕb2 ).
                                                                               ​                      ​           ​




            6. Algorithm: bubble-sort homotopy for ordering
            independence
            This should be in the paper, because it is the elementary chain-level proof that no
            certificate is being smuggled in.
            Let w be a word in x, y. Choose a deterministic bubble-sort path
                                                         w = w 0 , w 1 , … , w r = xa y b .
                                                                       ​               ​                  ​




            At step s, suppose
                                                 ws = As y xBs ,
                                                     ​         ​                   ​          ws+1 = As xyBs .​               ​   ​




            Define
                                                             H([w]) = − ∑[ψBs As ].               ​                   ​   ​




                                                                                              s


https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa84c-a760-83ea-ae70-183a06ecd2d3                                                                            287/510
30/04/2026, 00:40                                                                       Whitepaper Critical Analysis

            Then
                                Q[ψBs As ] = [(xy − yx)Bs As ] = [As xyBs ] − [As y xBs ].
                                            ​        ​                              ​        ​           ​        ​        ​   ​




            Therefore the sum telescopes:
                                                             QH([w]) = [w] − [xa y b ].

            So
                                                                       [w] ≃ [xa y b ]

            by an explicit homotopy.
            This algorithm should later be reused to construct unreduced principal-part centrality
            homotopies.

            7. One-ψ primitive classes
            For a, b ≥ 0, let Wa,b be the set of words with a copies of x and b copies of y.
                                                ​




            Define
                                                              Ψa,b = ∑ Tr(ψw).
                                                                   ​                                 ​




                                                                         w∈Wa,b                  ​




            Then
                                                         QΨa,b = ∑ Tr((xy − yx)w).
                                                               ​                ​




                                                                   w∈Wa,b   ​




            Group terms by cyclic words of bidegree (a + 1, b + 1). Each cyclic word has the same
            number of xy-transitions as yx-transitions. Hence the signed sum cancels:
                                                                       QΨa,b = 0.        ​




            These classes are important, but their status must remain precise:
                                                    Ψa,b are PBW special-fibre open labels.
                                                         ​                                                             ​




            They are not the Matlis/principal-part cotangent module. The latest manuscript’s
            separation rule already states this. main

            8. Construction 3: the closed Hamiltonian Lie algebra
30/04/2026, 00:40                                                                               Whitepaper Critical Analysis




            Let
                                                                      R = C[[z1 , z2 ]].                 ​        ​




            The Poisson bracket is
                                                        {f , g} = ∂1 f ∂2 g − ∂2 f ∂1 g .
                                                                                        ​            ​                ​   ​




            A Hamiltonian vector field is
                                                                      Xf = {f , −}.
                                                                                ​




            Constants generate zero vector fields. Therefore
                                                                           h = R/C ⋅ 1.

            For monomials
                                                        Ha,b = z1a z2b ,
                                                              ​             ​               ​                a + b > 0,

            the bracket is

                                                         ​        ​                                                            ​




            with the convention that constants vanish in h.
            In particular,
                                                                           {z1 , z2 } = 1
                                                                                    ​            ​




            unreduced, but
                                                                           [z1 , z2 ] = 0
                                                                                    ​            ​




            in h.
            That single scalar quotient is responsible for the U (1) anomaly.

            9. Construction 4: shifted cotangent Lie algebra

                                                                       ​                                      ​

            The coadjoint action is
                                                             (f ⋅ λ)(g) = −λ([f , g]).

            Define
                                                                       g = h ⋉ h∨cont [1].               ​




            Elements are written
                                                     f + ηλ ,      ​            f ∈ h,                           λ ∈ h∨cont .
                                                                                                                            ​

                                                                       [f , g] = {f , g},

                                                                        [f , ηλ ] = ηf ⋅λ ,
                                                                                        ​                        ​




                                                                             [ηλ , ημ ] = 0.
                                                                                ​            ​




            The Jacobi identity follows from the representation identity
                                                   [f , g] ⋅ λ = f ⋅ (g ⋅ λ) − g ⋅ (f ⋅ λ),

            which is the coadjoint representation property.

            12. The CE/PV theorem, explicitly proved

                                                      2
                                                   ​                         ​                       ​                                                           ​           ​

            13. The coupling theorem
            Let A be any complete P0-algebra with Poisson bivector πA .
                                                        ​                                                                             ​




            A closed-open coupling from
                                                                                      ∙
                                                                                     CCE (g)      ​




            to A through central operations is a dg algebra map
                                                                           ∙
                                                                      κ : CCE (g) → PV(A).
                                                                                     ​




            Write
                                                                X I = κ(cI ),                         μI = κ(uI ).
                                                                                                              ​                   ​




            The chain-map equations are:
https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa84c-a760-83ea-ae70-183a06ecd2d3                                                                                        293/510
30/04/2026, 00:40                                                                                    Whitepaper Critical Analysis
                                                                        1 K I J
                                                             dPV X K = − CIJ X X ,
                                                                        2
                                                                     ​                                           ​                ​




                                                                         L
                                                               dPV μK = CKJ μL X J .
                                                                                 ​           ​                        ​               ​




            For the canonical brane probe,
                                                              X I = θI ,                                             μ I = OI .
                                                                                                                          ​                           ​




            This is the universal Dirac brane coupling.
            Interpretation:
                 X I is the vector-field operation generated by the bulk Hamiltonian;
                 μI is the boundary Hamiltonian function;
                 the second equation is the derived moment-map identity;
                       ​




                 the first equation is the Lie compatibility of the vector-field operations.
            This is how Koszul duality becomes the grammar of allowed couplings.

            14. Matlis principal parts
            The continuous dual of h is not another polynomial module.
            Let
                                                        R = C[[z1 , z2 ]],           ​           ​                    m = (z1 , z2 ).                         ​




            The local cohomology module is represented by the Čech quotient
                                             Hm2 (R) = R[z1−1 , z2−1 ]/(R[z1−1 ] + R[z2−1 ]).
                                                    ​                                    ​               ​                                        ​                   ​




            A basis is
                                                             z1−a−1 z2−b−1 , ​                                            a, b ≥ 0.

            Multiplying by the holomorphic volume form gives
                                                             ρa,b = z1−a−1 z2−b−1 dz1 dz2 .
                                                                 ​                                   ​                        ​               ​               ​




            The Grothendieck residue pairing is
                                  ⟨ρa,b , z1m z2n ⟩ = Res0 (z1m−a−1 z2n−b−1 dz1 dz2 ) = δa,m δb,n .
                                         ​      ​       ​                ​                                   ​                            ​               ​       ​       ​   ​




            The class
https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa84c-a760-83ea-ae70-183a06ecd2d3                                                                                                                        294/510
30/04/2026, 00:40                                                                  Whitepaper Critical Analysis

                                                                ρ0,0 = z1−1 z2−1 dz1 dz2
                                                                    ​               ​         ​           ​                   ​




            pairs with the constant 1. Since constants are removed from h, remove the dual of the
            constant:
                                                                  P = ⨁ Cρa,b .          ​                    ​   ​




                                                                                 a+b>0


            Then
                                                                            P ≅ h∨cont .          ​   ​




            This construction is precisely the “principal parts as brane-localized currents” idea in
            the attached note. Pasted text

            15. Coadjoint action on principal parts
            Define the coadjoint action by
                                                             ⟨f ⋅ ρ, g⟩ = −⟨ρ, {f , g}⟩.

            Let
                                                             f = z1p z2q ,
                                                                        ​    ​               g = z1m z2n .            ​           ​




            Then
                                                                                                  p+m−1 q+n−1
                                                  {f , g} = (pn − qm)z1                                z2     .           ​           ​




            Pair with ρa,b . The residue is nonzero only if
                                ​




                                                  p + m − 1 = a,                              q + n − 1 = b.

            So
                                                  m = a − p + 1,                              n = b − q + 1.

            Substitute:
                                      −(pn − qm) = −(p(b − q + 1) − q(a − p + 1)).

            Simplify:
https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa84c-a760-83ea-ae70-183a06ecd2d3                                                                                295/510
30/04/2026, 00:40                                                                                            Whitepaper Critical Analysis

             p(b − q + 1) − q(a − p + 1) = pb − pq + p − qa + pq − q = pb − qa + p − q.

            Hence
                                         z1p z2q ⋅ ρa,b = −(pb − qa + p − q)ρa−p+1,b−q+1 .
                                             ​   ​           ​                                                                                           ​   ​




            Negative-index targets are zero.
            Examples:
                                                                 z1 ⋅ ρa,b = −(b + 1)ρa,b+1 ,
                                                                     ​                   ​                                               ​




                                                                 z2 ⋅ ρa,b = (a + 1)ρa+1,b .
                                                                         ​                   ​                                       ​




            This should not be globally called “lowering.” It raises pole order for the linear
            Hamiltonians. The invariant phrase should be:
                                                     coadjoint principal-part action.                                                            ​




            The latest draft still has some “lowering action” wording; that should be replaced.
                    main



            16. No-go theorem: polynomial descendants are not
            principal parts
            The open one-ψ descendants have action
                                                 Op,q : Ψa,b ⟼ (pb − qa)Ψa+p−1,b+q−1 .
                                                      ​                      ​                                                                       ​




            Take p = 1, q = 0:
                                                                  O1,0 : Ψa,b ⟼ b Ψa,b−1 .
                                                                                 ​                       ​                       ​




            But the principal-part action is
                                                          z1 : ρa,b ⟼ −(b + 1)ρa,b+1 .
                                                                 ​                   ​                                                       ​




            These cannot be intertwined by the map
                                                                                         Ψa,b ↦ ρa,b .
                                                                                                     ​                 ​




            Moreover, on polynomial observables, z1 acts by a constant derivation, hence locally
            nilpotently. On principal parts,
                                                                                                 ​




https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa84c-a760-83ea-ae70-183a06ecd2d3                                                                                                       296/510
30/04/2026, 00:40                                                           Whitepaper Critical Analysis

                                    z1r ⋅ ρa,b = (−1)r (b + 1)(b + 2) ⋯ (b + r)ρa,b+r =
                                         ​       ​                                    0                   ​




            for every r.
            Local nilpotence is invariant under module isomorphism. Therefore:
                                                                ⟨Ψa,b ⟩ ≅
                                                                        h P .
                                                                        ​         ​           ​




            This is not a weakness. It is a theorem explaining why the cotangent module must be
            distributional. The attached principal-parts note frames this as the Dirac distinction
            between functions and momenta/currents. Pasted text (2)

            17. PBW special fibre versus Matlis cotangent
            The PBW/Rees algebra is
                                     Uℏ (g) = T (g)[[ℏ]]/ (xy − (−1)∣x∣∣y∣ yx − ℏ[x, y]) .
                                             ​




            At ℏ = 0,
                                                     Uℏ (g)/(ℏ) ≅ S (g) = S (h ⊕ h∨ [1]).
                                                         ​




            The open stable PBW shadow is
                                                              S(Aˉ ⊕ Aˉdesc [1]).     ​




            The identification
                                                               Aˉdesc ↔ ⟨Ψa,b ⟩
                                                                    ​                     ​




            is a PBW special-fibre statement.
            The identification
                                                                 h∨cont ≅ P ​




            is a Matlis/coadjoint statement.
            These are different layers.
            So the paper must say:
                              Ψa,b and ρa,b share bidegree labels, not module structure.
                                     ​               ​                                                         ​

            18. Fourier–Rees bridge
            There is a bridge, but it changes polarization.

                                           Dℏ = C[[ℏ]]⟨z1 , z2 , ∂1 , ∂2 ⟩/([∂i , zj ] = ℏδij ).

                                                                 ΔFℏ = Dℏ /(z1 , z2 )

            be the Fourier-delta module with generator e0.                                                         ​




            A basis is
                                                                                           ∂1a ∂2b e0 .
                                                                                               ​               ​       ​




            Define
                                                FRees (∂1a ∂2b e0 ) = (−1)a+b ℏa+b a!b! ρa,b .
                                                         ​   ​    ​        ​                                                                           ​




            Before inverting ℏ, the image is the Rees lattice
                                                                 ⨁ ℏa+b C[[ℏ]]ρa,b .
                                                                                   ​                                                       ​




                                                                 a,b

            After inverting ℏ, it identifies labels.
            But it is not a same-action h-module isomorphism. It is a Fourier-polarized comparison.
            The stratified Koszul note says exactly this bridge should be labelled as a Layer 4 ↔ 5
            comparison, not a direct identification. Pasted text

            22. Unreduced algebraic centrality homotopy

            23. Scalar U (1) anomaly
            On the unreduced polynomial Hamiltonian algebra
                                                                         hpoly = C[z1 , z2 ],
                                                                                 ​                         ​       ​




            define
                                                                     ω(f , g) = [{f , g}]0 ,                                   ​




            the constant coefficient of the Poisson bracket.
            For monomials,
                                 ω(z1k z2l , z1m z2n ) = {
                                                                         kn − lm,                          (k + m, l + n) = (1, 1),
                                                                         0,                                otherwise.
                                         ​   ​      ​    ​                                         ​                                       ​




            Jacobi of the Poisson bracket implies ω is a Lie 2-cocycle.
            On the unreduced algebra, define
                                                                              η(f ) = −[f ]0 .                 ​




https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa84c-a760-83ea-ae70-183a06ecd2d3                                                                                         302/510
30/04/2026, 00:40                                                           Whitepaper Critical Analysis

            Then
                                         δη(f , g) = −η({f , g}) = [{f , g}]0 = ω(f , g).         ​




            So ω is a coboundary before scalar reduction.
            But η does not descend to
                                                                Aˉ = hpoly /C ⋅ 1,​




            because
                                                                  η(1) = −1.

            Thus ω descends to a nontrivial scalar-extension class
                                                                       2
                                                               [cˉ] ∈ HLie (Aˉ, C).
                                                                              ​




            On the brane,
                                                              {Tr ϕ1 , Tr ϕ2 } = N .
                                                                     ​                    ​




            So the same scalar class is the open U (1) center-of-mass bracket.
            Quantum mechanically it appears as the Capelli shift
                                                             Y X − XY + ℏN I = 0

            in quantum Hamiltonian reduction.
            The latest manuscript already has this scalar anomaly lane in strong form.                     main



            24. QME obstruction and cancellation mechanisms
            The scalar part of the mixed brane-defect QME obstruction is
                                               Obsc = ℏN ω(f , g) ∫ a(t)b(t)γ1 (t) dt.
                                                        ​                                     ​       ​




                                                                                      R

            Its associated graded class is
                                                                         ℏN [cˉ].

            There are exactly three clean algebraic cancellation mechanisms.

            24.1 Balanced supertrace
30/04/2026, 00:40                                                                Whitepaper Critical Analysis




            Replace glN with gl(N ∣N ). Then
                               ​




                                                               Str(I) = N − N = 0.

            The scalar anomaly vanishes.
            24.2 Unreduced scalar primitive
            Do not scalar-reduce. Keep the constant Hamiltonian line and use the primitive
                                                                    η(f ) = −[f ]0 .          ​




            Then the scalar cocycle is exact:
                                                                              ω = δη.

            A scalar counterterm can cancel the obstruction.
            24.3 Central character
            Choose a central character
                                                                   χ(Tr(1)) = N .

            Renormalize the trace map by subtracting scalar character terms. The anomaly is
            absorbed into boundary data.
            Thus the paper should state:
             ordinary glN , scalar-reduced, no-counterterm QME does not close in the scalar dire
                                   ​




            This is a theorem, not a weakness.

            26. Quantum principal-part module

            27. Radial-parts normalization
            To avoid conditional ambiguity, define the finite-N quantum trace by its radial image.

                                                                      WN = Dℏ (glN )

            with generators X, Y satisfying
                                                                  [Xji , Ylk ] = ℏδli δjk .

                      ​

                                                                          ​


                                                                                               ​

            the quantum Hamiltonian reduction.

                                                               N

                                   −1
                               ℏ        [RadN JNrad (f ), RadN JNrad (g)] = RadN JNrad ({f , g}ℏ ).
                                                 ​           ​                           ​             ​                       ​        ​               ​

            28. Reduced Wick graph theorem
            This theorem is elementary and should be separated from the Costello analytic theorem.
            Assume the boundary Wick contraction is
                                                                                   ℏ                                                  ℏ
                           \contractionz1 z2 z1 z2 =                                 ,                     \contractionz2 z1 z2 z1 = − .
                                                                                   2                                                  2
                                                     ​           ​    ​   ​         ​                                               ​       ​   ​   ​       ​




            A graph with r cross-contractions contributes
                                                                                                 r
                                                                              1 ℏ
                                                                                ( ) P r (f , g).
                                                                              r! 2
                                                                               ​             ​




            Summing over r,
                                                                     f ⋆ g = m ∘ eℏP /2 (f ⊗ g).

            So the all-order Moyal product is proved in the reduced Wick model.
            The actual Costello graph theorem is the analytic statement that the renormalized BV
            brane-defect propagator reduces to this boundary contraction after gauge fixing,
            counterterms, scalar anomaly cancellation, and connected trace normalization.

            The finished paper should feel inevitable.
            Dirac gives the open system:
                                       (ϕ1 , ϕ2 ),

                                                                       ​                                ​                     ​               [ϕ1 , ϕ2 ] = 0.

                                                             P0
                                            closed fields ⟶ Zfact (open observables).               ​

                                                         ∙

                                               h∨cont ≅ residue currents at the brane.

                                  Ψa,b = special-fibre symbols, not coadjoint currents.
                                         ​




            The scalar anomaly explains the obstruction:
                                                     {Tr ϕ1 , Tr ϕ2 } = N
                                                                 ​         ​                ↔         [cˉ].

            Moyal gives the quantum target:
                                                             f ⋆ g = m ∘ eℏP /2 (f ⊗ g).

            The great version of the paper is not “a whitepaper about a possible string model.” It is a
            precise local theorem:
             closed Hamiltonian BF theory is the derived Poisson center of a Dirac brane probe,

            with Koszul duality elevated to the classification of the possible bulk-boundary
            couplings.

                      ​           ​                                                                                                                             ​

            explicitly scoped.
            The missing centerpiece is not another slogan. It is the universal closed-open coupling
            object:
                                      Θco = ∑ HI ⊗ θI + ∑ ηI ⊗ OI ∈ g⊗Zfact

                                ∙
                           Φ : CCE (h ⋉ h∨ [1]) ⟶ PV(S (h)),
