            This is the object to construct at Kac–Etingof–Kazhdan level. Everything else—PBW,
            Matlis, Moyal, factorization currents, scalar anomaly—is a structural layer around this
            coupling element.

            1. Ground field, grading, and index set
            Work over C. Let
                                                   R = C[[z1 , z2 ]],
                                                                    ​       ​
                                                                                                     m = (z1 , z2 ).       ​




            Let
                                                       I = {(a, b) ∈ Z2≥0 ∣ a + b > 0}.      ​




            For I = (a, b) ∈ I , write
                                                                 HI = Ha,b = z1a z2b .
                                                                        ​                ​               ​   ​




https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa84c-a760-83ea-ae70-183a06ecd2d3                                                                                                          317/510
30/04/2026, 00:40                                                                                        Whitepaper Critical Analysis

            The reduced Hamiltonian Lie algebra is
                                                                                        h = R/C ⋅ 1.

            We always use the unique zero-constant representative of an element of h.
            The Poisson bracket is
                                                           {f , g} = ∂z1 f ∂z2 g − ∂z2 f ∂z1 g ,
                                                                                             ​
                                                                                                 ​

                                                                                                             ​
                                                                                                                 ​

                                                                                                                         ​
                                                                                                                             ​

                                                                                                                                                 ​
                                                                                                                                                     ​




            followed by projection modulo constants. On basis monomials,
                                                [Ha,b , Hc,d ] = (ad − bc)Ha+c−1,b+d−1 ,
                                                                 ​              ​                                                                        ​




            with the convention
                                          Hr,s = 0
                                                 ​                    if r < 0, s < 0, or (r, s) = (0, 0).

            Thus the structure constants are
                                                         (r,s)
                                               C(a,b),(c,d) = (ad − bc) δr,a+c−1 δs,b+d−1 ,
                                                                        ​                                                                ​




            again with the constant target removed.
            This is the starting Lie algebra. No string theory has entered.

            2. The Matlis cotangent module
            The continuous dual of h is not another polynomial module. It is a reduced local-
            cohomology module.
            Define
                                            Hm2 (R) = R[z1−1 , z2−1 ]/(R[z1−1 ] + R[z2−1 ]).
                                                     ​                                   ​               ​                           ​                       ​




            A basis is
                                                                     z1−a−1 z2−b−1 ,​                                a, b ≥ 0.

            Tensor with the holomorphic volume form:
                                                                     ρa,b = z1−a−1 z2−b−1 dz1 dz2 .
                                                                            ​                        ​               ​           ​           ​




            The Grothendieck residue pairing is
https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa84c-a760-83ea-ae70-183a06ecd2d3                                                                                                       318/510
30/04/2026, 00:40                                                                          Whitepaper Critical Analysis

                                    ⟨ρa,b , z1m z2n ⟩ = Res0 (z1m−a−1 z2n−b−1 dz1 dz2 ) = δa,m δb,n .
                                         ​      ​       ​          ​                           ​                ​                   ​       ​                       ​   ​




            The class
                                                                ρ0,0 = z1−1 z2−1 dz1 dz2
                                                                       ​                   ​        ​       ​               ​




            pairs with the constant 1. Since h = R/C ⋅ 1, the cotangent module is
                                                        P = Annres (C ⋅ 1) = ⨁ Cρa,b .
                                                                               ​                                                ​                   ​       ​




                                                                                                        a,b≥0
                                                                                                        a+b>0                   ​




            Thus
                                                                               h∨cont ≅ P .
                                                                                       ​                ​




            This is the Matlis/Grothendieck layer of the theory. The latest manuscript records this as
            the proved principal-part cotangent lane and explicitly says the polynomial descendants
            do not realize this coadjoint module.

            3. The coadjoint action, calculated
            Define the coadjoint action by
                                                             ⟨f ⋅ ρ, g⟩ = −⟨ρ, {f , g}⟩.

            Let
                                                             f = z1p z2q , ​       ​               g = z1m z2n .    ​                   ​




            Then
                                                    {f , g} = (pn − qm)z1p+m−1 z2q+n−1 .                                ​                               ​




            Pair with ρa,b :    ​




                                        −⟨ρa,b , {f , g}⟩ = −(pn − qm)δa,p+m−1 δb,q+n−1 .
                                                    ​                                                                                           ​               ​




            The residue is nonzero precisely when
                                                    m = a − p + 1,                                  n = b − q + 1.

            Substitute into the coefficient:
https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa84c-a760-83ea-ae70-183a06ecd2d3                                                                                                                  319/510
30/04/2026, 00:40                                                                                              Whitepaper Critical Analysis

                                      −(pn − qm) = −(p(b − q + 1) − q(a − p + 1)).

            Simplify:
             p(b − q + 1) − q(a − p + 1) = pb − pq + p − qa + pq − q = pb − qa + p − q.

            Therefore
                                         z1p z2q ⋅ ρa,b = −(pb − qa + p − q)ρa−p+1,b−q+1 .
                                             ​   ​           ​                                                                                              ​   ​




            Negative-index targets are zero.
            In particular,
                                                                 z1 ⋅ ρa,b = −(b + 1)ρa,b+1 ,
                                                                   ​                   ​                                                                ​




                                                                 z2 ⋅ ρa,b = (a + 1)ρa+1,b .
                                                                       ​                   ​                                                        ​




            This action is not polynomial. It is not locally nilpotent. It is the coadjoint principal-part
            action.

            4. The shifted cotangent Lie algebra
            Define
                                                                               g = h ⋉ P [1].

            Let
                                                                                                           ηa,b     ​




            denote the shifted generator corresponding to ρa,b ∈ P .                                                                ​




            The brackets are
                                                                                     K
                                                                       [HI , HJ ] = CIJ
                                                                               ​
                                                                                        HK ,           ​                        ​               ​




                                                                  [HI , ηJ ] = (HI ⋅ ρJ )[1],
                                                                           ​                   ​                        ​               ​




                                                                                   [ηI , ηJ ] = 0. ​            ​




            Equivalently, if
                                                                           HI ⋅ ρJ = ALIJ ρL ,
                                                                                   ​                       ​                ​               ​




https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa84c-a760-83ea-ae70-183a06ecd2d3                                                                                                          320/510
30/04/2026, 00:40                                                                                   Whitepaper Critical Analysis

            then
                                                                   [HI , ηJ ] = ALIJ ηL .
                                                                                ​               ​                         ​               ​




            The Jacobi identity has three cases.
            For H, H, H , it is Jacobi of the Poisson bracket.
            For H, H, η, it is the representation identity
                                      [HI , HJ ] ⋅ ρK = HI ⋅ (HJ ⋅ ρK ) − HJ ⋅ (HI ⋅ ρK ),
                                            ​       ​         ​                         ​             ​               ​                               ​   ​   ​




            which follows from the coadjoint action definition.
            For any bracket involving two η’s, Jacobi is immediate because P [1] is abelian.
            Thus g is a graded Lie algebra.

            5. The closed CE algebra
            Let cI be the CE coordinate dual to HI .
            Let uI be the CE coordinate dual to ηI .
                     ​                                                              ​

                                                              ∣cI ∣ = 1,                                      ∣uI ∣ = 0.

            The second formula is the coadjoint representation written in coordinates. Its sign
            convention is fixed by the requirement that the CE/PV map below be a chain map.

            7. The centerpiece map
            Define
                                                                        ∙
                                                                   Φ : CCE (g) ⟶ PV(A∂ )    ​                                                                          ​




            on generators by
                                                              Φ(cI ) = θI ,                                             Φ(uI ) = OI .              ​                           ​       ​




            Check the differential.
            For cK ,
                                                      1 K I J
                                        Φ(dCE cK ) = − CIJ θ θ = dπ θK = dπ Φ(cK ).
                                                      2
                                                      ​                             ​               ​                                                      ​                               ​




            For uK ,   ​




                                                       L
                                         Φ(dCE uK ) = CKJ OL θJ = dπ OK = dπ Φ(uK ).
                                                          ​    ​                        ​                   ​                          ​                       ​                   ​           ​




            Both differentials are derivations. Therefore
                                                                                ΦdCE = dπ Φ.                    ​              ​                       ​




            The source and target are completed free graded-commutative algebras on
            corresponding generators. Therefore Φ is an isomorphism of completed dg
            commutative algebras.
            It also preserves the degree −1 cotangent/Schouten bracket:
                                                                   [cI , uJ ] ↦ [θI , OJ ]S = δJI ,
                                                                                ​                                                  ​       ​                               ​




            and all other generator brackets vanish on both sides.
            Thus
https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa84c-a760-83ea-ae70-183a06ecd2d3                                                                                                                                         324/510
30/04/2026, 00:40                                                                                       Whitepaper Critical Analysis

                                                         ∙
                                                        CCE (h ⋉ P [1]) ≅ PV(S (h)).                                                      ​




            This is the main theorem’s algebraic core. The stratification note identifies this as Layer
            3, the CE/PV incarnation of the derived P0-center; it is proved by direct generator
            computation and does not rely on the Tate Quillen envelope.
                                                                                            ​




            8. The actual centerpiece object: the universal coupling
            Maurer-Cartan element

                                                                  I                                                       I

            It is the universal closed-open coupling.
            Let us verify the Maurer-Cartan equation by components.
            Write
                                           ΘH = ∑ H I ⊗ θ I ,
                                                 ​           ​        ​
                                                                                                                    ΘP = ∑ η I ⊗ O I .
                                                                                                                          ​           ​       ​       ​




                                                         I                                                                        I

                                                                     1
                                                                 dΘ + [Θ, Θ] = 0
                                                                     2

            is

                                             ∑ HK ⊗ d π θ K +                                                         ∑[HI , HJ ] ⊗ θI θJ .

                                                   ​         ​                  ​                                    ​                   ​                       ​               ​




                                              K                                                                              I,J

                                                                                     K

                                                                           2

            This is the fully explicit bulk-boundary coupling object.
            In strict product/direct-sum topologies, the infinite diagonal sum defining Θco may fail to
            converge. Therefore the construction has three honest forms:
                                                                                                                                                                                             ​




             1. Finite-dimensional form: ΘF for any finite-dimensional Lie algebra l.
                                                                        ​

             2. Coordinate-map form: the generator map Φ(cI ) = θI , Φ(uI ) = OI , which does
                 not require a global diagonal tensor.
                                                                                                                                                      ​   ​




             3. Weighted/admissible form: Θco as an actual element once the coefficient category
                 makes the diagonal Casimir converge.
                                                                            ​




            The latest draft’s ledger correctly distinguishes the coordinate CE/PV theorem from the
            stricter kernel/Casimir and endpoint problems.

            9. Koszul duality as the classification of couplings
            Let A be any complete classical P0-algebra. Define
                                                        Z P0 (A) = (PV(A), [πA , −]).
                                                              ​




                                                                                                                                      ​




            A closed-open coupling from
                                                                                     ∙
                                                                                B = CCE (g)       ​




            to A is
                                                                      κ : B → Z P0 (A).                   ​




            Equivalently,
                                                  κ          ⟺                  Θκ ∈ MC(g⊗Z P0 (A)).
                                                                                     ​                                            ​
                                                                                                                                              ​




            In coordinates,
                                                      X I := κ(cI ),                              μI := κ(uI ).   ​                               ​




            The CE equations become
                                                                       1 K I J
                                                             dZ X K = − CIJ X X ,
                                                                       2
                                                                  ​                           ​               ​




                                                                           L
                                                                  dZ μK = CKJ
                                                                       ​      μL X J .
                                                                                 ​                    ​               ​




            If κ is also a P0-map, then the cotangent bracket gives the additional canonical relations
                                   ​




                                                                           [X I , μJ ] = δJI ,
                                                                                          ​                                   ​




                                                      [X I , X J ] = 0,                               [μI , μJ ] = 0.     ​               ​




            For the Dirac brane probe,
https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa84c-a760-83ea-ae70-183a06ecd2d3                                                                                                    327/510
30/04/2026, 00:40                                                                    Whitepaper Critical Analysis
                                                               I             I
                                                             X =θ ,                       μ I = OI .
                                                                                              ​                 ​




            Thus the CE/PV theorem is not just a comparison. It is the universal solution of the
            closed-open coupling equations for the brane probe.
            This is the conceptual upgrade: Koszul duality is the grammar of allowable bulk-
            boundary couplings.

            10. The open trace representation
            Now construct the open side explicitly.
            Let
                                                               x = ϕ1 ,          ​        y = ϕ2 .      ​




            Let
                                                                    0
                                                                   Ccyc = C⟨x, y⟩cyc
                                                                         ​                          ​




            be cyclic words in x, y, and let
                                                                −1
                                                               Ccyc = C⟨x, y, ψ⟩1ψ
                                                                     ​




                                                                                cyc                         ​




            be cyclic words with one ψ.
            Define
                                                  Qx = Qy = 0,                           Qψ = xy − yx.

            Then
                                                             Q[ψvu] = [(xy − yx)vu].

            By cyclicity,
                                                               [(xy)vu] = [uxyv],

                                                               [(yx)vu] = [uyxv].

            Hence
                                                         Q[ψvu] = [uxyv] − [uyxv].


https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa84c-a760-83ea-ae70-183a06ecd2d3                                                          328/510
30/04/2026, 00:40                                                                      Whitepaper Critical Analysis

            So adjacent swaps are boundaries.
            Define
                                                                          0
                                                                     N : Ccyc → Aˉ       ​




            by
                                                                                       #x(w) #y(w)
                                                             N ([w]) = z1                   z2     . ​                   ​




            Define
                                                                          i : Aˉ → Ccyc
                                                                                    0
                                                                                                             ​




            by
                                                                   i(z1a z2b ) = [xa y b ].
                                                                           ​       ​




            Choose a deterministic bubble-sort path
                                                         w = w 0 , w 1 , … , w r = xa y b .
                                                                      ​        ​                         ​




            At step s,
                                                 ws = As y xBs ,
                                                     ​         ​           ​                 ws+1 = As xyBs .    ​               ​   ​




            Define
                                                             H([w]) = − ∑[ψBs As ].              ​                   ​       ​




                                                                                             s

            Then
                                                             QH([w]) = [w] − [xa y b ].

            Therefore
                                                              QH + HQ = id −iN

            on degree-zero cyclic words.
            This is the explicit chain homotopy implementing commutativization.

            11. The trace bracket calculation

            12. One-ψ descendants and why they are not P
            Define
                                                              Ψa,b = ∑ Tr(ψw),
                                                                             ​                          ​




                                                                                         w∈Wa,b     ​




            where Wa,b is the set of words with a x’s and b y’s.
                              ​




https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa84c-a760-83ea-ae70-183a06ecd2d3                                                                              330/510
30/04/2026, 00:40                                                                                           Whitepaper Critical Analysis

            Then
                                                    QΨa,b = ∑ Tr((xy − yx)w).
                                                                  ​                                 ​




                                                                               w∈Wa,b           ​




            Group the resulting words by cyclic class of bidegree (a + 1, b + 1). Each cyclic word
            has the same number of xy-transitions and yx-transitions, so the sum cancels:
                                                                                    QΨa,b = 0.               ​




            The polynomial descendant action is
                                                 Op,q : Ψa,b ↦ (pb − qa)Ψa+p−1,b+q−1 .
                                                        ​             ​                                                                              ​




            In particular,
                                                              O1,0 : Ψa,b ↦ b Ψa,b−1 ,
                                                                           ​                            ​                                    ​




            whereas
                                                            z1 : ρa,b ↦ −(b + 1)ρa,b+1 .
                                                              ​                 ​                                                                ​




            Therefore
                                                                               Ψa,b ≅
                                                                                    h ρa,b .
                                                                                            ​                            ​   ​           ​




            The latest manuscript’s local dictionary and Matlis lane already enforce this separation
            and mark the Fourier-Rees bridge as an associated-graded label comparison, not an h-
            module identification.

            Jacobi follows from the coadjoint representation identity:

            This is the strict reduced P0 defect-current model. It is not yet the analytic unreduced
            BV factorization-center morphism. The manuscript’s claim ledger states exactly this
                                                       ​




            scope: the reduced principal-part boundary sector is post-contraction only, while the

            16. Algebraic unreduced centrality homotopy
            Now construct the missing algebraic centrality homotopy.
            Let
                                             C = Cyc C⟨x, y, ψ⟩,                     Qψ = xy − yx.

            Let N : C 0 → Aˉ and i : Aˉ → C 0 be as above. Let H be the bubble-sort homotopy:
                                                             QH + HQ = id −iN .

            For ρ ∈ P , define
                                                                 Θρ : C 0 → C
                                                                    ​




            by
                                                           Θρ ([w]) = Res(ρ N ([w])).
                                                             ​




            Then
                                                                   Θρ Q = 0.
                                                                        ​




            Let Vfcyc be the chain-level Hamiltonian derivation induced by the trace Hamiltonian Of .
            It commutes with Q:
                         ​                                                                                 ​




                                                                 [Q, Vfcyc ] = 0.
                                                                              ​




            Let Vfcomm be the induced Hamiltonian derivation of Aˉ.
                             ​




            By construction,
https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa84c-a760-83ea-ae70-183a06ecd2d3                                                     334/510
30/04/2026, 00:40                                                                                               Whitepaper Critical Analysis

                                                                               N Vfcyc i = Vfcomm .     ​                                   ​




            Therefore
                                                N Vfcyc − Vfcomm N = N Vfcyc (id −iN ).
                                                              ​                                     ​                                               ​




            Using
                                                                      id −iN = QH + HQ,

            and [Q, Vfcyc] = 0, define
                                ​




                                                                                                                              cyc
                                                                                Kf = N Vf H.​                                       ​




            Then
                                                           N Vfcyc − Vfcomm N = Kf Q​                                         ​                                 ​




            on degree-zero chains. If one extends H to all relevant trace-count degrees, the full
            chain-homotopy form is
                                                           cyc
                                                       N Vf           ​       − Vfcomm N = QKf + Kf Q,      ​                                   ​                       ​




            with zero differential on the commutative target.
            Now use the coadjoint definition:
                                                                          Θf ⋅ρ = −Θρ Vfcomm N .
                                                                                        ​                                 ​             ​




            Hence
                                             cyc                                                            cyc
                                    Θρ V f
                                         ​         ​
                                                       + Θf ⋅ρ = Θρ (Vf   ​                     ​                 ​
                                                                                                                          − Vfcomm N ) = Θρ Kf Q.       ​                       ​   ​




            Thus the centrality failure is Q-exact:
                                                         {Of , Θρ } + Θf ⋅ρ = [Q, Θρ Kf ].
                                                                  ​             ​                                     ​                                     ​       ​       ​




            This is the algebraic unreduced cotangent centrality homotopy. It is not a Costello heat-
            kernel theorem, but it is the chain-level construction that the reduced principal-part
            model is shadowing.

            17. Scalar anomaly

            Define
                                                                   hpoly = C[z1 , z2 ],

            including constants.

            Jacobi of the Poisson bracket implies ω is a Lie 2-cocycle.
            On hpoly , define

            in quantum Hamiltonian reduction.

            18. QME scalar cancellation data
            The scalar brane-defect QME obstruction is
                                                  Obsc = ℏN ω(f , g) ∫ a(t)b(t)γ1 (t) dt.
                                                        ​                            ​                   ​




                                                                                 R

            Its associated graded class is
                                                                           ℏN [cˉ].

            There are three algebraic cancellation mechanisms.
            18.1 Balanced supertrace
            Replace glN by gl(N ∣N ). Then
                               ​




                                                              Str(I) = N − N = 0.

            So the scalar term vanishes.
            18.2 Unreduced scalar primitive
            Do not scalar-reduce. Keep 1 ∈ hpoly . Since           ​




                                                            ω = δη,           η(f ) = −[f ]0 ,       ​




            add a counterterm Cη satisfying   ​




                                                            (Q + {I0 , −})Cη = −ℏN ω.
                                                                       ​             ​




            Then the scalar obstruction cancels.
            18.3 Central character
            Choose a central character
                                                                 χ(Tr(1)) = N .



https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa84c-a760-83ea-ae70-183a06ecd2d3                                                   337/510
30/04/2026, 00:40                                                                                 Whitepaper Critical Analysis

            Renormalize trace observables by subtracting their scalar character. The scalar anomaly
            is absorbed into boundary data.
            Thus the ordinary glN , scalar-reduced, no-counterterm brane-defect QME does not
            close in the scalar direction. This is a theorem about the obstruction class, not a
                                                ​




            deficiency.

            20. Quantum principal-part module

            Let
                                                                       {f , g}ℏ = ℏ−1 [f , g]⋆ .​                         ​

                                                                 ⟨f ⋅ℏ ρ, g⟩ = −⟨ρ, {f , g}ℏ ⟩.

                                                                            gℏ = Aˉℏ ⋉ Pℏ [1]

                                              ℏr−1

            22. Radial normalization without cheating
            Let
                                                                  WN = Dℏ (glN )      ​                           ​                   ​




            with generators X, Y satisfying
                                                                 [Xji , Ylk ] = ℏδli δjk .
                                                                                  ​               ​                               ​           ​




https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa84c-a760-83ea-ae70-183a06ecd2d3                                                                                                            340/510
30/04/2026, 00:40                                                                                      Whitepaper Critical Analysis

            Let IN be the quantum moment ideal and
                      ​




                                                                      RN = NWN (IN )/IN .                   ​
                                                                                                                    ​           ​               ​




            Let
                                                                      RadN : RN → Drad,N
                                                                                   ​                    ​                                           ​




            be the radial-parts map.
            Instead of defining the all-N trace by ordinary Weyl trace first, define the radially
            normalized trace by its universal property:
                                                               N
                                             RadN (JN (f )) = ∑ OpW (f )(xi , ℏ∂xi ).
                                                    rad
                                                          ​                   ​                                         ​               ​               ​

                                                                                                                                                                        ​
                                                                                                                                                                                ​       ​




                                                              i=1


            Then, in the radial image,
                                  ℏ−1 [RadN JNrad (f ), RadN JNrad (g)] = RadN JNrad ({f , g}ℏ ).
                                                 ​            ​                            ​                    ​                                           ​               ​               ​




            If RadN is injective on the generated subalgebra, then
                          ​




                                                ℏ−1 [JNrad (f ), JNrad (g)] = JNrad ({f , g}ℏ )
                                                                          ​                    ​                                            ​                       ​               ​




            inside RN .       ​




            The ordinary Weyl trace
                                                                              Tr OpW (f )(X, Y )   ​




            is then compared to JNrad(f ) by triangular Capelli corrections.
                                                     ​




            This avoids certificate cheating: the normalized object is defined by the radial property,
            and the ordinary trace comparison is a separate theorem.

            23. Reduced Wick graph theorem
            Assume the boundary Wick contraction is
                                                                                  ℏ                                                 ℏ
                                                         ⟨z1 z2 ⟩ =                 ,                                   ⟨z2 z1 ⟩ = − .
                                                                                  2                                                 2
                                                                  ​   ​                ​                                    ​       ​                           ​




            A graph with r cross-contractions contributes
https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa84c-a760-83ea-ae70-183a06ecd2d3                                                                                                                                      341/510
30/04/2026, 00:40                                                                               Whitepaper Critical Analysis
                                                                                            r
                                                                   1 ℏ
                                                                     ( ) P r (f , g).
                                                                   r! 2
                                                                        ​       ​




            Summing all graphs gives
                                                             f ⋆ g = m ∘ eℏP /2 (f ⊗ g).

            Thus the reduced Wick model gives the Moyal product to all orders.
            The Costello graph theorem is the additional analytic statement that the chosen
            renormalized BV brane-defect propagator induces this boundary contraction and solves
            the QME. That requires explicit data:
                         (E, Q, QGF , Kt , Pϵ,L , brane kernel, Ict , Δ, trace normalization).
                                                       ​      ​                                                   ​




            Without those data, the reduced Wick theorem is complete, but the Costello realization
            is not a theorem.

            25. What this achieves
           The construction now has no certificate gap at the algebraic level.
           You have:
            1. the Hamiltonian Lie algebra;
            2. its continuous Matlis dual;
            3. the shifted cotangent Lie algebra;
            4. the CE algebra;
            5. the open Kirillov-Poisson algebra;
            6. the polyvector derived center;
            7. the explicit CE/PV map;
            8. the universal coupling Maurer-Cartan element;
            9. the trace representation on the Dirac brane probe;
           10. the brane-line current version;
           11. the reduced principal-part current model;
           12. the unreduced algebraic centrality homotopy;
           13. the scalar anomaly and its cancellation mechanisms;
           14. the Moyal deformation target;
           15. the principal-part quantum coadjoint action;
           16. the radial normalization scheme;
           17. the precise separation from analytic Costello/QME realization.
           The remaining distinction is not mathematical vagueness. It is source-data dependence:
           a full Costello graph theorem requires choosing a specific elliptic BV complex, gauge
           fixing, propagator, brane kernel, counterterm scheme, and QME trivialization. Once
           those are supplied, the reduced Wick/Moyal model above gives the target normalization.
           The clean conceptual sentence becomes:

            This is the only finite-dimensional CE/PV theorem. It is not a theorem about a finite
            quotient h≤M . Your first blocker is exactly right: the paper must stop suggesting naive
            finite Lie quotients of h.

            3. Formal-disk reconstruction without fake finite Lie
            quotients
            The formal disk is reconstructed by coordinate tests, not by Lie quotients.
            Let
                                                                            V = ⨁ CeI           ​                       ​




                                                                                          I∈I

            be the free vector space on the monomial labels. The formal Hamiltonian space h is the
            product completion
https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa84c-a760-83ea-ae70-183a06ecd2d3                                                                                                   350/510
30/04/2026, 00:40                                                                            Whitepaper Critical Analysis

                                                                               h = ∏ CHI          ​                   ​




                                                                                            I∈I

            with the m-adic topology.
            The continuous dual is
                                                                   h∨cont = ⨁ CδI .     ​                 ​               ​




                                                                                             I∈I

            A continuous functional depends on only finitely many Taylor coefficients.
            Now define the coordinate product completion of a polynomial algebra on variables xI ,
            I ∈ I , by
                                                                                                                                               ​




                                                                 S Π (x I ) = ∏ C x α ,
                                                                   ​            ​                             ​




                                                                                             α∈N(I)

            where α ranges over finite-support multiindices.
            Multiplication is well-defined because for a fixed finite-support multiindex γ , there are
            only finitely many decompositions
                                                                               γ = α + β.

            So the coefficient of xγ in a product is a finite sum.
            For finite S ⊂ I and degree d, define the finite coordinate projection
                                                    πS,d : SΠ (xI ) ⟶
                                                             ​         ​            ​
                                                                                                              ⨁                   ​   C xα .
                                                                                                      α:supp α⊂S
                                                                                                         ∣α∣≤d
                                                                                                                                  ​




            A map between coordinate product completions is coordinate-continuous if every finite
            projection πS,d of its output depends on only finitely many input coordinates.
                                   ​




            This replaces all naive finite Lie quotients.

            4. Coordinate CE algebra for the formal disk
            Define
                                                      ∙
                                                     CCE,coord (g) = SΠ (uI )⊗ΛΠ (cI ).
                                                                           ​                          ​           ​           ​       ​




https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa84c-a760-83ea-ae70-183a06ecd2d3                                                                                         351/510
30/04/2026, 00:40                                                                     Whitepaper Critical Analysis

            Here
                                                                                     cI

            is the CE coordinate dual to HI ∈ h, and             ​




                                                                                     uI    ​




            is the CE coordinate dual to the shifted cotangent generator ηI = δI [1].                                  ​   ​




            The differential is specified on generators:
                                                                       1
                                                             dCE cK = − ∑ CIJ c c .
                                                                           K I J
                                                                       2
                                                                     ​                ​            ​       ​




                                                                                          I,J

            For fixed K = (r, s), the set of pairs (I, J) satisfying
                                                                     I + J − (1, 1) = K

            is finite. Hence dCEcK is a finite sum.
                                          ​




            For uK ,    ​




                                                                 dCE uK = ∑ CKJ
                                                                         ​
                                                                             L
                                                                             ​
                                                                                uL c J .       ​       ​       ​




                                                                                     J,L

            For fixed K , this is generally an infinite sum. But it is coordinate-continuous: after
            projecting to any finite coordinate window in the output, only finitely many J, L survive.
            Indeed, for fixed K, J , the bracket determines L uniquely:
                                                                     L = K + J − (1, 1).

            Conversely, for fixed finite output set of L, J , only finitely many pairs occur. Therefore
            dCE uK is a well-defined element of the coordinate product completion.
                    ​       ​




            Extend dCE as a derivation.
                                ​




            The local finiteness of the Hamiltonian structure constants implies:
                                                           ∙               ∙
                                                    dCE : CCE,coord
                                                             ​      (g) → CCE,coord
                                                                                 ​  (g)                            ​




            is coordinate-continuous.
            The identity
https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa84c-a760-83ea-ae70-183a06ecd2d3                                                                     352/510
30/04/2026, 00:40                                                                                            Whitepaper Critical Analysis

                                                                                          d2CE = 0       ​




            holds because every finite coordinate projection reduces to a finite Jacobi computation.
            No finite Lie quotient is being used; finite windows are only tests of equality.

            5. Coordinate polyvectors for the formal disk
            Define
                                                                      A∂,coord = SΠ (OI ).   ​                                       ​                       ​




            Define
                                                    PVcoord (A∂ ) = SΠ (OI )⊗ΛΠ (θI ).
                                                                          ​           ​                                  ​                       ​                   ​   ​




            The coordinate Poisson bivector is formally
                                                                              1
                                                             π∂ =               ∑ CIJ
                                                                                   K
                                                                                      OK θ I θ J .
                                                                              2
                                                                  ​               ​                          ​                   ​                   ​




                                                                                          I,J,K

            This is generally an infinite object, but its Lichnerowicz differential on generators is
            coordinate-continuous:
                                                                      1
                                                             dπ θK = − ∑ CIJK I J
                                                                             θ θ ,
                                                                      2 I,J
                                                              ​                                      ​                       ​                           ​




            which is finite for fixed K , and
                                                             dπ OK = ∑ CKJ
                                                                      ​
                                                                        L
                                                                           OL θ J ,
                                                                              ​                                  ​                           ​                       ​




                                                                                            J,L

            which is coordinate-continuous for the same local-finiteness reason as dCEuK .                                                                                   ​   ​




            Thus
                                                                                                 dπ                  ​




            is a coordinate-continuous derivation of PVcoord (A∂ ).                                                                      ​                       ​




            Again,
                                                                                           d2π = 0
                                                                                                 ​

30/04/2026, 00:40                                                                                               Whitepaper Critical Analysis

            because every finite coordinate test reduces to Jacobi.
            Important: the full Schouten bracket
                                                                                                   [−, −]S                     ​




            is not automatically defined on the entire coordinate product completion. The
            contraction formula involves a sum over all labels I :
                                                                                                   ∂F ∂G     ∂F ∂G
                                              [F , G]S ∼ ∑ (                                               ±         ).
                                                                                                   ∂θI ∂OI   ∂OI ∂θI
                                                                       ​                   ​                     ​                         ​                               ​           ​




                                                                                                                                       ​                               ​




                                                                                   I

            For arbitrary product-series F , G, a coefficient can receive infinitely many
            contributions. Thus the coordinate product completion is a dg algebra but not
            automatically a global P0-algebra.     ​




            This resolves blockers 2 and 3.

            6. Coordinate CE/PV theorem for the formal disk
            Define
                                                          ∙
                                                Φcoord : CCE,coord (g) ⟶ PVcoord (A∂ )
                                                                   ​                                ​                                                                          ​           ​




            by
                                                Φcoord (cI ) = θI ,
                                                               ​                                                         Φcoord (uI ) = OI .           ​           ​                               ​   ​




            On cK ,
                                                               1
                                           Φcoord (dCE cK ) = − ∑ CIJ
                                                                   K I J
                                                                      θ θ = dπ θ K .
                                                               2
                                                       ​                   ​                                ​              ​                       ​                                           ​




                                                                                                                     I,J

            On uK ,   ​




                                             Φcoord (dCE uK ) = ∑ CKJ
                                                           ​
                                                                   L
                                                                      OL θ J = dπ OK .
                                                                               ​       ​                             ​                         ​           ​                       ​                       ​




                                                                                                    J,L

            Therefore
                                                                           Φcoord dCE = dπ Φcoord .
                                                                                               ​        ​                          ​                           ​




https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa84c-a760-83ea-ae70-183a06ecd2d3                                                                                                                                                     354/510
30/04/2026, 00:40                                                                Whitepaper Critical Analysis

            Since the source and target are coordinate product completions on corresponding
            generators, Φcoord is an isomorphism of completed coordinate dg algebras.
                                        ​




            Thus:
                                              ∙
                                             CCE,coord (h ⋉ h∨cont [1]) ≅ PVcoord (SΠ (h)).
                                                             ​               ​                         ​        ​   ​




            This is the central reduced formal theorem. It is not a statement about naive finite
            quotients. It is not yet a global P0-algebra statement on the entire product completion.
                                                                  ​




            The current manuscript already says the coordinate comparison is pro finite-
            window/mixed-completed and that a global P0-bracket needs mixed or weighted
            continuity. main The theorem above makes that precise.
                                                                                     ​




            7. Bracket-admissible completions
            To obtain an honest P0-algebra, choose a subspace
                                               ​




                                                                      B ⊂ PVcoord (A∂ )  ​        ​




            satisfying:
             1. B is closed under multiplication;
             2. B is closed under dπ ;
             3. the Schouten bracket is continuous and convergent on B;
                                                   ​




             4. Φ−1 coord (B) is closed under the transported cotangent bracket.
                             ​




            Call such B bracket-admissible.
            Examples:
                 finite-support polynomial polyvectors;
                 weighted completions with exponential decay/growth chosen so the contraction
                 sum converges;
                 mixed nuclear completions where contraction is trace-class;
                 nilpotent truncations where only finitely many labels survive.
            For every bracket-admissible B, define
                                                                  ∙
                                                                 CCE,B (g) = Φ−1
                                                                         ​




                                                                              coord (B).      ​




            Then
https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa84c-a760-83ea-ae70-183a06ecd2d3                                                              355/510
30/04/2026, 00:40                                                                                                          Whitepaper Critical Analysis

                                                                                        ∙                                                                       ∼
                                                                                ΦPB0 : CCE,B (g)
                                                                                              ​




                                                                                                  ​                            ​                                    ​   B       ​




            is an isomorphism of dg P0-algebras.                ​




            This is the correct bracketed theorem.
            The strict product/direct-sum object is not automatically one such B. The obstruction is
            the infinite contraction/Casimir phenomenon, which belongs to the endpoint problem,
            not to coordinate CE/PV.

            8. The missing central object: coordinate Maurer-Cartan
            coupling
            For a finite-dimensional Lie algebra l, the CE/PV isomorphism corresponds to the
            Maurer-Cartan element
                                Θl = ∑ ei ⊗ θi + ∑ ηi ⊗ Oi ∈ (l ⋉ l∨ [1]) ⊗ PV(S(l)).
                                    ​           ​       ​                                                     ​   ​                        ​




                                            i                                             i

            It satisfies
                                                                                         1
                                                                                    dΘl + [Θl , Θl ] = 0.
                                                                                         2
                                                                                                      ​               ​            ​                ​




            Proof:
            Write
                                            ΘH = ∑ e i ⊗ θ i ,
                                                    ​                           ​     ​                                                        ΘP = ∑ η i ⊗ O i .
                                                                                                                                                            ​                       ​   ​   ​




                                                                        i                                                                                                   i

            The l-component is
                                                                                                                          1
                                                    ∑ ek ⊗ dπ θ k +                                                         ∑[ei , ej ] ⊗ θi θj .
                                                                                                                          2
                                                                    ​       ​                             ​                ​                    ​               ​       ​




                                                            k                                                                          i,j

            Using
                                                                                             1
                                                                                    dπ θk = − Cijk θi θj ,
                                                                                             2
                                                                                          ​                                            ​                ​




            and
https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa84c-a760-83ea-ae70-183a06ecd2d3                                                                                                                                      356/510
30/04/2026, 00:40                                                                                                         Whitepaper Critical Analysis

                                                                          [ei , ej ] = Cijk ek ,
                                                                                              ​               ​                          ​               ​




            this vanishes.
            The cotangent component is
                                                  ∑ ηk ⊗ dπ Ok + ∑[ei , ηj ] ⊗ θi Oj .
                                                        ​    ​                    ​               ​                              ​               ​                       ​                       ​




                                                    k                                                                      i,j

            The first term is
                                                                         ∑ ηk ⊗ Ckj
                                                                                 ℓ
                                                                                    Oℓ θ j .
                                                                                          ​           ​                              ​               ​




                                                                         k,j,ℓ

            The second term is exactly the negative of this after rewriting the coadjoint action in the
            dual basis. Hence the cotangent component vanishes.
            So Θl is a Maurer-Cartan element.
                     ​




            For the formal disk, the expression
                                                            ∑ H I ⊗ θ I + ∑ ηI ⊗ O I
                                                                     ​        ​                                                              ​                   ​                       ​




                                                             I                                                                   I

            is not automatically an element of the strict product/direct-sum tensor product.
            Therefore define the central object as follows.
            A coordinate Maurer-Cartan coupling is a dg algebra map
                                                           ∙
                                                 Φcoord : CCE,coord (g) → PVcoord (A∂ ).                              ​                                                              ​       ​




            Equivalently, it is a family of finite coordinate Maurer-Cartan equations, one for every
            finite coordinate projection πS,d, compatible under refinement.
                                                                 ​




            Thus the universal closed-open coupling object is
                                                                          Ucoord
                                                                           h     = Φcoord .                       ​                                          ​       ​




            In any completion where the diagonal tensor converges, it is represented by the actual
            Maurer-Cartan element
                                                 Θadm = ∑ HI ⊗ θI + ∑ ηI ⊗ OI .       ​                   ​                                                                  ​   ​                   ​   ​




                                                                          I                                                                              I
