30/04/2026, 00:40                                                                      Whitepaper Critical Analysis

            This resolves the missing-object problem without smuggling in a non-existent infinite
            Casimir.

            9. Matlis cotangent module
            Let
                                               Hm2 (R) = R[z1−1 , z2−1 ]/(R[z1−1 ] + R[z2−1 ]).
                                                      ​                            ​       ​                           ​                           ​




            A basis is
                                                               z1−a−1 z2−b−1 ,
                                                                       ​                           a, b ≥ 0.

            Set
                                                               ρa,b = z1−a−1 z2−b−1 dz1 dz2 .
                                                                   ​                   ​               ​           ​               ​




            The residue pairing is
                                    ⟨ρa,b , z1m z2n ⟩ = Res0 z1m−a−1 z2n−b−1 dz1 dz2 = δa,m δb,n .
                                           ​      ​        ​               ​                   ​               ​               ​       ​               ​   ​




            Since ρ0,0 pairs with 1, and constants are removed from h, define
                           ​




                                                          P = Annres (C ⋅ 1) = ⨁ Cρa,b .
                                                                               ​                                           ​               ​   ​




                                                                                                       a+b>0


            Then
                                                                               P ≅ h∨cont .        ​       ​




            The current draft’s Matlis appendix states the same point carefully: P is the scalar-
            annihilator inside local cohomology, and the R-module structure on the injective hull
            does not restrict to P ; the Hamiltonian coadjoint action is transported separately by the
            residue pairing. main

            10. Coadjoint action on principal parts
            Define
                                                               ⟨f ⋅ ρ, g⟩ = −⟨ρ, {f , g}⟩.

https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa84c-a760-83ea-ae70-183a06ecd2d3                                                                                                     358/510
30/04/2026, 00:40                                                                          Whitepaper Critical Analysis

            Take
                                                                 f = z1p z2q ,
                                                                           ​           ​       g = z1m z2n .​           ​




            Then
                                                     {f , g} = (pn − qm)z1p+m−1 z2q+n−1 .                       ​                       ​




            Pair with ρa,b :    ​




                                        −⟨ρa,b , {f , g}⟩ = −(pn − qm)δa,p+m−1 δb,q+n−1 .
                                                     ​                                                                              ​           ​




            Thus
                                                     m = a − p + 1,                              n = b − q + 1.

            The coefficient becomes
                                    −(p(b − q + 1) − q(a − p + 1)) = −(pb − qa + p − q).

            Therefore
                                         z1p z2q ⋅ ρa,b = −(pb − qa + p − q)ρa−p+1,b−q+1 .
                                             ​   ​           ​                                                                              ​       ​




            Negative-index targets are zero.
            In particular,
                                                                 z1 ⋅ ρa,b = −(b + 1)ρa,b+1 ,
                                                                   ​           ​                                            ​




                                                                 z2 ⋅ ρa,b = (a + 1)ρa+1,b .
                                                                       ​           ​                                ​




            So this should not be called simply “lowering.” It is the coadjoint principal-part action.
            The uploaded principal-part note motivates the same point physically: principal parts are
            brane-localized residue currents, whereas polynomial descendants are PBW special-
            fibre labels and cannot replace the coadjoint module. Pasted text

            11. No polynomial realization theorem
            Let MΨ be the span of the polynomial one-ψ classes Ψa,b .
                        ​                                                                                                       ​




            The polynomial action has the form
https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa84c-a760-83ea-ae70-183a06ecd2d3                                                                                              359/510
30/04/2026, 00:40                                                                         Whitepaper Critical Analysis

                                                     Op,q ⋅ Ψa,b = (pb − qa)Ψa+p−1,b+q−1 .
                                                         ​             ​                                                     ​




            For p = 1, q = 0,
                                                               O1,0 ⋅ Ψa,b = b Ψa,b−1 .
                                                                           ​                                ​




            Thus O1,0 acts locally nilpotently on every finite polynomial descendant expression.
                            ​




            But on P ,
                                                             z1 ⋅ ρa,b = −(b + 1)ρa,b+1 .
                                                               ​               ​                                ​




            Hence
                                     z1r ⋅ ρa,b = (−1)r (b + 1)(b + 2) ⋯ (b + r)ρa,b+r =
                                        ​        ​                                     0                                         ​




            for all r ≥ 1.
            Therefore z1 is not locally nilpotent on P .
                                 ​




            Local nilpotence is preserved under module isomorphism. Hence
                                                                                   MΨ ≅
                                                                                      h P .
                                                                                      ​       ​      ​




            This theorem is the firewall between PBW descendants and Matlis cotangent data.

            12. Koszul duality layers
            The paper should use the following layer index.
              Layer             Object                                                                                           Status
                0               operadic Lie/coComm Koszul duality                                                               textbook
                1               Tate CE/bar-cobar identity                                                                       only for admissible conilpo
                2               Tate Quillen enhancement                                                                         only in admissible envelop
                3       ​       CE/PV derived P0 center            ​                                                     ​       direct generator theorem
                4               PBW/Rees special-fibre shadow                                                                    degreewise stable
                5               Matlis/Grothendieck cotangent module                                                             residue theorem
                6               brane-line factorization currents                                                                Ω∙c -current theorem
                                                                                                                                      ​




                7               stable large-N bulk-boundary Koszul horizon                                                      conjectural

            This matches the stratification note: the CE/PV identity is Layer 3, the PBW shadow is
            Layer 4, the Matlis module is Layer 5, the interval-current statement is Layer 6, and the
https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa84c-a760-83ea-ae70-183a06ecd2d3                                                                                             360/510
30/04/2026, 00:40                                                                   Whitepaper Critical Analysis

            stable bulk-boundary Koszul duality slogan is Layer 7, not a theorem used in the proof.
                    Pasted text

            Every occurrence of “Koszul duality” in the paper should be rewritten as “Layer-i Koszul
            duality.”

            13. Bar-cobar and conilpotence
            The bar-cobar/enveloping theorem must be stated only for an admissible system.
            A filtered dg Lie algebra gadm is bar-cobar admissible if it has a complete separated
            descending filtration
                                                         ​




                                                              F 1 gadm ⊃ F 2 gadm ⊃ ⋯
                                                                            ​                         ​




            such that:
             1. gadm ≅ lim gadm /F r ;
             2. [F p , F q ] ⊂ F p+q ;
                           ​          ​       ​




             3. each grpF gadm is finite-dimensional;
             4. the CE coalgebra is conilpotent with respect to the induced filtration;
                                  ​       ​




             5. the PBW filtration on U (gadm ) is complete and separated.
                                                                ​




            Then
                                                                                         ∼
                                                             ΩC∗CE (gadm )
                                                                    ​           ​            ​   U (gadm ).
                                                                                                          ​




            This is Layer 1/2. It is not used to prove the Layer 3 CE/PV coordinate theorem.
            The full Hamiltonian algebra h is perfect; pronilpotence must not be silently substituted
            for conilpotence. Any use of bar-cobar/Koszul enveloping duality must first specify gadm
            .
                                                                                                                   ​




            This directly resolves blocker 4.

            14. Factorization variance
            Let
                                                                    hI = Ω∙c (I)⊗h.
                                                                        ​            ​            ​




https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa84c-a760-83ea-ae70-183a06ecd2d3                                                             361/510
30/04/2026, 00:40                                                                                          Whitepaper Critical Analysis

            For an inclusion j : I ↪ J , extension by zero gives
                                                                               j! : hI → hJ .
                                                                                       ​           ​                                   ​




            Thus I ↦ hI is a cosheaf.
                                 ​




            The CE chains
                                                                                           C∗CE (hI )      ​           ​




            are covariant under j! . For disjoint intervals I1, … , In ⊂ J , the direct-sum map
                                             ​                                                                     ​                                   ​




                                                                                   n
                                                                               ⨁ hIr ⟶ hJ  ​


                                                                                               ​
                                                                                                       ​                                           ​




                                                                               r=1

            induces
                                            C∗CE (hI1 ) ⊗ ⋯ ⊗ C∗CE (hIn ) ⟶ C∗CE (hJ ).
                                                    ​


                                                             ​
                                                                 ​                                             ​


                                                                                                                               ​
                                                                                                                                   ​                           ​   ​




            This is the covariant factorization coalgebra structure.
            CE cochains
                                                         ∙
                                                        CCE (hI ) = Hom(C∗CE (hI ), C)
                                                                     ​     ​                                                                   ​           ​




            are contravariant under this structure. Therefore the closed CE cochain object is
            naturally a cofactorization algebra unless one passes through a compactly supported
            density-dual or finite/reflexive admissible duality.
            To get a covariant factorization algebra of compactly supported observables, define
                                                                     Obsc (I) = S (h!I [−1]),
                                                                               ​                                           ​




            where h!I is the compactly supported density-dual cosheaf. For inclusions j : I ↪ J ,
            pushforward of distributions gives
                          ​




                                                                               j! : h!I → h!J .
                                                                                       ​           ​                                   ​




            Then
                                                                         Obsc (I) → Obsc (J)
                                                                                   ​                                                       ​




            is covariant.

https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa84c-a760-83ea-ae70-183a06ecd2d3                                                                                                             362/510
30/04/2026, 00:40                                                                                            Whitepaper Critical Analysis

            The comparison with CE cochains is valid only in finite/admissibly reflexive situations,
            where the chain/cochain duality is controlled.
            Thus the factorization statement must be written as:
             CE cochains give cofactorization data; compactly supported dual observables give

            For disjoint intervals I1, … , In ⊂ J , the covariance equation for observables is:
                                                   ​               ​




                                  mJ (j1,! F1 , … , jn,! Fn ) = (j1 , … , jn )! (F1 ⊗ ⋯ ⊗ Fn ).
                                        ​      ​        ​                   ​           ​                             ​                   ​       ​       ​       ​




            The bracket locality equation is:
                                                                           {j1,! F1 , j2,! F2 } = 0
                                                                                                ​        ​        ​       ​




            if I1 ∩ I2 = ∅.
                    ​     ​




            For smeared Hamiltonians,
                                                       Bf (a) = ∫ a(t) Tr f (ϕ1 (t), ϕ2 (t))dt,
                                                            ​                       ​                                             ​                   ​




                                                                                I

            the equal-time bracket gives
                                                                {Bf (a), Bg (b)} = B{f ,g} (ab).
                                                                       ​                             ​                                        ​               ​




            If supp a ∩ supp b = ∅, then ab = 0.
            This resolves blocker 6.

            15. Brane-line current source
            The source is
                                                                                hI = Ω∙c (I)⊗h.
                                                                                            ​                 ​               ​




            For
                                                                                a(t)dt ⊗ f ∈ hI ,                                     ​




            the corresponding shifted cotangent CE coordinate is
                                                                                                    ua(t)dt⊗f .           ​

            The boundary Hamiltonian is
                                     ua(t)dt⊗f ⟼ Bf (a) = ∫ a(t) Tr f (ϕ1 (t), ϕ2 (t))dt.
                                                   ​                        ​                          ​                                       ​   ​   ​




                                                                                                  I


            There should be no formula

            That is the old source error.

            16. Reduced principal-part current target
            Define
                                                                                PI = Dc′ (I)⊗P .
                                                                                     ​                     ​               ​




            Define
                                                              App
                                                               ∂ (I) = S (hI )⊗S (PI [1]).
                                                                        ​                                          ​   ​               ​




            For B ∈ Dc′ (I) and ρ ∈ P , write
                               ​




                                                                                                 Θρ (B)
                                                                                                   ​




            for the corresponding principal-part boundary current.
            Define brackets:
                                                         {Bf (a), Bg (b)} = B{f ,g} (ab),
                                                                    ​                        ​                                             ​




                                                         {Bf (a), Θρ (B)} = Θf ⋅ρ (aB),
                                                                ​                        ​                                         ​




                                                                {Θρ (B), Θσ (C)} = 0.
                                                                                 ​                             ​




            Here aB is multiplication of a distribution by a smooth compactly supported function.
            No product of two distributions appears.
            Jacobi follows because
                                                       [f , g] ⋅ ρ = f ⋅ (g ⋅ ρ) − g ⋅ (f ⋅ ρ).

            This is a genuine reduced P0-algebra.         ​

            17. Universal principal-part extension
            The reduced principal-part target has the following universal property.
            Let A be a scalar-reduced Hamiltonian P0-algebra containing Hamiltonian currents
            Bf (a). A principal-part cotangent extension of A is a P0 -algebra E with an abelian
                                                                                               ​




            shifted ideal M [1] and a map
                    ​                                                                                                      ​




                                                                             PI → M           ​




            such that
                                                          {Bf (a), Θρ (B)} = Θf ⋅ρ (aB),
                                                               ​              ​                                    ​




            and
                                                                           {Θρ , Θσ } = 0.
                                                                                      ​




            Then
                                                              pp
                                                             A∂ (I) = S (hI )⊗S (PI [1])
                                                                       ​                              ​    ​           ​




            is initial among such extensions.
            Proof: any such extension receives a unique algebra map from the free graded-
            commutative algebra on hI ⊕ PI [1], and the bracket relations force exactly the
            displayed P0-structure. Jacobi is exactly the coadjoint representation identity.
                                                      ​            ​




                                ​




            This supplies a mathematically constructed replacement target for cotangent modes. It
            does not claim those generators are already present in the unreduced polynomial open
            BV algebra.

            20. Scalar anomaly and QME obstruction
            Define the unreduced polynomial Hamiltonian algebra
                                                                          hpoly = C[z1 , z2 ].
                                                                                  ​             ​       ​




            Define
                                                                     ω(f , g) = [{f , g}]0 ,                ​




            the constant Taylor coefficient.
            On monomials,
                                           ω(z1k z2l , z1m z2n ) = (kn − lm) 1(k+m,l+n)=(1,1) .
                                                   ​   ​         ​    ​                                                         ​




            Jacobi implies ω is a Lie 2-cocycle.
            On hpoly , define
                         ​




                                                                               η(f ) = −[f ]0 .     ​




            Then
                                         δη(f , g) = −η({f , g}) = [{f , g}]0 = ω(f , g).                           ​




            So ω is exact before scalar reduction.
            But η does not descend to
                                                                          Aˉ = hpoly /C ⋅ 1,​




            because
                                                                                η(1) = −1.
https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa84c-a760-83ea-ae70-183a06ecd2d3                                                                          368/510
30/04/2026, 00:40                                                                    Whitepaper Critical Analysis

            Thus ω defines a nontrivial scalar-extension class
                                                                          2
                                                                  [cˉ] ∈ HLie (Aˉ, C).        ​




            On the brane,
                                                                {Tr ϕ1 , Tr ϕ2 } = N .
                                                                              ​                   ​




            Quantum mechanically, the same class appears as the Capelli/Weyl shift
                                                              Y X − XY + ℏN I = 0.

            The current manuscript records the scalar QME representative
                                               Obsc = ℏN ω(f , g) ∫ a(t)b(t)γ1 (t) dt
                                                        ​                                             ​                       ​




                                                                                                  R

            and states the cancellation alternatives.                                 main

            The rigorous statement is:
                                                                                  ℏN [cˉ]                 ​




            is the scalar brane-defect QME obstruction class.
            It is killed by:
             1. retaining the unreduced scalar primitive η;
             2. choosing a central character that absorbs the scalar;
             3. replacing Tr by a balanced supertrace;
             4. constructing an actual closed-exchange counterterm with leading class −ℏN [cˉ].
            These are mechanisms, not an exhaustive classification unless one proves the full
            obstruction cohomology.
            This resolves blocker 10.

            25. Global and cross-volume firewall
            The local theorem supplies only the formal-disk data

           No compact Calabi-Yau, Vol III, Igusa/BKM, MNOP, chiral-volume, or global BCOV
           theorem follows without separate matched-conventions data.
           A matched-conventions datum must preserve:

            2. orientations;
            3. trace pairings;

           This resolves blockers 11 and 12.

            1. First correction: no naive finite Lie quotients
            The manuscript still contains wording like “finite cotangent windows” h≤M ⋉ D≤M [1],
            with obstruction symbols obr , ocoad, oCas. That is useful as bookkeeping, but it is not the
                                                                                                                          ​   ​




            right foundational theorem because ordinary Taylor-degree truncations of the
            Hamiltonian algebra are not naturally Lie quotients unless one imposes extra truncation
            rules. The current status table already warns that the Casimir term belongs to the
            stronger endpoint problem, not coordinate CE/PV; the fix is to formalize this distinction
            completely.
            Replace the finite-window theorem with two separate statements.
            Theorem 1.1: finite cotangent CE/PV for an abstract finite Lie
            algebra
            Let l be a finite-dimensional Lie algebra over C. Choose a basis ei , dual basis ϵi , and
            structure constants
                                                                                                                      ​




                                                                     [ei , ej ] = Cijk ek .
                                                                           ​       ​            ​           ​




            Define the shifted cotangent Lie algebra
                                                                    T ∗ [1]l = l ⋉ l∨ [1],

            with bracket
                                                                     [ei , ej ] = Cijk ek ,
                                                                           ​       ​            ​           ​




                                                             [ei , ϵj [1]] = (ad∨ei ϵj )[1],
                                                                ​



                                                                                                    ​
                                                                                                        ​




                                                                     [ϵi [1], ϵj [1]] = 0.

            Let ci be the CE coordinate dual to ei . Let ui be the CE coordinate dual to ϵi [1]. Use the
            cotangent-coordinate grading
                                                                       ​               ​




                                                             ∣ci ∣ = 1,                    ∣ui ∣ = 0.   ​




https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa84c-a760-83ea-ae70-183a06ecd2d3                                                                        378/510
30/04/2026, 00:40                                                                                                       Whitepaper Critical Analysis

            Then
                                                        ∙
                                                       CCE (T ∗ [1]l) = S(ui ) ⊗ Λ(ci ).
                                                                 ​                                                                                                    ​




            The CE differential is
                                                                               1
                                                                     dCE ck = − Cijk ci cj ,
                                                                               2
                                                                                ​                                                         ​                               ​




                                                                                ℓ
                                                                      dCE uk = Ckj uℓ c j .     ​           ​                                         ​                               ​




            Now let
                                                                     Al = S(l) = O(l∨ ),
                                                                        ​




            with linear coordinate Oi corresponding to ei . The Kirillov-Poisson bracket is
                                                  ​                                                                       ​




                                                                     {Oi , Oj } = Cijk Ok . ​                       ​                                                         ​                   ​




            Let
                                                                                                                         ∂
                                                                                                θi =                        .
                                                                                                                        ∂Oi
                                                                                                                                                              ​




                                                                                                                                                          ​




            Then
                                                             PV(Al ) = S(Oi ) ⊗ Λ(θi ), ​                                                     ​




            with Poisson bivector
                                                                                                            1 k

                                                                                                            2

            The Schouten differential

            Define

            Then
                                                          1

            and

                                                                                             ​     ​                    ​




            All same-type generator brackets vanish. The biderivation rule gives bracket
            compatibility on the full algebra.
            That is the finite theorem. It is independent of the formal disk. The draft already has this
            theorem in spirit in the cotangent CE/PV block.
            Theorem 1.2: the formal disk is reconstructed by coordinate tests,
            not Lie quotients
            The formal disk theorem is not obtained by pretending that Taylor truncations are finite
            Lie algebras. It is obtained by a coordinate-completion construction with finite
            coordinate tests.

            2. Coordinate category for the formal disk
            Let
                                                      I = {(a, b) ∈ Z2≥0 ∣ a + b > 0}.                     ​




            Write

30/04/2026, 00:40                                                                           Whitepaper Critical Analysis

                                                                             Ha,b = z1a z2b .
                                                                                        ​             ​   ​




            Let
                                                      h = C[[z1 , z2 ]]/C ⋅ 1 ≅ ∏ CHI .
                                                                         ​          ​                                    ​       ​




                                                                                                              I∈I

            Its continuous dual is
                                                                        h∨cont = ⨁ CδI .
                                                                                ​                 ​           ​




                                                                                            I∈I

            The bracket is
                                                [Ha,b , Hc,d ] = (ad − bc)Ha+c−1,b+d−1 ,
                                                         ​          ​                                                                ​




            with
                                           Hr,s = 0
                                                 ​
                                                              if r < 0, s < 0, or (r, s) = (0, 0).

            Thus
                                                     (r,s)
                                                C(a,b),(c,d) = (ad − bc)δr,a+c−1 δs,b+d−1 .                                  ​           ​




            Now define a coordinate product algebra.
            Let X = {xi }i∈I be countably many even or odd variables. Let
                                ​      ​




                                                                                        N(I)

            denote finite-support multiindices. For exterior variables, let
                                                                               {0, 1}(I)

            denote finite subsets.
            The coordinate-completed symmetric/exterior algebra on variables uI , cI is                                                      ​




                                                      Ocoord =  ​
                                                                                            ∏                     ​   C uα c β .
                                                                             α∈N(I) , β∈{0,1}(I)

            Multiplication is well-defined because for each fixed output pair (γ, δ), there are only
            finitely many decompositions
                                                             (γ, δ) = (α, β) + (α′ , β ′ ).

https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa84c-a760-83ea-ae70-183a06ecd2d3                                                                                       381/510
30/04/2026, 00:40                                                                   Whitepaper Critical Analysis

            A linear map
                                                                T : Ocoord → Ocoord     ​                             ​




            is coordinate-continuous if for every output monomial coefficient, the coefficient of
            T (F ) depends on only finitely many coefficients of F .

            This is the correct replacement for “finite windows.”
            A finite coordinate test is:
                                                              πΓ : Ocoord → ⨁ C m
                                                                    ​           ​                             ​




                                                                                                 m∈Γ

            for a finite set Γ of monomials. Two coordinate-continuous maps are equal if and only if
            their composites with πΓ agree for all finite Γ.
                                                  ​




            3. Coordinate CE algebra of the formal disk
            Define
                                                              ∙
                                                             CCE,coord (g) = ∏ C uα cβ ,
                                                                            ​                             ​




                                                                                             α,β

            where
                                                                        g = h ⋉ h∨cont [1].           ​




            Here cI is dual to HI , and uI is dual to the shifted cotangent generator δI [1].
                                            ​            ​                                                                ​




            Define the differential on generators by
                                                                       1
                                                             dCE cK = − ∑ CIJ c c .
                                                                           K I J
                                                                       2
                                                                ​                   ​             ​               ​




                                                                                            I,J

            For fixed K = (r, s), the condition
                                                                I + J − (1, 1) = K

            has finitely many solutions in I × I . Thus dCEcK is a finite sum.               ​




            For uK , define
                       ​




https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa84c-a760-83ea-ae70-183a06ecd2d3                                                                    382/510
30/04/2026, 00:40                                                            Whitepaper Critical Analysis

                                                             dCE uK = ∑ CKJ
                                                                ​       ​
                                                                         L
                                                                            uL c J .
                                                                                  ​       ​           ​




                                                                            J,L

            For fixed K, J , L is determined by
                                                              L = K + J − (1, 1).

            Hence dCEuK is generally infinite, but it is coordinate-continuous: for any fixed output
            monomial, only finitely many input monomials can contribute to its coefficient.
                               ​       ​




            Extend dCE as a graded derivation.
                                   ​




            Lemma 3.1: dCE is coordinate-continuous
                                            ​




            Fix an output monomial uγ cδ . In dCE(uα cβ ), a contributing term arises by replacing one
            factor cK by two c’s or replacing one factor uK by uLcJ .
                                                                    ​




                                                                                      ​           ​




            Because uγ cδ has finite support, there are only finitely many positions and only finitely
            many possible K, L, J that can produce it. Therefore its coefficient in dF depends on
            finitely many coefficients of F .
            So dCE is a well-defined coordinate-continuous derivation.
                           ​




            Lemma 3.2: d2CE = 0                 ​




            It is enough to check on generators.
            For cK , d2cK = 0 is exactly the Jacobi identity for the structure constants CIJK .               ​




            For uK , the identity d2uK = 0 is exactly the representation identity for the coadjoint
            action.
                       ​                                ​




            Every coefficient of d2uK is a finite sum, so the formal coordinate calculation is
            legitimate.
                                                    ​




            Thus
                                                                 ∙
                                                               (CCE,coord (g), dCE )
                                                                              ​               ​           ​




            is a well-defined coordinate dg commutative algebra.

            4. Coordinate polyvector algebra

            Define
                                                              A∂,coord = ∏ C Oα .   ​                           ​




                                                                                                    α∈N(I)

            Define
                                                         PVcoord (A∂ ) = ∏ C Oα θβ .
                                                                       ​                ​                           ​

            The linear Poisson tensor is written formally as
                                                                      1
                                                             π=         ∑ CIJ
                                                                           K
                                                                              OK θ I θ J .
                                                                      2
                                                                               ​                ​          ​                ​




                                                                                   I,J,K

            Do not claim that π is an element of an unrestricted ambient product with a globally
            defined Schouten bracket. Instead define its Lichnerowicz differential on generators:
                                                                      1
                                                             dπ θK = − ∑ CIJ θ θ ,
                                                                          K I J
                                                                      2
                                                              ​                             ​              ​                    ​




                                                                                                     I,J

                                                                  ​
                                                                        L
                                                                           OL θ J .
                                                                           ​                          ​                 ​           ​




                                                                                        J,L

            The first formula is finite for fixed K . The second is coordinate-continuous for the same
            reason as dCEuK .          ​   ​




            Extend dπ as a graded derivation.
                           ​




            Lemma 4.1: dπ is coordinate-continuous
                                               ​




            The same finite-output argument as Lemma 3.1 applies. For any fixed output monomial,
            only finitely many input monomials and generator replacements can contribute.
            Lemma 4.2: d2π = 0                     ​




            On θK , d2π = 0 is Jacobi.
                               ​




            On OK , d2π = 0 is the representation identity of the coadjoint action.
                       ​           ​




            Equivalently, every finite coordinate projection is the corresponding finite
            Schouten/Jacobi calculation.
            Thus
https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa84c-a760-83ea-ae70-183a06ecd2d3                                                                              384/510
30/04/2026, 00:40                                                                                     Whitepaper Critical Analysis

                                                                         (PVcoord (A∂ ), dπ )                 ​       ​       ​




            is a coordinate dg commutative algebra.
            Important restriction
            The full Schouten contraction
                                                                       ∂F ∂G          (∣F ∣−1)(∣G∣−1) ∂G ∂F
                                [F , G]S = ∑ (                                 − (−1)                         )
                                                                       ∂θI ∂OI                        ∂θI ∂OI
                                             ​             ​                 ​            ​                                                                   ​           ​




                                                                                      ​                                                                               ​




                                                       I

            is not automatically defined for arbitrary coordinate-product series F , G. A single
            output coefficient may receive infinitely many contributions from the sum over I .
            Therefore
                                                                                 PVcoord (A∂ )           ​        ​




            is a coordinate dg commutative algebra, but not automatically a global P0-algebra.                                                                                ​




            This is exactly the coordinate-versus-continuous distinction the manuscript must not
            blur. The current draft already warns that the strict ambient product completion is not
            asserted to carry the global continuous contraction bracket. main

            5. The coordinate CE/PV theorem
            Define
                                                               ∙
                                                     Φcoord : CCE,coord
                                                                   ​    (g) → PVcoord (A∂ )   ​                                           ​   ​




            by
                                                 Φcoord (cI ) = θI ,
                                                               ​                                             Φcoord (uI ) = OI .
                                                                                                                          ​           ​           ​       ​




            Then
                                                 1
                             Φcoord (dCE cK ) = − ∑ CIJK I J
                                                        θ θ = dπ θK = dπ Φcoord (cK ).
                                                 2 I,J
                                        ​        ​                       ​        ​               ​                               ​                   ​           ​




            Also,

https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa84c-a760-83ea-ae70-183a06ecd2d3                                                                                                                        385/510
30/04/2026, 00:40                                                                                 Whitepaper Critical Analysis

                                 Φcoord (dCE uK ) = ∑ CKJ
                                         ​         ​
                                                       L
                                                          OL θJ = dπ OK = dπ Φcoord (uK ).
                                                           ​          ​                   ​        ​                                    ​               ​       ​       ​       ​




                                                                J,L

            Since both differentials are derivations,
                                                               Φcoord dCE = dπ Φcoord .
                                                                                      ​       ​                     ​                           ​




            The map is a bijection of coordinate product bases:
                                                                      uα c β ⟷ O α θ β .

            Therefore:
                                               ∙                                                                                ∼
                                     Φcoord : CCE,coord
                                               ​        (h ⋉ h∨cont [1])
                                                                  ​                                    ​                                    ​   PVcoord (SΠ (h))
                                                                                                                                                            ​       ​       ​




            is an isomorphism of coordinate-completed dg commutative algebras.
            This is the fully rigorous central theorem in the reduced formal Hamiltonian sector.
            It proves the generator identity already highlighted in the latest manuscript:
                                                               cI ↦ θ I ,                                      uI ↦ O I .
                                                                                                                        ​                           ​




            Boundary Hamiltonian observables come from uI , not from cI .                                                   ​




            6. Bracket-admissible P0 -realizations                                ​




            The coordinate theorem is not yet a global P0-theorem. To get one, define admissibility.   ​




            A subalgebra
                                                                  B ⊂ PVcoord (A∂ )                             ​                   ​




            is bracket-admissible if:
             1. B is a complete graded-commutative subalgebra;
             2. B is stable under dπ ;
             3. the Schouten contraction sum defining [F , G]S converges for all F , G ∈ B;
                                                       ​




             4. [F , G]S ∈ B;
                                                                                                                                ​




             5. Φ−1coord (B) is closed under the transported cotangent bracket.
                                 ​




                             ​




            Then
                                                                ΦPB0 : Φ−1
                                                                        coord (B) → B
                                                                          ​




                                                                              ​                            ​




https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa84c-a760-83ea-ae70-183a06ecd2d3                                                                                                                          386/510
30/04/2026, 00:40                                                                                         Whitepaper Critical Analysis

            is an isomorphism of dg P0-algebras.        ​




            This is the bracketed theorem.
            Explicit admissible realization 1: finite-support polynomial
            polyvectors
            Let
                                                                    Bfin = C[OI ]fin ⊗ Λ(θI )fin ,
                                                                                  ​                   ​      ​                           ​




            where every element uses only finitely many labels I .
            Then the Schouten contraction sum is finite. Hence Bfin is bracket-admissible.                                       ​




            This gives the rigorous finite-support P0-model.                              ​




            Explicit admissible realization 2: weighted rapidly decaying
            completion
            Fix q > 1. For n ∈ N, define the weight
                                                            wq,n (a, b) = q a+b (1 + a + b)n .
                                                                          ​




            Let
                       Eq = {x = ∑ xI HI ∥x∥q,n := ∑ ∣xI ∣wq,n (I) < ∞ for all n} .
                            ​                       ​       ​         ​       ​               ​                      ​       ​               ​




                                               I                                                                 I

            This is a Fréchet space of exponentially decaying Taylor coefficients with all polynomial
            moments finite.
            The Poisson bracket is continuous on Eq . Indeed, for monomials,                      ​




                                                   [Ha,b , Hc,d ] = (ad − bc)Ha+c−1,b+d−1 ,
                                                                ​                     ​                                                          ​




            and
                                                                    ∣ad − bc∣ ≤ (a + b)(c + d).

            Because the exponential weight is submultiplicative up to a constant,
                                wq,n (a + c − 1, b + d − 1) ≤ Cq,n wq,n+2 (a, b)wq,n+2 (c, d),
                                      ​                                                                                  ​           ​               ​




            the convolution estimate gives
https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa84c-a760-83ea-ae70-183a06ecd2d3                                                                                               387/510
30/04/2026, 00:40                                                                             Whitepaper Critical Analysis

                                                   ∥[x, y]∥q,n ≤ Cq,n ∥x∥q,n+2 ∥y∥q,n+2 .
                                                                     ​                        ​                           ​               ​




            Thus the bracket is continuous.
            Similarly, the contraction sum defining the Schouten bracket is continuous on the
            corresponding nuclear/Köthe polyvector completion built from Eq and its strong dual. In
            that weighted realization, the diagonal contraction is summable because coefficients in
                                                                                                                                              ​




            one factor decay exponentially while dual coefficients grow at most with the controlled
            reciprocal weight.
            Call this completion
                                                                                          Bq .    ​




            Then
                                                                                          Bq              ​




            is bracket-admissible.
            This gives an explicit mixed/weighted P0-realization.             ​




            Non-example: strict product/direct-sum endpoint
            Let
                                                   hΠ = ∏ CHI ,
                                                       ​         ​                ​                           h∨⊕ = ⨁ CδI .
                                                                                                                  ​               ​   ​




                                                             I                                                                I

            The diagonal tensor
                                                                         ∑ HI ⊗ δ I   ​                               ​




                                                                          I

            does not lie in
                                                                              hΠ ⊗h∨⊕     ​           ​       ​




            because the second factor has infinitely many nonzero components. Equivalently, the
            infinite contraction sum is not a continuous operation on the entire product coordinate
            algebra.
            This is the Casimir/endpoint obstruction. It is not a hypothesis for the coordinate CE/PV
            theorem. It belongs to the strict kernel, propagator, endpoint, and Costello problem. The
            current draft already says the Casimir transition term diagnoses failure of strict global
https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa84c-a760-83ea-ae70-183a06ecd2d3                                                                                        388/510
30/04/2026, 00:40                                                                                             Whitepaper Critical Analysis

            kernel/ambient-product bracket/bar-cobar package and is not an extra hypothesis for
            the finite coordinate CE/PV identity. main

            7. The missing object: the universal coordinate coupling
            For a finite-dimensional l, the map
                                                                                                              Φl   ​




            is equivalently the Maurer-Cartan element
                               Θl = ∑ ei ⊗ θi + ∑ ϵi [1] ⊗ Oi ∈ (T ∗ [1]l) ⊗ PV(S(l)).
                                    ​           ​   ​                                     ​                                ​




                                            i                             i

            It satisfies
                                                                       1
                                                                  dΘl + [Θl , Θl ] = 0.
                                                                       2
                                                                                  ​                       ​        ​           ​




            For the formal disk, the expression
                                                             ∑ H I ⊗ θ I + ∑ ηI ⊗ O I
                                                                  ​                   ​                                                    ​               ​               ​




                                                              I                                                        I

            is not automatically a tensor in the strict product/direct-sum topology. Therefore the
            universal object is not a naked infinite Casimir. It is the coordinate morphism plus its
            admissible kernel realizations.
            Define
                                                                          Ucoord
                                                                           h     := Φcoord .          ​                                                ​           ​




            For every bracket-admissible B, define
                                                                                          UBh := ΦPB0 .
                                                                                                  ​
                                                                                                                                   ​




                                                                                                                                       ​           ​




            For every kernel-admissible B in which the diagonal tensor converges, define
                                                    ΘB = ∑ H I ⊗ θ I + ∑ η I ⊗ O I .
                                                         ​                    ​               ​                                                                ​       ​       ​   ​




                                                                      I                                                                        I


            Then ΘB is a Maurer-Cartan element:
                          ​




https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa84c-a760-83ea-ae70-183a06ecd2d3                                                                                                                             389/510
30/04/2026, 00:40                                                                                        Whitepaper Critical Analysis
                                                                      1
                                                                 dΘB + [ΘB , ΘB ] = 0.
                                                                      2
                                                                               ​             ​                 ​                                   ​




            Proof is finite-coordinate. For every finite output coordinate, the equation reduces to the
            finite CE/PV computation and Jacobi.
            Thus the central object is:
                                                       Uh = (Φcoord , {ΦPB0 }, {ΘB }) .
                                                             ​                                   ​
                                                                                                                               ​




                                                                                                                                   ​                       ​       ​




            This is the rigorous replacement for any informal “universal Casimir closed-open map.”

            8. Koszul duality as a coupling classifier
            Let A be a complete classical P0-algebra with a bracket-admissible polyvector center
                                                                  ​




                                                                                            Z P0 (A).​




            Let
                                                                                        ∙
                                                                                   B = CCE (g)                     ​




            be a closed CE algebra.
            A closed-open coupling is a morphism
                                                                          κ : B → Z P0 (A).                                ​




            In coordinates, a map
                                                                           ∙
                                                                          CCE (g) → Z P0 (A)
                                                                                    ​
                                                                                                                                               ​




            is equivalent to a family
                                                       X I = κ(cI ),                                               μI = κ(uI ),            ​                   ​




            satisfying
                                                                       1 K I J
                                                             dZ X K = − CIJ X X ,
                                                                       2
                                                                      ​                                    ​                           ​




                                                                           L
                                                                  dZ μK = CKJ
                                                                           ​  μL X J .  ​                              ​                               ​




            For the Dirac brane probe,
https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa84c-a760-83ea-ae70-183a06ecd2d3                                                                                                             390/510
30/04/2026, 00:40                                                                      Whitepaper Critical Analysis
                                                                      I        I
                                                              X =θ ,                                μ I = OI .
                                                                                                         ​      ​




            Thus the CE/PV theorem is a universal solution to the closed-open coupling equations.
            If a bar-cobar/Koszul duality theorem is available in an admissible conilpotent envelope,
            then
                                                                              B ! ≃ U (g),

            and the same coupling is an action of U (g) by derived central operations.
            That is the correct meaning of “Koszul duality” here:
             Koszul duality classifies bulk-boundary couplings once the coefficient category is ad

            The current manuscript’s stratified Koszul note already recommends separating the
            layers: operadic Lie/coComm duality, Tate CE/bar-cobar, Tate Quillen, CE/PV, PBW
            special fibre, Matlis cotangent, brane-line currents, and the conjectural stable bulk-
            boundary horizon. Pasted text

            9. Bar-cobar admissibility
            Do not use bar-cobar/Koszul enveloping duality for the full formal Hamiltonian algebra
            without hypotheses.
            A filtered Tate Lie algebra gadm is bar-cobar admissible if:
                                                              ​




             1. it has a complete separated descending filtration F ∙gadm ;
             2. [F p , F q ] ⊂ F p+q ;
                                                                                                                            ​




             3. each grpF gadm is finite-dimensional;
             4. the CE coalgebra C∗CE(gadm ) is conilpotent for the induced filtration;
                                 ​        ​




             5. the completed PBW filtration on U (gadm ) is complete and separated;
                                                      ​           ​




             6. the completed symmetric associated graded exists:
                                                                                            ​




                                                                      gr U (gadm ) ≅ S (gadm ).
                                                                                        ​                               ​




            Only then assert
                                                                                                ∼
                                                             ΩC∗CE (gadm )​        ​                 ​   U (gadm ). ​




            The full Hamiltonian algebra h is perfect and has linear/quadratic nonconilpotent pieces.
            Pronilpotence must not be silently treated as conilpotence.
https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa84c-a760-83ea-ae70-183a06ecd2d3                                                                      391/510
30/04/2026, 00:40                                                                                           Whitepaper Critical Analysis

            The current draft’s theorem lane already states that the conilpotent bar-cobar template
            is used only in its own hypothesis, and that the untruncated formal Hamiltonian algebra
            is not used as lower-central conilpotent input.

            10. Matlis cotangent module
            Let
                                                          R = C[[z1 , z2 ]],​                       ​                 m = (z1 , z2 ).                             ​




            The local cohomology module is
                                               Hm2 (R) = R[z1−1 , z2−1 ]/(R[z1−1 ] + R[z2−1 ]).
                                                  ​                                         ​               ​                             ​                                           ​




            A basis is
                                                                z1−a−1 z2−b−1 ,
                                                                        ​
                                                                                                                      a, b ≥ 0.

            Define
                                                                ρa,b = z1−a−1 z2−b−1 dz1 dz2 .
                                                                    ​                                   ​                 ​           ​                               ​




            The residue pairing is
                                    ⟨ρa,b , z1m z2n ⟩ = Res0 z1m−a−1 z2n−b−1 dz1 dz2 = δa,m δb,n .
                                           ​          ​     ​                   ​                               ​                 ​                       ​               ​               ​   ​




            Since ρ0,0 pairs with the constant 1, and constants have been removed from h, define
                           ​




                                                          P = Annres (C ⋅ 1) = ⨁ Cρa,b .​                                                             ​                       ​




                                                                                                                          a+b>0

            Then
                                                                                        P ≅ h∨cont .                  ​       ​




            The coadjoint action is
                                                                ⟨f ⋅ ρ, g⟩ = −⟨ρ, {f , g}⟩.

            For
                                                                f = z1p z2q ,       ​           ​                   g = z1m z2n ,             ​               ​




                                                          {f , g} = (pn − qm)z1p+m−1 z2q+n−1 .                                                    ​                               ​




https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa84c-a760-83ea-ae70-183a06ecd2d3                                                                                                                                        392/510
30/04/2026, 00:40                                                                          Whitepaper Critical Analysis

            Therefore
                                            z1p z2q ⋅ ρa,b = −(pb − qa + p − q)ρa−p+1,b−q+1 .
                                              ​       ​      ​                                                                ​




            This is the Hamiltonian coadjoint principal-part action. It is not the Matlis R-module
            action, and it is not the PBW action on Ψa,b . The local dictionary in the latest draft
            already states this separation. main
                                                                                       ​




            11. PBW Ψ versus Matlis ρ
            The polynomial one-ψ descendants Ψa,b are PBW special-fibre labels.​




            They are not the Matlis coadjoint module.
            The no-go proof is elementary.
            The polynomial descendant action contains
                                                                   O1,0 ⋅ Ψa,b = b Ψa,b−1 .
                                                                       ​                                       ​




            Thus O1,0 acts locally nilpotently on every finite polynomial descendant expression.
                           ​




            But on principal parts,
                                                                 z1 ⋅ ρa,b = −(b + 1)ρa,b+1 .
                                                                   ​       ​                                       ​




            So
                                    z1r ⋅ ρa,b = (−1)r (b + 1)(b + 2) ⋯ (b + r)ρa,b+r =
                                        ​         ​                                   0                                  ​




            for every r.
            Local nilpotence is invariant under module isomorphism. Hence
                                                                           ⟨Ψa,b ⟩ ≅
                                                                                   h P .
                                                                                   ​             ​         ​




            This proves the firewall:
                    Ψa,b = PBW special-fibre label,
                               ​
                                                                                                ρa,b = Matlis coadjoint datum.
                                                                                                       ​                          ​




            The principal-parts note gives the physical reading: principal parts are brane-localized

            modules.

            12. Factorization variance
            This is another place where the paper must become more precise.
            Let
                                                                    hI = Ω∙c (I)⊗h.
                                                                          ​                       ​                       ​




            For an inclusion j : I ↪ J , extension by zero gives
                                                                      j! : hI → hJ .
                                                                              ​       ​                               ​




            So
                                                                                  I ↦ hI                      ​




            is a cosheaf of dg Lie algebras.
            The CE chains
                                                                                  C∗CE (hI )  ​           ​




            are covariant under j! .         ​




            The CE cochains
                                                                                   ∙
                                                                                  CCE (hI )
                                                                                          ​           ​




            are contravariant under pullback.
            Therefore raw CE cochains are naturally cofactorization data, not covariant factorization
            data.
            To get a covariant factorization algebra of compactly supported observables, use
            compactly supported density-duals:
                                                                 Obsc (I) = S (h!I [−1]),
                                                                      ​                                           ​




            where h!I is the compactly supported distributional dual. Inclusions act by extension of
            distributions:
                          ​




                                                                      j! : h!I → h!J .
                                                                              ​       ​                               ​




            For disjoint intervals I1, … , In ⊂ J , the factorization product is
                                                 ​           ​




https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa84c-a760-83ea-ae70-183a06ecd2d3                                                                    394/510
30/04/2026, 00:40                                                                                 Whitepaper Critical Analysis

                                           μI1 ,…,In ;J (F1 ⊗ ⋯ ⊗ Fn ) = j1,! F1 ⋯ jn,! Fn .
                                                ​       ​
                                                             ​         ​                                       ​                               ​           ​       ​   ​




            This is the variance-correct equation.
            The brane-line current theorem should be written with this convention.
            The latest draft’s brane-line lane already uses Ω∙c (I), PI = Dc′ (I)⊗P , extension by
            zero, and the shifted-cotangent source ua(t)dt⊗f .

            13. Brane-line current theorem
            For an interval I ⊂ R, define
                                                                            hI = Ω∙c (I)⊗h,
                                                                                      ​                ​                               ​




                                                                            PI = Dc′ (I)⊗P ,
                                                                                  ​                ​                               ​




                                                                           gI = hI ⋉ PI [1].
                                                                              ​               ​                                ​




            The bracket is
                                                     [α ⊗ f , β ⊗ g] = (α ∧ β) ⊗ {f , g},

                                               [α ⊗ f , B ⊗ ρ[1]] = (αB) ⊗ (f ⋅ ρ)[1],

                                                                 [B ⊗ ρ[1], C ⊗ σ[1]] = 0.

            Here αB is multiplication of a distribution by a smooth form/function in the degree-zero
            case.
            The reduced Hamiltonian boundary algebra is
                                                                           AHam
                                                                            ∂   (I) = S (hI ).
                                                                                          ​                                                ​




            The reduced principal-part defect-current algebra is
                                                            App
                                                             ∂ (I) = S (hI )⊗S (PI [1]).
                                                                   ​                                       ​           ​                           ​




            The bracket is generated by
                                                       {a ⊗ f , b ⊗ g} = ab ⊗ {f , g},

                                                    {a ⊗ f , B ⊗ ρ[1]} = aB ⊗ (f ⋅ ρ)[1],

                                                             {B ⊗ ρ[1], C ⊗ σ[1]} = 0.
