            the shifted cotangent CE coordinate maps to

                                                                         pi = id.

                                                                        ∫ ω = 0,  ​




                                                                         I

            choose a compactly supported primitive Kω satisfying
                                                                        dKω = ω.

            17. Scalar anomaly and QME obstruction
            Define
                                                                          hpoly = C[z1 , z2 ].
                                                                                      ​                     ​       ​




            Define
                                                                         ω(f , g) = [{f , g}]0 ,                                ​




            the constant Taylor coefficient.
            For monomials,
                                           ω(z1k z2l , z1m z2n ) = (kn − lm)1(k+m,l+n)=(1,1) .
                                                   ​   ​             ​    ​                                                                             ​




            Jacobi implies ω is a Lie 2-cocycle.
            On hpoly , define
                         ​




                                                                                  η(f ) = −[f ]0 .              ​




            Then
                                         δη(f , g) = −η({f , g}) = [{f , g}]0 = ω(f , g).                                               ​




            Thus ω is exact before scalar reduction.
            But η does not descend to
                                                                              Aˉ = hpoly /C ⋅ 1         ​




https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa84c-a760-83ea-ae70-183a06ecd2d3                                                                                                  400/510
30/04/2026, 00:40                                                           Whitepaper Critical Analysis

            because
                                                                  η(1) = −1.

            So ω defines a distinguished scalar-extension class
                                                                       2
                                                               [cˉ] ∈ HLie (Aˉ, C).
                                                                              ​




            On the brane,
                                                              {Tr ϕ1 , Tr ϕ2 } = N .
                                                                     ​                ​




            Quantum mechanically, the same scalar appears as
                                                             Y X − XY + ℏN I = 0.

            The current draft already proves this closed/open/quantum scalar equivalence and
            marks it as the U (1) anomaly lane.
            The mixed brane-defect QME scalar obstruction is
                                               Obsc = ℏN ω (f , g ) ∫ a(t)b(t)γ1 (t) dt.
                                                        ​                                 ​         ​




                                                                                  R

            Its class is
                                                                         ℏN [cˉ].

            The known cancellation mechanisms are:
             1. unreduced scalar primitive: keep 1 and use ω = δη;
             2. central character: absorb the scalar by a boundary central character;
             3. balanced supertrace: replace Tr by a trace with zero superdimension;
             4. closed-exchange counterterm: construct a graph/counterterm with class −ℏN [cˉ].
            These are mechanisms, not an exhaustive classification of all possible cancellations. The
            draft correctly says QME cancellation mechanisms are partial, not exhaustive. main

            This construction plugs the real central gap.
            It gives:
             finite theorem ⇒ coordinate formal-disk theorem ⇒ bracket-admissible P0 theorem            ​

             The reduced Hamiltonian current model does not prove the full unreduced closed-o

            This is not merely a rhetorical caution. It is a precise non-implication theorem.

            1. The full theorem that is not proved
            Let
                                                                 h = C[[z1 , z2 ]]/C ⋅ 1    ​           ​




            be the reduced Hamiltonian Lie algebra of the formal symplectic disk, and let
                                                                     g = h ⋉ h∨cont [1]                                         ​




            be the shifted cotangent Hamiltonian Lie algebra.
            The full desired unreduced theorem would be a morphism of P0-factorization objects                                                              ​




                                                            ∙         P0
                                                  Φunred : CCE
                                                             ​ (g) ⟶ Zfact
                                                                        ​  (Obsopen )                                               ​




                                                                                                                                        ​               ​       ​




            where Obsopen is the unreduced open BV factorization algebra of the brane, not its
            stable scalar-reduced Hamiltonian cohomology and not the post-contraction principal-
                                   ​




            part current model.
            At the level of generators, such a theorem must produce:
                                                         cI ⟼ θI ∈ Zfact
                                                                    P0
                                                                         (Obsopen ),                ​




                                                                                                                    ​                       ​




                                                                   P0
                                                        uI ⟼ OI ∈ Zfact (Obsopen ),
                                                                            ​
                                                                                                                ​




                                                                                                                            ​                       ​




https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa84c-a760-83ea-ae70-183a06ecd2d3                                                                                                          409/510
30/04/2026, 00:40                                                                               Whitepaper Critical Analysis

            where cI is the CE coordinate dual to a Hamiltonian generator HI , and uI is the CE
            coordinate dual to the shifted cotangent generator. The reduced theorem already fixes
                                                                                                                                                       ​               ​




            the correct type convention:
                                                                        cI ↦ θ I ,                  uI ↦ O I .
                                                                                                        ​                   ​




            The boundary Hamiltonian observable comes from uI , not from cI . The manuscript
            states this explicitly and treats it as the type check behind the factorization-current
                                                                                                                    ​




            theorem. main
            But the full unreduced theorem asks for those images inside the derived P0-
            factorization center of the full open BV algebra.
                                                                                                                                                                           ​




            That is much stronger than a reduced current identity.

            2. The unreduced open BV algebra
            The ghost-zero local open model at a point is generated by two even matrices and one
            odd Koszul variable:
                                                       ϕ1 , ϕ2 ∈ glN ,
                                                                ​           ​               ​
                                                                                                      ψ ∈ glN [1],              ​




            with
                                                 Qϕ1 = Qϕ2 = 0,
                                                        ​                           ​                  Qψ = [ϕ1 , ϕ2 ].               ​       ​




            Equivalently,
                                   RN = C[(ϕ1 )ij , (ϕ2 )ij ] ⊗ Λ((ψ)ij ),
                                         ​                  ​       ​           ​       ​                   ​                       Qψ = [ϕ1 , ϕ2 ].           ​   ​




            On an interval I ⊂ R, the actual open BV/factorization object is not merely RN . It
            includes the compactly supported de Rham direction, local fields, jets, gauge/BV data,
                                                                                                                                                                               ​




            and factorization products. Schematically,
                                                                                                                                                  GLN
                                             Obsopen,N (I) = (S (Eopen,c
                                                                  !
                                                                         (I)), Qopen )
                                                                                                                                                           ​




                                                                        ​                                       ​                         ​




            with
                                       Qopen = dR + QKoszul + Qgauge/BV + {Iopen , −}.
                                                 ​              ​                           ​                           ​                          ​




            The reduced local algebra
https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa84c-a760-83ea-ae70-183a06ecd2d3                                                                                                                         410/510
30/04/2026, 00:40                                                                                       Whitepaper Critical Analysis
                                                                                GLN     0
                                                                            H (RN   , Q)                             ​




            is only the cohomology of the pointwise ghost-zero Koszul model. The full factorization
            algebra contains more information:
             1. chain-level Q-data, not only Q-cohomology;
             2. de Rham dependence along the interval, not only its contracted stalk;
             3. products and higher factorization operations, not only primitive indecomposables;
             4. non-Hamiltonian open observables, not only stable connected trace Hamiltonians;
             5. finite-N trace identities before stabilization;
             6. scalar U (1) data before scalar reduction;
             7. gauge/BV descendants and homotopies.
            The reduced Hamiltonian model deliberately discards or contracts most of this
            information.

            3. The reduced Hamiltonian current model
            The reduced brane-line Hamiltonian current algebra is
                                                                           hI = Ω∙c (I)⊗h.
                                                                                    ​                        ​                   ​




            The reduced Hamiltonian boundary algebra is
                                                                          AHam
                                                                           ∂   (I) = S (hI ).
                                                                                            ​                                            ​




            The corresponding reduced principal-part extension is
                                                             pp
                                                         A∂ (I) = S (hI )⊗S (PI [1]),
                                                                      ​                                          ​       ​                       ​           ​




            where
                                                                           PI = Dc′ (I)⊗P ,
                                                                                ​                        ​                   ​




            and
                                   P = h∨cont ≅ Annres (C ⋅ 1) ⊂ Hm2 (C[[z1 , z2 ]]) dz1 dz2 .
                                                    ​                       ​                                                        ​                   ​       ​   ​   ​




            The reduced brackets are strict:
                                                        {Bf (a), Bg (b)} = B{f ,g} (ab),
                                                                  ​                                 ​                                                ​




                                                        {Bf (a), Θρ (B)} = Θf ⋅ρ (aB),
                                                              ​                                 ​                                            ​




https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa84c-a760-83ea-ae70-183a06ecd2d3                                                                                                                   411/510
30/04/2026, 00:40                                                                     Whitepaper Critical Analysis

                                                             {Θρ (B), Θσ (C)} = 0.
                                                                          ​               ​




            This is exactly the reduced post-contraction defect-current lane in the manuscript. It is
            explicitly stated to be not an unreduced BV/factorization theorem and not to supply
            centrality homotopies against arbitrary open observables. main
            So the reduced theorem proves a map of the form
                                                               ∙
                                                       Φred : CCE
                                                              ​
                                                                         P0
                                                                  (g) ⟶ Zfact (App
                                                                              ​




                                                                                ∂ )
                                                                                                              ​




                                                                                                                  ​            ​   ​




            or its Hamiltonian subcase
                                                              ∙
                                                             CCE (g) ⟶ PV(S (h)).
                                                                      ​




            It does not automatically prove
                                                          ∙         P0
                                                         CCE (g) ⟶ Zfact
                                                                  ​
                                                                         (Obsopen ).              ​




                                                                                                      ​                    ​




            4. The reduction functor and why it loses information
            The reduced model is obtained from the unreduced open BV algebra by a composite of
            operations. Abstractly, write
                                                                                                                  pp
                                                             R : Obsopen ⟶ A∂ .               ​                        ​




            This R is not a quasi-isomorphism of full P0-factorization algebras. It is a reduction
            procedure consisting of several non-faithful steps:
                                                                                      ​




                                            Obsopen ⟶ pointwise BV/Koszul model
                                                         ​




                                                                      ⟶ H ∙ (QKoszul )                    ​




                                                             ⟶ GLN -invariants    ​




                                      ⟶ N → ∞ degreewise stable connected traces

                                           ⟶ scalar-reduced Hamiltonian quotient

                                            ⟶ primitive indecomposable projection

                                                  ⟶ de Rham-current contraction

                                          ⟶ adjoined principal-part current model.

https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa84c-a760-83ea-ae70-183a06ecd2d3                                                                             412/510
30/04/2026, 00:40                                                    Whitepaper Critical Analysis

            Each arrow loses structure.
            Most importantly:
                passing to H ∙(Q) loses chain-level homotopies;
                passing to primitives kills decomposable products;
                scalar reduction kills the U (1) center;
                de Rham contraction kills positive interval-chain data;
                adjoining PI [1] introduces a reduced local-dual current sector not yet constructed
                inside Obsopen;
                                      ​




                stable large N erases finite-N trace identities;
                                          ​




                the principal-part currents are distributional, while the ordinary polynomial open
                observables do not realize them.
            The manuscript makes this distinction directly: the reduced principal-part current
            equation QΘρ = 0 is part of an added reduced local-dual factor and is not yet derived
            from an unreduced de Rham current complex or Hochschild centrality homotopies.
                                  ​




                    main

            Therefore R is not conservative and not full on centers.

            5. Formal non-implication lemma
            Here is the elementary categorical reason the reduced theorem does not imply the
            unreduced theorem.
            Let
                                                               q : A ⟶ Ared       ​




            be a morphism of P0- or E1-algebras. It induces a map on centers
                                              ​          ​




                                                             Z(A) ⟶ Z(Ared ).         ​




            A central operation in Ared does not generally lift to a central operation in A.
                                                     ​




            Equivalently, a diagram
                                                              ∙
                                                             CCE​(g) ⟶ Z(Ared )           ​




            does not imply the existence of a lift
https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa84c-a760-83ea-ae70-183a06ecd2d3                                            413/510
30/04/2026, 00:40                                                          Whitepaper Critical Analysis
                                                                 ∙
                                                                CCE (g) ⟶ Z(A).
                                                                      ​




            The obstruction is the homotopy fiber
                                                             Fib (Z(A) ⟶ Z(Ared )) .         ​




            More explicitly, a lift exists only if the reduced map lies in the image of
                                          ∙                    ∙
                                     Map(CCE (g), Z(A)) ⟶ Map(CCE
                                                      ​           (g), Z(Ared )).                ​        ​




            The reduced theorem only constructs the target point in the right-hand mapping space.
            It does not show that this point lifts.

            6. Elementary counterexample: abelianization does not lift
            centrality
            This is the simplest model of what goes wrong.
            Let
                                                                     A = C⟨x, y⟩

            be the free associative algebra, regarded as a locally constant factorization algebra on
            the line.
            Let
                                                             Ared = A/[x, y] ≅ C[x, y].
                                                                 ​




            In Ared , the class of x is central.
                       ​




            So there is a degree-zero central element
                                                                     ˉ ∈ Z(Ared ).
                                                                     x              ​




            But x ∈ A is not central, because
                                                                     xy − yx =
                                                                              0.

            Thus xˉ ∈ Z(Ared ) does not lift to an element of Z(A).
                                        ​




            In Hochschild language,
https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa84c-a760-83ea-ae70-183a06ecd2d3                                                    414/510
30/04/2026, 00:40                                                                     Whitepaper Critical Analysis
                                                                     0
                                                    HH (Ared ) = Z(Ared ) = C[x, y],
                                                                              ​                     ​




            whereas
                                                                     HH 0 (A) = Z(A) = C.

            Therefore the map
                                                                     HH 0 (A) → HH 0 (Ared )                        ​




            is not surjective.
            This elementary example proves:
             centrality after quotient/reduction does not imply centrality before quotient/reduc

            The open BV model is subtler because
                                                                         xy − yx = Qψ

            rather than strictly zero. But then the conclusion becomes: one must construct coherent
            Q-homotopies. The reduced model shows equality in cohomology or after primitive
            projection; it does not automatically produce those coherent Hochschild/P0-centrality
            homotopies.
                                                                                                                            ​




            7. What the full unreduced theorem would require
            The full theorem would require actual data.
            For each interval I , one needs a map
                                                         ∙           P0
                                             Φunred,I : CCE (gI ) ⟶ Zfact
                                                             ​            (Obsopen )(I).
                                                                          ​       ​
                                                                                                        ​




                                                                                                                ​       ​




            Equivalently, one must assign central operations
                                                                         C I = Φunred (cI ),    ​




                                                                         UI = Φunred (uI ), ​               ​




            inside the unreduced open P0-factorization center.   ​




            These operations must satisfy the CE/PV equations inside the unreduced center, not
            merely after reduction.
https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa84c-a760-83ea-ae70-183a06ecd2d3                                                                      415/510
30/04/2026, 00:40                                                                                                  Whitepaper Critical Analysis

            The equations are:
                                                                       1 K I J
                                                             DZ C K = − CIJ C C ,
                                                                       2
                                                                     ​                                                             ​            ​




                                                                          L
                                                                 DZ UK = CKJ UL C J .
                                                                             ​         ​                                                   ​                ​




            They must also preserve the cotangent/Schouten bracket:
                                                                             [C I , UJ ] = δJI ,                           ​                            ​




                                                                                 [C I , C J ] = 0,

                                                                                 [UI , UJ ] = 0,
                                                                                               ​                               ​




            up to the appropriate coherent homotopies if the center is modeled homotopically.
            The reduced model verifies these equations only after replacing Obsopen by                                                                                                      ​




                                                                         AHam
                                                                          ∂                        ​               or                          App
                                                                                                                                                ∂ .                     ​




            It does not construct the unreduced operations C I , UI .                                                                                                               ​




            8. The obstruction equations
            Suppose one tries to lift the reduced map.
            Choose arbitrary lifts
                                                                        P0
                                                                 C I ∈ Zfact (Obsopen ),                   ​




                                                                                                                   ​                                                        ​




                                                                       P0
                                                                 UI ∈ Zfact
                                                                         ​  (Obsopen )                         ​




                                                                                                                       ​                                                        ​




            whose reductions are
                                                                                 R(C I ) = θI ,

                                                                                 R(UI ) = OI .         ​                                            ​




            Define curvature terms:
                                                                   1 K I J
                                                         C = DZ C + CIJ C C ,
                                                        oK       K
                                                                   2
                                                                                   ​                                                   ​                        ​




                                                                        L
                                                        oU K = DZ UK − CKJ
                                                             ​   ​         UL C J .        ​                       ​                                                ​                   ​




https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa84c-a760-83ea-ae70-183a06ecd2d3                                                                                                                                      416/510
30/04/2026, 00:40                                                           Whitepaper Critical Analysis

            Because the reduced CE/PV theorem holds,
                                                             R(oK
                                                                C ) = 0,    ​




                                                             R(oU K ) = 0.
                                                                        ​       ​




            Therefore
                                         C , oU K ∈ ker (Zfact (Obsopen ) → Zfact (A∂ )) .
                                                          P0                 P0     pp
                                        oK   ​     ​   ​
                                                                ​




                                                                    ​                    ​
                                                                                                      ​




                                                                                                           ​       ​




            The full theorem requires these kernel-valued curvatures to vanish or be killed by
            coherent higher homotopies.
            The reduced model proves only that the obstructions vanish after reduction.
            It does not prove they vanish before reduction.
            This is the exact obstruction-theoretic form of the gap.

            9. Product centrality homotopies missing from the reduced
            model
            The full P0-factorization center is not just a Poisson bracket identity.
                            ​




            It contains product centrality.
            For a candidate central operation Z and an arbitrary unreduced open observable F , one
            needs a homotopy
                                                                            prod
                                                                    HZ,F            ​




            such that
                                                                                        prod         prod              prod
                    m(Z, F ) − (−1)∣Z∣∣F ∣ m(F , Z) = Qopen HZ,F + HZ,QF + (−1)∣F ∣ HQZ,F .    ​               ​              ​




            This equation must hold for all unreduced open observables F , not just for stable
            connected Hamiltonian traces.
            The reduced model checks product commutativity only after:
             1. passing to stable connected traces;
             2. killing decomposables;
https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa84c-a760-83ea-ae70-183a06ecd2d3                                                                        417/510
30/04/2026, 00:40                                                                          Whitepaper Critical Analysis

             3. imposing Q-cohomology;
             4. contracting the interval de Rham complex;
             5. replacing the cotangent sector by an added current generator.
            That does not supply the homotopies
                                                                                          prod
                                                                                         HZ,F             ​




            inside the unreduced open BV algebra.
            The manuscript itself states that the reduced result does not supply centrality
            homotopies against arbitrary open observables. main

            10. P0 -bracket centrality homotopies missing from the
            reduced model
                          ​




            The full P0-center also requires compatibility with the Poisson bracket.
                              ​




            For a scalar central operation Z , one expects
                                                                              {Z, F }P0 ≃ 0.  ​
                                                                                                  ​




            For Hamiltonian operations, the bracket is not zero; it is identified with the
            corresponding Hamiltonian vector-field operation.
            At the reduced CE/PV level this is encoded by
                                                                              L
                                                                     dπ OK = CKJ
                                                                          ​
                                                                                 OL θ J .
                                                                                 ​                                ​   ​




            At the unreduced level, one must construct a chain homotopy
                                                                                          br
                                                                                         HK,F         ​




            such that
                                                                  br     br
                                   {UK , F }P0 − XK (F ) = Qopen HK,F
                                           ​

                                                     ​
                                                         ​
                                                                      + HK,QF
                                                                      ​
                                                                              +⋯ .                            ​           ​       ​




            Again, this must hold for every unreduced open observable F , not just for reduced
            Hamiltonian traces.
            The reduced current identity
                                                             {Bf (a), Bg (b)} = B{f ,g} (ab)
                                                                 ​                   ​                                        ​




https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa84c-a760-83ea-ae70-183a06ecd2d3                                                                            418/510
30/04/2026, 00:40                                                            Whitepaper Critical Analysis

            only verifies the bracket on the Hamiltonian current subalgebra.
            It does not verify P0-centrality against arbitrary open observables, nor does it produce
            bracket homotopies.
                                           ​




            This is exactly why the manuscript says the full Zfact
                                                                P
                                                                   theorem remains open and
                                                                                         0   ​




            requires centrality homotopies, descent, and QME data. main
                                                                                                     ​




            11. The principal-part current sector is adjoined in the
            reduced model
            The reduced principal-part model introduces generators
                                                                      Θρ (B)
                                                                         ​




            with
                                                             ρ ∈ P,              B ∈ Dc′ (I).            ​




            In the reduced model,
                                                                      QΘρ = 0​




            because Θρ is placed in an added local-dual factor.
                               ​




            But the full unreduced theorem would require Θρ to arise from an actual chain-level
            boundary construction inside, or acting on, Obsopen.
                                                                                     ​




                                                                                                 ​




            The manuscript explicitly says the reduced equation QΘρ = 0 is part of the added
            reduced local-dual factor and is not yet derived from an unreduced de Rham current
                                                                                                             ​




            complex, a chain-level primitive projection, or Hochschild centrality homotopies.
                    main

            Thus the reduced model proves:
                    If we adjoin PI [1] as a reduced current sector, then the bracket closes.
                                               ​




            It does not prove:
            PI [1] is realized by genuine unreduced boundary observables with centrality homoto
                ​




            That is a different theorem.
https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa84c-a760-83ea-ae70-183a06ecd2d3                                                       419/510
30/04/2026, 00:40                                                           Whitepaper Critical Analysis




            12. The primitive projection is only an indecomposable
            shadow
            The chain-level primitive projection kills decomposable products. But the factorization
            center must control products.
            The manuscript explicitly says the primitive projection gives only the primitive
            indecomposable P0-shadow of the reduced comparison, and that it does not construct
            the unreduced cotangent factorization-center morphism or its centrality homotopies.
                                           ​




                    main

            This is a fundamental logical point.
            A factorization-center theorem must know how an operation acts on:
                                                                  F1 F2 ,
                                                                        ​         ​




                                                                 F1 F2 F3 ,
                                                                    ​         ​           ​




            and all higher products in the open algebra.
            An indecomposable projection
                                                                  πprim               ​




            is designed to remove such products.
            Therefore a theorem after πprim cannot, by itself, prove a theorem about the full
            factorization center.
                                                             ​




            It proves only the primitive shadow.

            13. Polynomial one-ψ descendants cannot realize principal
            parts
            The full unreduced theorem needs boundary representatives for the shifted cotangent
            generators.
            A tempting idea is to use the polynomial one-ψ classes
https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa84c-a760-83ea-ae70-183a06ecd2d3                                                 420/510
30/04/2026, 00:40                                                                             Whitepaper Critical Analysis

                                                                                      Ψa,b .     ​




            But this is ruled out.
            In the polynomial descendant sector,
                                                              O1,0 : Ψa,b ↦ b Ψa,b−1 .
                                                                          ​               ​                      ​




            In the principal-part module,
                                                         z1 : ρa,b ↦ −(b + 1)ρa,b+1 .
                                                              ​               ​                                              ​




            Similarly,
                                                             O0,1 : Ψa,b ↦ −a Ψa−1,b ,
                                                                      ​               ​                              ​




            whereas
                                                             z2 : ρa,b ↦ (a + 1)ρa+1,b .
                                                                  ​               ​                                      ​




            So the polynomial one-ψ action and the principal-part coadjoint action shift in opposite
            directions. The manuscript records this explicitly and concludes that the primitive
            projection records labels only; it does not supply centrality homotopies against arbitrary
            unreduced open observables. main
            More invariantly:
                 linear Hamiltonians act locally nilpotently on polynomial observables;
                 linear Hamiltonians do not act locally nilpotently on principal parts.
            Therefore no ordinary polynomial boundary observable sector can realize the principal-
            part coadjoint module.
            Hence the reduced principal-part current target is not obtained from the existing
            polynomial open BV algebra by the primitive projection.
            It has to be built as an additional distributional boundary sector with its own centrality
            homotopies.

            14. The candidate unreduced enlargement and what it still
            lacks
            The manuscript proposes the shape of the needed unreduced enlargement:
https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa84c-a760-83ea-ae70-183a06ecd2d3                                                                       421/510
30/04/2026, 00:40                                                              Whitepaper Critical Analysis
                                            cl,pp
                                         Obs∂,N (I) = Obscl
                                                      ​
                                                                        pp,unred
                                                         open,N (I)⊗S (M∂        (I)),
                                                                                     ​                ​           ​




            where
                                           M∂pp,unred (I ) = Cc,cur
                                                             ​
                                                              dR
                                                                    (I)⊗ Annres (C ⋅ 1)[1].
                                                                           ​              ​                   ​




           Here Cc,cur
                    dR
                        (I) is the compactly supported de Rham current cosheaf, and Annres (C ⋅
           1) is the principal-part residue module.
                               ​                                                                                      ​




                                                          main

           But this is only a candidate object until one constructs:
            1. its full BV differential;
            2. its compactly supported de Rham functoriality before contraction;
            3. its pairing with ordinary open observables before primitive trace projection;
            4. its product centrality homotopies;
            5. its P0-bracket homotopies;
            6. its factorization coherence maps;
                           ​




            7. its compatibility with Qopen;
            8. its compatibility with scalar reduction;
                                                                 ​




            9. its compatibility with stable connected quotient;
           10. its QME behavior if quantized.
           The manuscript states these as remaining obligations. main
           Thus the candidate enlargement clarifies what must be built. It does not itself prove the
           full theorem.

            15. Factorization variance is another missing ingredient
            There is also a variance issue.
            The interval Hamiltonian current algebra
                                                                     hI = Ω∙c (I)⊗h
                                                                       ​         ​                ​




            is covariant under extension by zero:
                                                                 I⊂J       ⇒             hI → hJ .
                                                                                              ​           ​




            Thus it is a cosheaf.

https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa84c-a760-83ea-ae70-183a06ecd2d3                                                                422/510
30/04/2026, 00:40                                                              Whitepaper Critical Analysis

            CE chains are covariant with this cosheaf. CE cochains are contravariant unless one
            passes to a compactly supported density-dual model.
            A full factorization theorem must therefore specify whether it uses:
             1. CE chains and dualizes later;
             2. compactly supported density-dual observables;
             3. a finite/reflexive admissible envelope;
             4. a cofactorization formulation.
            The reduced current model can hide this variance because it works with explicit
            smeared currents and strict brackets.
            The full theorem cannot hide it.
            It must give the coproduct-to-product equations for factorization products on disjoint
            intervals.
            For disjoint intervals
                                                                 I1 , … , In ⊂ J,
                                                                   ​               ​




            one needs maps
                                      Obsopen (I1 ) ⊗ ⋯ ⊗ Obsopen (In ) ⟶ Obsopen (J)
                                                    ​    ​                                 ​   ​              ​




            and corresponding centrality homotopies natural with respect to those maps.
            The reduced pointwise bracket
                                                        {Bf (a), Bg (b)} = B{f ,g} (ab)
                                                             ​             ​                       ​




            does not supply these higher factorization coherence equations.

            16. The scalar U (1) obstruction is killed in the reduced
            model
            The full unreduced theorem must also deal with the scalar line.
            Before scalar reduction,
                                                             {Tr ϕ1 , Tr ϕ2 } = N .
                                                                       ​               ​




https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa84c-a760-83ea-ae70-183a06ecd2d3                                                        423/510
30/04/2026, 00:40                                                              Whitepaper Critical Analysis

            After scalar reduction this is killed.
            On the closed side, the scalar cocycle is
                                                                    ω(f , g) = [{f , g}]0 .     ​




            The scalar-reduced Hamiltonian algebra has no constant Hamiltonian line on which the
            primitive
                                                                       η(f ) = −[f ]0      ​




            can live.
            Quantum mechanically, the same scalar appears as the Capelli/Weyl shift
                             Tr(AXY ) − Tr(AY X) = ℏN Tr(A) mod moment ideal.

            With the central ghost γ1, the scalar contact representative is
                                                   ​




                                               Obsc = ℏN ω(f , g) ∫ a(t)b(t)γ1 (t) dt.
                                                        ​                             ​                ​




                                                                                  R

            A scalar-reduced ordinary glN lift requires a closed-exchange contribution whose
            leading class cancels
                                                                ​




                                                                           ℏN [cˉ].

            Alternatively, one changes the data by using a central character, an unreduced scalar
            primitive, or a balanced supertrace. The manuscript states these alternatives and treats
            the scalar QME obstruction as a real obstruction class. main
            The reduced Hamiltonian current model avoids this obstruction by quotienting the
            scalar.
            Therefore it cannot prove the unreduced scalar-compatible theorem.

            17. The analytic Costello/QME data are also absent
            For the classical reduced P0 current model, one can write strict brackets algebraically.
                                                            ​




            For the full BV/factorization theorem, and especially for its quantum or Costello
            realization, one needs analytic data:

            18. The precise lifting problem
            The full theorem is a lifting problem.
            Let
                                                                       P0
                                                             Zunred = Zfact
                                                                      ​     (Obsopen ),      ​                             ​




            and
                                                                        P0
                                                                Zred = Zfact (App
                                                                              ​




                                                                               ∂ ).
                                                                                                     ​




                                                                                                         ​




            The reduction map induces
                                                               r : Zunred ⟶ Zred .    ​                        ​




            The reduced theorem gives
                                                                      ∙
                                                              Φred : CCE
                                                                  ​      (g) → Zred .
                                                                                  ​                                    ​




https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa84c-a760-83ea-ae70-183a06ecd2d3                                                                     425/510
30/04/2026, 00:40                                                                                        Whitepaper Critical Analysis

            The full theorem is the existence of
                                                                              ∙
                                                                    Φunred : CCE (g) → Zunred
                                                                                     ​                   ​                                                   ​




            such that
                                                                                r ∘ Φunred ≃ Φred .          ​                                   ​




            So the space of possible full lifts is
                                                   ∙                       ∙
                     Lift(Φred ) = hofibΦred [Map(CCE
                                     ​                (g), Zunred ) → Map(CCE
                                                                ​
                                                                    ​         (g), Zred )] .         ​                                               ​           ​   ​




            The reduced model proves that the target point
                                                                                                     Φred            ​




            exists.
            It does not prove that the homotopy fiber is nonempty.
            That is the exact mathematical content of the statement:
              The full theorem is not proved by the reduced Hamiltonian current model.                                                                                   ​




            19. The obstruction complex for the lift

                                                                                      ∙
                                                                                     CCE (g; KΦred ).

                                                                                                                                     ​
                                                                                                                                         ​

                                                                                         CI ,                            UI .        ​

                                                    oK       K

            20. What a complete proof would have to construct

            lifting the Hamiltonian vector-field operation θI .
            For each principal part ρ ∈ P , construct an unreduced distributional boundary
            operation
                                                                                           Θρ .          ​

                                                              DZ UK = CKJ
                                                                   ​      UL C J .
                                                                               ​                                ​       ​




            The reduced model checks these equations only after reduction.
            20.3 Product centrality homotopies

                                                                                         prod

            with
                                            m(U , F ) − (−1)∣U ∣∣F ∣ m(F , U ) = DHUprod

            but only after adding the reduced current target.
            20.5 Factorization coherence
            For inclusions
                                                                                    I⊂J

            and disjoint intervals
                                                                      I1 , … , In ⊂ J,
                                                                        ​                     ​




            all representatives and homotopies must commute with extension by zero and
            factorization products.
            That means diagrams of the form
                                                 Obs(I1 ) ⊗ ⋯ ⊗ Obs(In ) → Obs(J)
                                                             ​                                       ​




            must be compatible with the central operations and their homotopies.
            The reduced bracket identity
                                                        {Bf (a), Bg (b)} = B{f ,g} (ab)
                                                                 ​              ​                            ​




            does not supply these higher coherence diagrams.
            20.6 Scalar anomaly cancellation
            Before scalar reduction, the U (1) class
                                                                                    ℏN [cˉ]

            must be killed or absorbed.
            The reduced model kills it by quotienting.
            The unreduced theorem must provide one of:
             1. unreduced scalar primitive;
             2. central character;
             3. balanced supertrace;
https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa84c-a760-83ea-ae70-183a06ecd2d3                                                               429/510
30/04/2026, 00:40                                                         Whitepaper Critical Analysis

             4. closed-exchange counterterm;
             5. another explicitly constructed null-homotopy in the obstruction complex.
            20.7 Analytic/BV data if quantum
            For the quantum factorization-center theorem one must also construct:
             1. coefficient kernel;
             2. brane-defect propagator;
             3. BV Laplacian;
             4. renormalized counterterms;
             5. QME solution;
             6. compatibility with the Moyal principal-part target.
            The reduced algebraic current model does not include those analytic data.

            21. Why the reduced theorem is still valuable
            The reduced theorem is not wrong or weak. It proves exactly the correct shadow:
                                                               ∙
                                                              CCE (g) ≅ PV(S (h))
                                                                   ​




            and the reduced brane current realization
                                                         App
                                                          ∂ (I) = S (hI )⊗S (PI [1]).
                                                               ​            ​     ​        ​




            It also proves the correct source dictionary
                                                             cI ↦ θ I ,         uI ↦ O I ,
                                                                                      ​        ​




            and the correct reduced principal-part brackets.
            This is the mathematical core of the closed-open picture.
            But it is a theorem about the reduced Hamiltonian current model, not a theorem about
            the full unreduced open BV factorization center.
            The manuscript’s current status ledger says exactly this: the reduced coordinate CE/PV
            comparison is proved under the stated continuity conditions, while the full Zfact
                                                                                         P
                                                                                              of the     0   ​




            open BV algebra remains open and requires centrality homotopies, descent, and QME
                                                                                                                 ​




            data. main

30/04/2026, 00:40                                                              Whitepaper Critical Analysis

            Proof
            The reduction R is not an equivalence of full P0-factorization algebras. It passes
            through cohomology, stable connected trace extraction, scalar reduction, primitive
                                                                                         ​




            indecomposable projection, de Rham contraction, and an adjoined local-dual current
            sector. These operations are not conservative and do not preserve or reflect full derived
            centers.
            An elementary counterexample is the quotient
                                                                      C⟨x, y⟩ → C[x, y].

            The image of x is central in the quotient but x is not central in the free algebra. Hence
            centrality in a reduced quotient does not imply centrality before quotient.
            In the present model, the reduced current theorem proves strict identities only in
                                                             pp
                                                         A∂ (I) = S (hI )⊗S (PI [1]).
                                                                  ​              ​           ​   ​




            The unreduced theorem requires chain-level centrality homotopies against arbitrary
            observables in
                                                                         Obsopen (I).​




            The primitive projection records only the indecomposable shadow and does not control
            products. The principal-part current Θρ is an added reduced local-dual generator and is
            not yet derived from an unreduced boundary BV current complex. The polynomial
                                                                           ​




            one-ψ classes cannot supply it because their Hamiltonian action does not match the
            Matlis coadjoint action. Finally, the scalar U (1) class is killed by the reduced quotient
            but must be cancelled or absorbed in an unreduced theory.
            Therefore the reduced Hamiltonian current model does not prove the full unreduced P0-
            factorization-center theorem. □
                                                                                                              ​

            theorem

            Define the candidate unreduced principal-part extension

            24. Final conclusion
            The full theorem
                                                          ∙         P0
                                                         CCE (g) ⟶ Zfact
                                                                  ​      (Obsopen )                                  ​




                                                                                                                         ​                                ​




            requires a map into the derived P0-factorization center of the unreduced open BV
            algebra.
                                                                      ​




            The reduced Hamiltonian current model proves only the post-contraction statement
                                                              ∙         P0
                                                             CCE (g) ⟶ Zfact (App
                                                                              ​




                                                                               ∂ ),
                                                                                                                             ​




                                                                                                                                 ​                    ​




            where
https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa84c-a760-83ea-ae70-183a06ecd2d3                                                                                                                    433/510
30/04/2026, 00:40                                                          Whitepaper Critical Analysis

                                                         App
                                                          ∂ (I) = S (hI )⊗S (PI [1]).
                                                             ​                 ​   ​         ​




            The passage
                                                                  Obsopen ⇝ App
                                                                           ​




                                                                             ∂           ​




            kills or contracts precisely the data needed for the full theorem: products, higher open
            observables, scalar center, de Rham chains before contraction, finite-N identities,
            polynomial/principal-part mismatch, and chain-level centrality homotopies.
            Therefore the reduced current model is a shadow, not a proof, of the full unreduced
            factorization-center theorem.
            The reduced theorem is still the right algebraic core. But the full theorem exists only
            after constructing boundary principal-part representatives, product and bracket
            centrality homotopies, factorization coherence, scalar anomaly cancellation, and the
            relevant BV/QME data.

            Let

                                                  Qx = Qy = 0,                                 Qψ = xy − yx.

            2. Cyclic words and signs
            Let
                                                                          T (V ) = ⨁ V ⊗n                           ​




                                                                                                              n≥0

            be the tensor algebra.
            For homogeneous a1, … , an ∈ V , write
                                              ​                   ​




                                                                            [a1 a2 ⋯ an ]
                                                                                  ​               ​                     ​




            for the corresponding cyclic word.
            The graded cyclic relation is
                                     [a1 a2 ⋯ an ] = (−1)∣an ∣(∣a1 ∣+⋯+∣an−1 ∣) [an a1 ⋯ an−1 ].
                                          ​           ​       ​
                                                                                      ​                   ​                     ​




                                                                                                                                                ​   ​




            Thus
                     Cyc(T (V )) = T (V )/⟨a1 ⋯ an − (−1)∣an ∣(∣a1 ∣+⋯+∣an−1 ∣) an a1 ⋯ an−1 ⟩.
                                                                      ​       ​
                                                                                                                            ​       ​                   ​




                                                                                                                                                                ​   ​               ​




            The nonempty cyclic words are
https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa84c-a760-83ea-ae70-183a06ecd2d3                                                                                                                              437/510
30/04/2026, 00:40                                                                      Whitepaper Critical Analysis

                                                                      Cyc(T (V ))≥1 .                          ​




            The differential Q is the derivation of T (V ) determined by
                                                        Qx = Qy = 0,                               Qψ = xy − yx.

            Since Q is a derivation and the relation is cyclic, Q descends to cyclic words.
            Thus
                                                                           (popen , Q)     ​




            is a cochain complex.

            3. Cyclic derivatives
            For a cyclic word
                                                                     W = [a1 a2 ⋯ an ] ​           ​                   ​




            and a generator r ∈ {x, y, ψ}, define the cyclic derivative
                                                                       ∂r W ∈ T (V )
                                                                           ​




            by cutting the cyclic word immediately after every occurrence of r.
            Explicitly,
                                        ∂r [a1 ⋯ an ] = ∑ (−1)ϵi ai+1 ⋯ an a1 ⋯ ai−1 ,
                                           ​    ​            ​                 ​
                                                                                                       ​




                                                                                                                   ​       ​   ​       ​




                                                                     1≤i≤n
                                                                      ai =r
                                                                       ​
                                                                               ​




            where the Koszul sign ϵi is the sign required to cyclically rotate ai past ai+1, … , an
            and then remove it. Since x, y have degree 0, the only nontrivial signs occur when ψ
                                                    ​                                                                              ​       ​   ​




            crosses other odd symbols.
            For the Hamiltonian bracket below, only ∂x and ∂y enter. Since x, y are even, their
            removal introduces no sign except the cyclic rotation sign from moving the remaining
                                                                                   ​                       ​




            word.
            Examples:
                                                                                   a−1
                                                           ∂x [x y ] = ∑ xa−1−i y b xi ,
                                                                 ​
                                                                     a b
                                                                                               ​




                                                                                   i=0

                                                                                         j=0
