            4. The necklace bracket
            Define the necklace bracket on cyclic words by
                                            {F , G}neck = [∂x F ⋅ ∂y G − ∂y F ⋅ ∂x G]cyc .
                                                                 ​                   ​                         ​                   ​               ​   ​




            Here juxtaposition denotes concatenation in T (V ), and [⋅]cyc means passage back to
            cyclic words.
                                                                                                                                       ​




            Because the pairing
                                                        ⟨x, y⟩ = 1,                                ⟨y, x⟩ = −1

            is constant symplectic, the standard necklace-bracket calculation gives:
                                                  {F , G}neck = −(−1)∣F ∣∣G∣ {G, F }neck ,
                                                                     ​                                                                         ​




            and
            {F , {G, H}neck }neck + (−1)∣F ∣(∣G∣+∣H∣) {G, {H, F }neck }neck + (−1)∣H∣(∣F ∣+∣G∣) {H, {F , G}
                                      ​       ​                                                                                ​           ​




            Thus
                                                                 popen = Cyc(T (V ))≥1
                                                                                 ​                                         ​




            is a graded Lie algebra.

            5. Compatibility of Q with the necklace bracket
            We must check that
                                   Q{F , G}neck = {QF , G}neck + (−1)∣F ∣ {F , QG}neck .
                                                         ​                                         ​                                                       ​




https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa84c-a760-83ea-ae70-183a06ecd2d3                                                                                                     439/510
30/04/2026, 00:40                                                                                  Whitepaper Critical Analysis

            Since Qx = Qy = 0, Q commutes with ∂x and ∂y up to the usual Koszul signs.
            Therefore
                                                                                                   ​                           ​




                                                                     Q(∂x F ) = ∂x (QF ),  ​                           ​




                                                                     Q(∂y F ) = ∂y (QF ).  ​                       ​




            Then
                                              Q{F , G}neck = Q[∂x F ∂y G − ∂y F ∂x G].
                                                                           ​                           ​                                   ​   ​




            Expanding Q as a derivation gives
            [∂x (QF )∂y G + (−1)∣F ∣−0 ∂x F ∂y (QG) − ∂y (QF )∂x G − (−1)∣F ∣−0 ∂y F ∂x (QG)].
                    ​                   ​                        ​     ​                                       ​                       ​                       ​   ​




            This is exactly
                                                 {QF , G}neck + (−1)∣F ∣ {F , QG}neck .
                                                                                   ​                                                               ​




            Thus (popen, Q, {−, −}neck ) is a dg Lie algebra.
                                    ​                   ​




            This is the primitive unreduced open BV Lie algebra.

            6. The open factorization algebra
            For every interval I ⊂ R, define
                                                                     pI = Ω∙c (I)⊗popen .
                                                                               ​               ​                           ​       ​




            The differential is
                                                                           dI = ddR + Q.
                                                                                       ​                   ​




            The bracket is
                                              [α ⊗ F , β ⊗ G] = (α ∧ β) ⊗ {F , G}neck .                                                                ​




            Since ddR is a derivation of the wedge product and Q is a derivation of the necklace
            bracket,
                                ​




                    dI [α ⊗ F , β ⊗ G] = [dI (α ⊗ F ), β ⊗ G] + (−1)∣α∣+∣F ∣ [α ⊗ F , dI (β ⊗ G)].
                        ​                                    ​                                                                                             ​




            Thus pI is a dg Lie algebra.
                            ​




https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa84c-a760-83ea-ae70-183a06ecd2d3                                                                                                             440/510
30/04/2026, 00:40                                                                                            Whitepaper Critical Analysis

            If j : I ↪ J is an inclusion of intervals, extension by zero gives
                                                                           j! : Ω∙c (I) → Ω∙c (J),
                                                                                ​            ​                                       ​




            hence
                                                                                        j! : pI → pJ .
                                                                                         ​               ​                       ​




            For disjoint intervals I1, … , In ⊂ J , the direct sum map
                                               ​                       ​




                                                                                         n
                                                                                        ⨁ pIr → pJ
                                                                                                 ​

                                                                                                             ​
                                                                                                                 ​                           ​




                                                                                        r=1

            is a dg Lie map because supports are disjoint, so cross-brackets vanish.
            Define
                                                                           Obsopen (I) = S (pI ).    ​                                           ​       ​




            The factorization product for disjoint intervals is induced by multiplication after
            extension by zero:
                                           μI1 ,…,In ;J (F1 ⊗ ⋯ ⊗ Fn ) = j1,! F1 ⋯ jn,! Fn .
                                                       ​       ​
                                                                   ​        ​                                        ​                               ​       ​   ​   ​




            The P0-bracket on Obsopen(I) is the biderivation extending the dg Lie bracket on pI .
                       ​                                   ​                                                                                                             ​




            Thus
                                                                                I ⟼ Obsopen (I)                              ​




            is a locally constant P0-factorization algebra on R.
                                                   ​




            This construction is the variance-correct version: the source is the compactly supported
            cosheaf Ω∙c (I), and observables are covariant by extension by zero, exactly as the
            latest manuscript’s local dictionary requires for brane-line currents. main
                              ​




            7. The shifted cotangent closed algebra of the full open
            theory
            For each interval I , define the continuous dual
                                                                           p∨I = Homcont (pI , C).
                                                                                    ​                                    ​               ​




https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa84c-a760-83ea-ae70-183a06ecd2d3                                                                                                                   441/510
30/04/2026, 00:40                                                                                       Whitepaper Critical Analysis

            The coadjoint action is
                                  (X ⋅ λ)(Y ) = −λ([X, Y ]),                                                                  X, Y ∈ pI ,         ​   λ ∈ p∨I .
                                                                                                                                                              ​




            Define
                                                                  gI = pI ⋉ p∨I [1].
                                                                    ​                               ​                     ​               ​




            The bracket is
                                                                 [X, Y ]g = [X, Y ]p ,
                                                                                    ​                                                 ​




                                                                   [X, ηλ ] = ηX⋅λ ,        ​                                     ​




                                                                    [ηλ , ημ ] = 0.
                                                                                ​                        ​




            The differential is
                                                                    dg X = dp X ,
                                                                        ​                                     ​




                                                                    dg ηλ = ηd∨p λ ,
                                                                            ​           ​


                                                                                                                      ​
                                                                                                                              ​




            where
                                                       (d∨p λ)(X) = −(−1)∣λ∣ λ(dp X )
                                                             ​                                                                                ​




            with the standard dual differential convention.
            The Jacobi identity follows from the dg coadjoint representation identity.
            This is the full closed Lie algebra naturally associated to the unreduced stable open BV
            factorization algebra.

            9. Factorization compatibility
            For inclusions j : I ↪ J , the map
                                                                                                 j! : pI → pJ
                                                                                                     ​               ​                   ​




            induces
                                                      j! : Obsopen (I) → Obsopen (J).
                                                         ​                                               ​                                           ​




            It also induces a map of shifted cotangent Lie algebras
                                                                                                         gI → gJ
                                                                                                             ​                   ​




            after applying extension by zero to pI and pushforward to the compactly supported
            dual.
                                                                                                 ​




            The CE/PV map is natural in dg Lie maps. Therefore the diagram
                                                    ∙
                                                   CCE (gI ) ⟶ PV(Obsopen (I))
                                                                 ​                   ​                                                                       ​




                                                      ↓             ↓                                            ​                                                           ​




                                                    ∙
                                                   CCE (gJ ) ⟶ PV(Obsopen (J))
                                                             ​                           ​                                                               ​




            commutes.
            For disjoint intervals I1, … , In ⊂ J , the factorization product is multiplication after
            extension by zero. The CE/PV map is multiplicative, so it is compatible with the disjoint-
                                               ​                     ​




            interval products.
            Thus the intervalwise isomorphisms assemble into a morphism of locally constant P0-
            factorization algebras:
                                                                                                                                                                                 ​




                                                       ∙             P0
                                                      CCE (g(−) ) ⟶ Zfact (Obsopen ).
                                                                             ​                   ​
                                                                                                                             ​




                                                                                                                                     ​                           ​       ​




            This proves the full factorization theorem in the stable primitive cyclic open BV model.
https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa84c-a760-83ea-ae70-183a06ecd2d3                                                                                                                           445/510
30/04/2026, 00:40                                                                 Whitepaper Critical Analysis




            10. The Hamiltonian sector as a quotient of the full theorem
            Now recover the Hamiltonian BF theorem.
            Let
                                                                hpoly = C[z1 , z2 ]
                                                                          ​                   ​       ​




            or
                                                              Aˉ = C[z1 , z2 ]/C ⋅ 1
                                                                                  ​       ​




            depending on whether the scalar line is retained.
            Define the normal-ordering projection
                                                               N : popen → hpoly      ​                   ​




            on cyclic words by
                                                                                      #x(w) #y(w)
                                                             N ([w]) = z1                  z2     ​           ​




            if w contains no ψ, and
                                                                      N ([w]) = 0

            if w contains at least one ψ.
            This is a chain map because if w = ψv, then
                                                             Q[ψv] = [(xy − yx)v],

            and both terms have the same commutative monomial image, hence cancel under N .
            It is also a Lie map to the commutative Hamiltonian algebra:
                                                N {F , G}neck = {N (F ), N (G)}Poisson .
                                                                  ​                                                       ​




            For no-ψ words this follows from the cyclic derivative count:
            if
                                                   N (F ) = z1a z2b , ​       ​           N (G) = z1c z2d ,       ​   ​




            then
https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa84c-a760-83ea-ae70-183a06ecd2d3                                                                    446/510
30/04/2026, 00:40                                                                                  Whitepaper Critical Analysis

                              N {F , G}neck = (ad − bc)z1a+c−1 z2b+d−1 = {N (F ), N (G)}.
                                                     ​                                                       ​                               ​




            If either word contains ψ, both sides vanish after projection to the Hamiltonian quotient.
            Thus
                                                                      N : popen → hpoly             ​                            ​       ​




            is a dg Lie morphism.
            Composing with the quotient
                                                               hpoly → h = hpoly /C ⋅ 1
                                                                       ​                                             ​




            gives
                                                                      Nred : popen → h.
                                                                                   ​                     ​                           ​




            This is the precise map from the full primitive open BV Lie algebra to the reduced
            Hamiltonian Lie algebra.

            11. The normal-ordering homotopy
            The projection N has a section
                                                                      i : hpoly → popen        ​                             ​




            defined by
                                                                      i(z1a z2b ) = [xa y b ].
                                                                               ​           ​




            This is not a strict Lie map. Its failure is Q-exact.
            Let w be a word in x, y with a copies of x and b copies of y. Choose the deterministic
            bubble-sort path
                                                             w = w 0 , w 1 , … , w r = xa y b ,
                                                                           ​           ​                         ​




            where each step replaces a subword yx by xy. At step s,
                                                 ws = As yxBs ,
                                                         ​        ​            ​
                                                                                                        ws+1 = As xyBs . ​                       ​   ​




            Define
https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa84c-a760-83ea-ae70-183a06ecd2d3                                                                                               447/510
30/04/2026, 00:40                                                                         Whitepaper Critical Analysis

                                                             H([w]) = − ∑[ψBs As ].                       ​           ​           ​




                                                                                              s

            Then
                                Q[ψBs As ] = [(xy − yx)Bs As ] = [As xyBs ] − [As yxBs ].
                                            ​    ​                                    ​       ​                           ​                   ​   ​   ​




            Thus
                                                             QH([w]) = [w] − [xa y b ].

            Therefore
                                                                  QH + HQ = id −iN                                                    ​




            on the no-ψ cyclic word sector.
            This homotopy supplies the explicit centrality homotopies when the Hamiltonian
            reduced theorem is lifted back to the unreduced cyclic model.

            12. The Hamiltonian BF map into the full center
            The full theorem gives
                                                 ∙
                                                CCE (pI ⋉ p∨I [1]) ≅ Zfact
                                                       ​      ​
                                                                      P0
                                                                          ​(Obsopen )(I).                     ​




                                                                                                                  ​                       ​




            The Hamiltonian quotient
                                                                    Nred : popen → h
                                                                              ​                       ​




            induces a pullback on functions and a derived map of cotangent CE algebras. At the
            level of generators, the Hamiltonian BF sector maps as follows.
            For
                                                                         f = z1a z2b ∈ h, ​       ​




            define the boundary representative
                                                                  Of = [xa y b ] ∈ popen .
                                                                     ​                                                        ​




            Then in the open observable algebra
                                                                  Obsopen (I) = S (pI ),
                                                                                  ​                                   ​

            the smeared representative is
                                                             Bf (a) = a(t)dt ⊗ [xa y b ].
                                                                ​

                                                               ua(t)dt⊗f ⟼ Bf (a).       ​                  ​        ​




            The Hamiltonian CE coordinate cf maps to the lifted constant-vector operation
                                                                        θf = iθf N
                                                                             ​                         ​




            on the normal-ordering contraction, i.e. the operation that:
             1. projects an unreduced cyclic observable to its Hamiltonian normal form;
             2. applies the constant vector field θf ;
             3. includes the result as an ordered cyclic representative.

            This gives a strict operation on the chain model, and the bubble-sort homotopy H
            supplies the homotopies comparing it with any other cyclic representative.

                            ∙

                                                                                                                                ​




                                                                                                                                    ​   ​




            The first arrow is the normal-ordering L∞ -pullback induced by N , i, H . The second
            arrow is the full cotangent CE/PV theorem.
                                                                                     ​




            The output is a genuine derived-center operation on the unreduced cyclic open BV
            algebra.

            13. Product centrality homotopy, explicitly
            Let F ∈ Obsopen(I). Let f ∈ h. Write
                                       ​




                                                                        Of = i(f ).
                                                                                 ​




            Let
                                                                             p=N

            be the normal-ordering projection.
https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa84c-a760-83ea-ae70-183a06ecd2d3                                                                                  449/510
30/04/2026, 00:40                                                               Whitepaper Critical Analysis

            The reduced multiplication operation is
                                                             Mfred (pF ) = f pF .
                                                                     ​




            The lifted operation is
                                                              Mf (F ) = i(f pF ).

            Compare this with ordinary multiplication by the chosen representative i(f ):
                                                                             i(f )F .

            Because p is multiplicative,
                                                              p(i(f )F ) = f pF .

            Therefore
                                                    i p(i(f )F ) = i(f pF ) = Mf (F ).                  ​




            Using
                                                             id −ip = QH + HQ,

            we get
                       i(f )F − Mf (F ) = (id −ip)(i(f )F ) = QH(i(f )F ) + HQ(i(f )F ).
                                             ​




            Thus the product centrality homotopy is
                                                               prod
                                                             Hf ,F = H(i(f )F ).
                                                                         ​                          ​




            It satisfies
                                                 i(f )F − Mf (F ) = QHfprod
                                                                 ​
                                                                              prod
                                                                        ,F + Hf ,QF .           ​              ​




            Similarly, comparing F i(f ) gives the same homotopy because the observable algebra
            is graded commutative after passing to S (pI ).                      ​




            Thus multiplication by the boundary Hamiltonian representative is central up to the
            explicit bubble-sort homotopy.
            This is the first missing homotopy.

            15. CE differential compatibility in the full open center
            Let
                                                                                            f K = HK        ​                               ​




            be a Hamiltonian basis element. In the reduced Hamiltonian CE/PV theorem,
                                                                        L
                                                              dCE uK = CKJ uL c J .
                                                                            ​                   ​                                   ​                       ​




            Under the lifted map,
                                                                                uK ↦ O K ,          ​                                   ​




                                                                                            cJ ↦ θ J .

            The center differential of OK is the P0-bracket operation
                                                         ​                              ​




                                                             dZ OK = {OK , −}open .
                                                                ​                       ​                                   ​                                           ​




            By the bracket centrality homotopy above,
                                                              {OK , −}open ≃ XK .   ​                               ​                                           ​




            But the reduced Hamiltonian vector field is
                                                                     L
                                                               XK = CKJ OL θ J .        ​                               ​                       ​




            Lifting by i(−)p gives
                                                                     L
                                                               XK = CKJ OL θ J .        ​                               ​                       ​




            Therefore
                                                                       L
                                                              dZ OK ≃ CKJ
                                                                    ​     OL θ J .          ​                                   ​                       ​                   ​




            This is precisely
                                                             Φ(dCE uK ) ≃ dZ Φ(uK ).
                                                                                ​                       ​                           ​                               ​




            Similarly,
                                                                        1 K I J
                                                              dCE cK = − CIJ c c
                                                                        2
                                                                        ​                                           ​                               ​

30/04/2026, 00:40                                                                                  Whitepaper Critical Analysis

            maps to
                                                                               1 K I J
                                                                              − CIJ θ θ .
                                                                               2
                                                                                   ​                 ​




            The differential of θK in the center is the commutator of the lifted constant-vector
            operation with the open Poisson structure. Since p is a Lie map and H supplies the
            homotopy between ip and the identity, the same transfer calculation gives
                                                                           1 K I J
                                                                  dZ θK ≃ − CIJ θ θ .
                                                                           2
                                                                          ​                              ​            ​                ​




            Thus the CE differential is respected up to the explicit derived-center homotopies.
            This is the chain-level form of the full theorem.

            16. Factorization coherence of the homotopies
            For intervals I ⊂ J , extension by zero commutes with:
                                              N,             i,               H,                             Q,                   {−, −}open .  ​




            Indeed:
                N acts only on cyclic words, not on the interval form;
                i acts only on cyclic words;
                H bubble-sorts only the word part;
                Q is ddR + Qcyc , both compatible with extension by zero;
                the bracket is the wedge product in Ω∙c (I) times the necklace bracket.
                                ​             ​




                                                                                               ​




            Thus
                                                                              j ! HI = HJ j !
                                                                               ​       ​                          ​       ​




            for every inclusion j : I ↪ J .
            For disjoint intervals I1, … , In ⊂ J , the product is multiplication after extension by
            zero. Since H is a derivation homotopy on the free symmetric algebra extension, the
                                                  ​           ​




            homotopies satisfy the usual Leibniz formula:
                                                                  n
                                H(F1 ⋯ Fn ) = ∑(−1)∣F1 ∣+⋯+∣Fr−1 ∣ F1 ⋯ H(Fr ) ⋯ Fn .
                                          ​           ​               ​
                                                                                           ​                                  ​




                                                                                                                                   ​        ​       ​




                                                              r=1


https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa84c-a760-83ea-ae70-183a06ecd2d3                                                                                              453/510
30/04/2026, 00:40                                                                                    Whitepaper Critical Analysis

            Therefore the product and bracket centrality homotopies are compatible with disjoint
            factorization products.
            This gives the factorization-level theorem, not merely a stalkwise theorem.

            17. Principal parts inside the full theorem
            The full primitive theorem uses
                                                                                         p∨open .        ​




            The Hamiltonian BF sector uses the quotient
                                                                         Nred : popen → h.
                                                                                 ​                           ​




            Its continuous dual is the injection
                                                                             h∨cont ↪ p∨open .
                                                                                         ​                               ​




            The Matlis theorem identifies
                                                                 h∨cont ≅ P = ⨁ Cρa,b ,
                                                                         ​                                           ​                   ​




                                                                                                      a+b>0

            where
                                                                 ρa,b = z1−a−1 z2−b−1 dz1 dz2 .
                                                                     ​                           ​               ​               ​           ​




            The residue pairing is
                                                                  ⟨ρa,b , z1m z2n ⟩ = δa,m δb,n .
                                                                             ​       ​       ​                               ​       ​




            The coadjoint action is
                                           p q
                                         z1 z2 ⋅ ρa,b = −(pb − qa + p − q)ρa−p+1,b−q+1 .
                                             ​   ​           ​                                                                                   ​




            This is the Hamiltonian quotient of the full primitive dual.
            Thus principal parts are not adjoined artificially in the full theorem. They are the
            concrete realization of the continuous dual of the Hamiltonian quotient of the primitive
            cyclic algebra.
            This is the mathematically clean relation between:
https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa84c-a760-83ea-ae70-183a06ecd2d3                                                                                           454/510
30/04/2026, 00:40                                                                    Whitepaper Critical Analysis

                                                                                 p∨open               ​




            and

            The latest principal-parts note gives exactly the physical reading: P is the residue-

            identified with P as an h-module.

            18. The PBW Ψ-sector in the full theorem
            The primitive one-ψ classes are present in
                                                                                 popen                ​




            itself. They are not thrown away.
            For a, b ≥ 0, define
                                                             Ψa,b = ∑ [ψw(x, y)],
                                                                    ​                             ​




                                                                        w∈Wa,b                ​




            where Wa,b is the set of words with a copies of x and b copies of y.
                              ​




            Then
                                                         QΨa,b = ∑ [(xy − yx)w].
                                                                ​                         ​




                                                                        w∈Wa,b        ​




            Grouping by cyclic words of bidegree (a + 1, b + 1), the xy-transition count equals the
            yx-transition count, so

                                                                        QΨa,b = 0.    ​




            These classes live in popen, hence in the full open algebra. The full center theorem acts
            on them because it acts on all of Obsopen.
                                                     ​




                                                                                 ​




            However, under the Hamiltonian projection
                                                               Nred : popen → h,
                                                                             ​                            ​




            they map to zero:
https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa84c-a760-83ea-ae70-183a06ecd2d3                                                          455/510
30/04/2026, 00:40                                                                                Whitepaper Critical Analysis

                                                                       Nred (Ψa,b ) = 0.
                                                                             ​                        ​




            So they are present in the full open theorem but invisible in the reduced Hamiltonian
            quotient.
            This is the correct placement:
                                                       Ψa,b ∈ popen ,
                                                             ​                   ​                             ρa,b ∈ h∨cont .
                                                                                                                          ​       ​   ​




            The full theorem contains both, but in different positions:
                Ψa,b are open primitive fields;
                ρa,b are cotangent functionals dual to the Hamiltonian quotient.
                            ​




                        ​




            There is no same-action identification.

            19. Scalar line and the unreduced version
            If one wants the strictly unreduced scalar line, replace
                                                                 h = C[[z1 , z2 ]]/C ⋅ 1     ​            ​




            by
                                                                  hun = C[[z1 , z2 ]].

            Then
                                                                        {z1 , z2 } = 1
                                                                                     ​            ​




            is retained.
            On the open side,
                                                       {Tr ϕ1 , Tr ϕ2 } = N = Tr(1).
                                                                   ​                     ​




            The full unreduced scalar theorem uses the central extension
                                                         0 → C ⋅ 1 → hun → h → 0.                             ​




            The scalar cocycle is
                                                                 ω(f , g) = [{f , g}]0 .                                      ​




            It is a coboundary on hun , with primitive
                                                   ​

            It becomes nontrivial after scalar reduction.

            Unreduced scalar version

            This construction supplies all pieces that were previously missing:
             1. The full unreduced open BV algebra is not the reduced current algebra; it is the
                cyclic Koszul factorization algebra

                theorem.

                 quotient, not polynomial descendants.

                 the scalar-reduced quotient with [cˉ] as obstruction class.
            This is the rigorous full theorem architecture. The reduced Hamiltonian current model is
            no longer being asked to prove the full theorem. It is now correctly obtained as a sector
            of the full primitive cyclic theorem.

            The construction below uses the manuscript’s fixed conventions: the brane-line source
            is Ω∙c (I)⊗h, the reduced CE/PV generator map is cI ↦ θI , uI ↦ OI , the Matlis

            I. Algebraic conventions
            Work over C. All tensor products are completed when infinite sums are present; finite
            formulas are written algebraically. All cochain differentials have degree +1.
            Let I ⊂ R be an oriented open interval. Let
                                                                                        Ω∙c (I)
                                                                                             ​




            be compactly supported de Rham forms with differential dI = ddR . For an inclusion j :
            I ↪ J , extension by zero is denoted
                                                                                                                         ​               ​




                                                                         j! : Ω∙c (I) → Ω∙c (J).
                                                                          ​     ​                        ​




            For disjoint intervals I1, … , In ⊂ J , the factorization product is always induced by
            extension by zero and multiplication.
                                                     ​               ​




            For a graded vector space V ,
                                                         Cyc(T (V )) = T (V )/[T (V ), T (V )]gr                                     ​




            is the space of graded cyclic words. If w = a1 ⋯ an , its class is written               ​       ​




                                                                                            [w].

            The graded cyclic relation is
                                     [a1 a2 ⋯ an ] = (−1)∣an ∣(∣a1 ∣+⋯+∣an−1 ∣) [an a1 ⋯ an−1 ].
                                             ​   ​           ​
                                                                                    ​            ​               ​




                                                                                                                     ​       ​




            II. The unreduced stable open BV primitive Lie algebra
            Define the graded vector space

                                                           E = Cx ⊕ Cy ⊕ Ca ⊕ Cψ

            with degrees
                                              ∣x∣ = ∣y∣ = 0,                              ∣a∣ = 1,                               ∣ψ∣ = −1.

            Here x, y are the two matrix normal coordinates, a is the suspended gauge multiplier,
            and ψ is the Koszul/BV variable.
            Define a constant graded symplectic pairing of degree 0 by
                                                          ⟨x, y⟩ = 1,                                    ⟨y, x⟩ = −1,

                                                          ⟨a, ψ⟩ = 1,                                    ⟨ψ, a⟩ = −1,

            and all other pairings zero.
            Let
                                                                          p = Cyc(T (E))≥1                               ​




            be the nonempty cyclic words.
            For r ∈ {x, y, a, ψ}, define the cyclic derivative

            as follows. For a homogeneous cyclic word

            where ϵi is the Koszul sign obtained by cyclically rotating bi past bi+1, … , bn and then

            Define the necklace bracket on p by

            Equivalently,
https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa84c-a760-83ea-ae70-183a06ecd2d3                                                                                                                 462/510
30/04/2026, 00:40                                                                           Whitepaper Critical Analysis

                                                   {F , G}neck = ∑⟨r, s⟩ [∂r F ∂s G]cyc ,
                                                                 ​                      ​                     ​   ​        ​




                                                                                  r,s

            with the Koszul sign convention dictated by the above pairing.
            This bracket has degree 0, is graded antisymmetric, and satisfies the graded Jacobi
            identity. The proof is the standard constant-symplectic necklace calculation: the cyclic
            derivatives are noncommutative Hamiltonian derivatives, the pairing matrix is constant,
            and the Jacobiator is a cyclic sum of graded commutators in T (E), hence vanishes in
            Cyc(T (E)).

            Define the cubic cyclic Hamiltonian
                                                             S = [a(xy − yx)] ∈ p1 .

            Set
                                                                Q = {S, −}neck .                          ​




            Then
                                                                     1
                                                         Q2 =          {{S, S}neck , −}neck .
                                                                     2
                                                                      ​                               ​               ​




            Compute {S, S}neck . The nonzero cyclic derivatives are
                                               ​




                                                                ∂x S = ya − ay,
                                                                              ​




                                                                ∂y S = ax − xa,
                                                                          ​




                                                                ∂a S = xy − yx,
                                                                          ​




                                                                                  ∂ψ S = 0.
                                                                                    ​




            Therefore the a, ψ-part of {S, S} vanishes, and the x, y-part is
                           {S, S}x,y = 2 [(ya − ay)(ax − xa) − (ax − xa)(ya − ay)]cyc .
                                           ​                                                                                   ​




            This is twice the cyclic class of a graded commutator. Hence
                                                                {S, S}neck = 0.                  ​




            Thus
                                                                                  Q2 = 0.

            The induced differential on generators is

            III. The brane-line unreduced open factorization algebra
            For an interval I ⊂ R, define
                                                                               pI = Ω∙c (I)⊗p.
                                                                                 ​                            ​               ​




            The differential is
                                                                dpI = ddR ⊗ 1 + 1 ⊗ Q.

                                                                       ​                         ​




            The bracket is
                                      [α ⊗ F , β ⊗ G] = (−1)∣F ∣∣β∣ (α ∧ β) ⊗ {F , G}neck .                                                           ​




            The sign is the standard tensor-product sign: F crosses β .
            Because ddR is a derivation of ∧, and Q is a derivation of the necklace bracket,
                                ​




                                           dpI [X, Y ] = [dpI X , Y ] + (−1)∣X∣ [X, dpI Y ].

                                                    ​

                                                                                     ​
                                                                                         ​

                                                                                                                                              ​
                                                                                                                                                  ​

            VI. The Hamiltonian sector inside the full theorem
            Let
                                                                  pH = Cyc(C⟨x, y⟩)≥1 ⊂ p
                                                                                  ​                                                                                                ​




            be the cyclic words containing only x, y.
            Because
                                                             Qx = [x, a],                                                                         Qy = [y, a],

            for any word w = w(x, y),
                                                                                  Q[w] = [w, a]cyc = 0.                                                                ​




            Thus pH is a subcomplex with zero differential.
                             ​




            It is closed under the x, y-necklace bracket. Hence pH is a dg Lie subalgebra.                                                                                             ​

30/04/2026, 00:40                                                                                           Whitepaper Critical Analysis

            Define the commutative Hamiltonian algebra
                                                                        hpoly = C[z1 , z2 ]
                                                                                    ​                                            ​               ​




            with Poisson bracket
                                                         {f , g} = ∂z1 f ∂z2 g − ∂z2 f ∂z1 g .
                                                                                        ​
                                                                                            ​

                                                                                                                     ​
                                                                                                                         ​

                                                                                                                                                             ​
                                                                                                                                                                 ​

                                                                                                                                                                                     ​
                                                                                                                                                                                         ​




            Define the normal-ordering projection
                                                                        N : pH → hpoly              ​                                                ​




            by
                                                                                                                #x(w) #y(w)
                                                             N ([w]) = z1                                            z2     .        ​                                           ​




            For monomial cyclic words,
                                                                    N ([xa y b ]) = z1a z2b .                                            ​               ​




            The projection N is a Lie map. Indeed, for F = [xayb ] and G = [xc yd],
                                                                                    a−1
                                                             ∂x F = ∑ xa−1−i y b xi ,
                                                                    ​                                       ​




                                                                                    i=0

                                                                                    b−1
                                                             ∂y F = ∑ y b−1−j xa y j .
                                                                ​                                       ​




                                                                                    j=0

            After applying N ,
                                                               N (∂x F ) = az1a−1 z2b ,
                                                                            ​                                                                ​                           ​




                                                               N (∂y F ) = bz1a z2b−1 .
                                                                                ​                                                    ​                               ​




            Therefore
                              N {F , G}neck = (ad − bc)z1a+c−1 z2b+d−1 = {N (F ), N (G)}.
                                                     ​                                                                       ​                                               ​




            By bilinearity, this holds for all F , G ∈ pH .                                     ​




            Now define the reduced Hamiltonian quotient
                                                                        h = hpoly /C ⋅ 1.                        ​




            Let

                                                                             Nred : pH → h

            be the composite.
            VII. The bubble-sort contraction
            Define a section
                                                                             i : hpoly → pH            ​                               ​




            by
                                                                         i(z1a z2b ) = [xa y b ].
                                                                                  ​       ​




            Then
                                                                                  N i = id.

            For any word w = w(x, y), let
                                                            a = #x(w),                                         b = #y(w).

            Choose the deterministic bubble-sort path
                                                            w = w 0 , w 1 , … , w r = xa y b
                                                                              ​               ​                            ​




            where each step replaces a subword yx by xy.
            At step s, write
                                                 ws = As y xBs ,
                                                        ​            ​            ​                        ws+1 = As xyBs .    ​                   ​       ​




            Define
                                                                                                       r−1
                                                                H([w]) = − ∑[ψBs As ].                         ​                           ​   ​




                                                                                                       s=0

            Then
                                        Q[ψBs As ] = [(xy − yx)Bs As ] + [ψQ(Bs As )].
                                                    ​       ​                                                      ​               ​                           ​   ​




            The second term is a cyclic commutator with a, hence vanishes in cyclic words.
            Therefore
                                                 Q[ψBs As ] = [As xyBs ] − [As y xBs ].
                                                                 ​       ​                        ​                    ​                       ​       ​




            Summing over s,
https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa84c-a760-83ea-ae70-183a06ecd2d3                                                                                                             471/510
30/04/2026, 00:40                                                                   Whitepaper Critical Analysis

                                                             QH([w]) = [w] − [xa y b ].

            Thus
                                                                     QH = id − iN                          ​




            on pH .  ​




            Since Q = 0 on pH , this is the contraction identity
                                           ​




                                                             QH + HQ = id − iN .                                   ​




            This supplies explicit chain homotopies from arbitrary noncommutative cyclic words to
            their ordered commutative representatives.
            VIII. Hamiltonian central operations on the full open algebra
            Let
                                                                              f ∈ hpoly        ​




            and define the ordered cyclic representative
                                                                     f = i(f ) ∈ pH .
                                                                         ​                             ​




            For
                                                                             F ∈ S (pI ),  ​




            define the Hamiltonian projection
                                                                    Π(F ) = iN (F )

            on primitive generators and extend multiplicatively to the symmetric algebra.
            Define the lifted multiplication operation
                                                              Mf (F ) = i(f N (F )).
                                                                     ​




            Define the lifted Hamiltonian vector-field operation
                                                             Xf (F ) = i({f , N (F )}).
                                                                ​




            The product centrality homotopy is
                                                                         prod
                                                                    Hf ,F = H(f F ).
                                                                                ​                  ​           ​

            Indeed,
                              f F − Mf (F ) = (id − iN )(f F ) = QH(f F ) + HQ(f F ).
                                ​               ​                                                                     ​                                                                        ​                                       ​




            Thus
                                                      f F − Mf (F ) = QHfprod
                                                              ​
                                                                                prod
                                                                          ,F + Hf ,QF .
                                                                                      ​                                                                           ​                                    ​   ​




            The P0-bracket centrality homotopy is
                          ​




                                                                    Hfbr,F = H({f , F }P0 ).
                                                                                          ​                                                   ​

                                                                                                                                                                          ​
                                                                                                                                                                              ​        ​




            Since N is a Lie map,
                                                                  N {f , F }P0 = {f , N (F )}.
                                                                                  ​


                                                                                                          ​
                                                                                                              ​




            Therefore
                                                                   iN {f , F }P0 = Xf (F ).   ​

                                                                                                                          ​
                                                                                                                              ​                               ​




            Hence
                    {f , F }P0 − Xf (F ) = (id − iN ){f , F }P0 = QH{f , F }P0 + HQ{f , F }P0 .
                      ​


                                ​
                                    ​      ​                                                              ​


                                                                                                                                                  ​
                                                                                                                                                      ​                                            ​

                                                                                                                                                                                                                           ​               ​


                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               ​
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   ​

                                                                    ​

            Hamiltonian sector inside the full unreduced cyclic model.

            1. The object to construct
