            The full open factorization algebra on an interval I ⊂ R is
                                                  Obsopen (I) = S (pI ),
                                                             ​                     ​                             pI = Ω∙c (I)⊗p.
                                                                                                                     ​                           ​       ​   ​




            The full closed source attached to this open algebra is its shifted cotangent dg Lie
            algebra
                                                          GI = T ∗ [1]pI = pI ⋉ p∨I [1].
                                                                  ​                        ​                 ​                       ​               ​




            The full theorem is
                                      ∙                             P0
                                     CCE (GI ) ≅ PV(Obsopen (I)) ≃ Zfact
                                              ​      ​                   (Obsopen )(I).​
                                                                                                                                 ​




                                                                                                                                         ​                       ​




            Then the reduced Hamiltonian BF theorem is obtained from this full theorem by normal
            ordering
                                                         N : p ⟶ h = C[[z1 , z2 ]]/C ⋅ 1                 ​               ​




            and Matlis residue duality
                                                                 h∨cont ≅ P = ⨁ Cρa,b .
                                                                       ​                                         ​                           ​




                                                                                               a+b>0




            2. Cyclic words
            Let T (E) be the tensor algebra on E :
                                                                      T (E) = ⨁ E ⊗n .               ​




                                                                                               n≥0

            For a word
                                                         w = a1 a2 ⋯ an ,  ​   ​               ​                     ai ∈ E, ​




            write its cyclic class as
                                                                                   [w].

            The cyclic quotient is

https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa84c-a760-83ea-ae70-183a06ecd2d3                                                                                                           482/510
30/04/2026, 00:40                                                                                       Whitepaper Critical Analysis

                                                   Cyc(T (E)) = T (E)/⟨uv − (−1)∣u∣∣v∣ vu⟩.

            Equivalently,
                                            [a1 a2 ⋯ an ] = (−1)∣an ∣(∣a1 ∣+⋯+∣an−1 ∣) [an a1 ⋯ an−1 ].
                                               ​   ​       ​
                                                                                    ​                   ​                               ​




                                                                                                                                                        ​   ​




            The nonempty cyclic words are
                                                                             p = Cyc(T (E))≥1 .                                     ​




            This is the stable connected trace space: a cyclic word [w] corresponds to the stable
            trace class
                                                                         Tr(w(ϕ1 , ϕ2 , γ, ψ)).
                                                                                        ​                           ​




            3. Cyclic derivatives
            For r ∈ {x, y, γ, ψ}, define the cyclic derivative
                                                               ∂r : Cyc(T (E)) → T (E)
                                                                     ​




            by cutting every occurrence of r.
            For a homogeneous cyclic word
                                                                             W = [a1 a2 ⋯ an ],     ​           ​               ​




            set
                                                   ∂r W = ∑ (−1)ϵi ai+1 ⋯ an a1 ⋯ ai−1 .
                                                       ​                       ​
                                                                                                ​




                                                                                                                        ​                   ​   ​               ​




                                                                1≤i≤n
                                                                 ai =r   ​
                                                                               ​




            The sign (−1)ϵ is the Koszul sign acquired by rotating ai past
                                    i   ​




                                                                                                                                                    ​




                                                                                   ai+1 ⋯ an​                               ​




            and then deleting it.
            For the even variables x, y, no sign occurs from removing x or y. Thus, for example,
                                                                                        a−1
                                                               ∂x [x y ] = ∑ xa−1−i y b xi ,
                                                                 ​
                                                                             a b
                                                                                                            ​




                                                                                        i=0


https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa84c-a760-83ea-ae70-183a06ecd2d3                                                                                                          483/510
30/04/2026, 00:40                                                                    Whitepaper Critical Analysis
                                                                                    b−1
                                                            ∂y [xa y b ] = ∑ y b−1−j xa y j .
                                                              ​                           ​




                                                                                    j=0

            After normal ordering, these become
                                                             N (∂x [xa y b ]) = az1a−1 z2b ,
                                                                        ​                                ​           ​




                                                             N (∂y [xa y b ]) = bz1a z2b−1 .
                                                                            ​                        ​           ​




            4. The necklace bracket
            Put the following constant even symplectic pairing on E :
                                                        ⟨x, y⟩ = 1,                           ⟨y, x⟩ = −1,

                                                            ⟨γ, ψ⟩ = 1,                       ⟨ψ, γ⟩ = 1,

            and all other pairings zero.
            The equality
                                                                   ⟨ψ, γ⟩ = ⟨γ, ψ⟩

            is correct because γ and ψ are both odd, so the even graded symplectic pairing is
            symmetric on that pair.
            Define the necklace bracket by
                    {F , G}neck = [∂x F ∂y G − ∂y F ∂x G + ∂γ F ∂ψ G + (−1)∣F ∣ ∂ψ F ∂γ G]
                                     ​          ​       ​           ​           ​                ​           ​           ​   ​         ​   .   ​




                                                                                                                                 cyc


            The last sign is the Koszul sign for the odd pair (γ, ψ). With this convention, the basic
            brackets are
                                                       {x, y} = 1,                            {y, x} = −1,

                                                        {γ, ψ} = 1,                           {ψ, γ} = 1.

            The bracket has degree 0. It is graded antisymmetric:
                                                       {F , G} = −(−1)∣F ∣∣G∣ {G, F }.

            It satisfies the graded Jacobi identity:
https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa84c-a760-83ea-ae70-183a06ecd2d3                                                                                         484/510
30/04/2026, 00:40                                                     Whitepaper Critical Analysis

                                 {F , {G, H}} = {{F , G}, H} + (−1)∣F ∣∣G∣ {G, {F , H}}.

            The computation is the standard constant-symplectic necklace calculation: every term
            of the Jacobiator is obtained by making two cyclic cuts, and the three possible orders of
            the two cuts cancel in pairs after cyclic rotation.
            Thus
                                                              (p, {−, −}neck )      ​




            is a graded Lie algebra.

            5. The BV Hamiltonian and the differential
            Define the cyclic Hamiltonian
                                                         S = [γ(xy − yx)] + [ψγγ].                  ​




            Set
                                                              Q = {S, −}neck .          ​




            The induced differential on generators is
                                                                 Qγ = γ 2 ,     ​




                                                              Qx = γx − xγ,                     ​




                                                              Qy = γy − yγ,                 ​




                                                         Qψ = xy − yx + γψ + ψγ.                        ​




            These are the BRST–Koszul equations for the derived quotient of the commuting-pair
            constraint by the gauge action.
            The terms have the following meaning:
                                                       Qx = [γ, x],        Qy = [γ, y],

            so γ generates infinitesimal conjugation;
                                                                 Qγ = γ 2

https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa84c-a760-83ea-ae70-183a06ecd2d3                                                  485/510
30/04/2026, 00:40                                                    Whitepaper Critical Analysis

            is the ghost Maurer–Cartan equation, with this sign convention;
                                                         Qψ = xy − yx + γψ + ψγ

            says that ψ imposes the moment map equation and transforms in the adjoint
            representation.

            6. Direct verification that Q2 = 0
            The differential Q is a degree +1 derivation. Therefore
                                                     Q(ab) = Q(a)b + (−1)∣a∣ aQ(b).

            6.1 The ghost
                                                                 Qγ = γ 2 .

            Then
                                      Q2 γ = Q(γ 2 ) = Qγ γ − γ Qγ = γ 2 γ − γγ 2 = 0.

            6.2 The variable x
                                                               Qx = γx − xγ.

            Then
                                                             Q2 x = Q(γx − xγ).

            Compute the first term:
                    Q(γx) = Qγ x − γQx = γ 2 x − γ(γx − xγ) = γ 2 x − γ 2 x + γxγ = γxγ.

            Compute the second term:
                    Q(xγ) = Qx γ + xQγ = (γx − xγ)γ + xγ 2 = γxγ − xγ 2 + xγ 2 = γxγ.

            Therefore
                                           Q2 x = Q(γx) − Q(xγ) = γxγ − γxγ = 0.

            6.3 The variable y
            The calculation is identical:
https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa84c-a760-83ea-ae70-183a06ecd2d3                                          486/510
30/04/2026, 00:40                                                     Whitepaper Critical Analysis

                                                                Qy = γy − yγ.

            Then
                                                                Q(γy) = γyγ,

                                                                Q(yγ) = γyγ,

            hence
                                                                  Q2 y = 0.

            6.4 The moment-map term
            Set
                                                                μ = xy − yx.

            Then
                                                              Qμ = Q(xy − yx).

            Since x, y are even,
                                                             Q(xy) = Qx y + xQy,

                                                             Q(yx) = Qy x + yQx.

            Thus
                           Qμ = (γx − xγ)y + x(γy − yγ) − (γy − yγ)x − y(γx − xγ).

            Expanding,
                            Qμ = γxy − xγy + xγy − xyγ − γyx + yγx − yγx + yxγ.

            Cancel the middle pairs:
                                                               −xγy + xγy = 0,

                                                               yγx − yγx = 0.

            Therefore
                           Qμ = γxy − xyγ − γyx + yxγ = γ(xy − yx) − (xy − yx)γ.

            So
https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa84c-a760-83ea-ae70-183a06ecd2d3                                           487/510
30/04/2026, 00:40                                                     Whitepaper Critical Analysis

                                                                Qμ = γμ − μγ.         ​




            6.5 The variable ψ
            Recall
                                                              Qψ = μ + γψ + ψγ.

            Then
                                                     Q2 ψ = Qμ + Q(γψ) + Q(ψγ).

            Compute
                                    Q(γψ) = Qγ ψ − γQψ = γ 2 ψ − γ(μ + γψ + ψγ).

            Thus
                                     Q(γψ) = γ 2 ψ − γμ − γ 2 ψ − γψγ = −γμ − γψγ.

            Next, since ∣ψ∣ = −1 is odd,
                                                             Q(ψγ) = Qψ γ − ψQγ.

            Thus
                                                   Q(ψγ) = (μ + γψ + ψγ)γ − ψγ 2

                                             = μγ + γψγ + ψγ 2 − ψγ 2 = μγ + γψγ.

            Therefore
                             Q(γψ) + Q(ψγ) = −γμ − γψγ + μγ + γψγ = −γμ + μγ.

            Using
                                                                Qμ = γμ − μγ,

            we get
                                               Q2 ψ = (γμ − μγ) + (−γμ + μγ) = 0.

            Thus
                                                                   Q2 = 0.     ​




https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa84c-a760-83ea-ae70-183a06ecd2d3                                           488/510
            7. Q is a derivation of the necklace bracket
30/04/2026, 00:40                                                                     Whitepaper Critical Analysis




            Because
                                                                   Q = {S, −}neck ,                 ​




            and the necklace bracket satisfies Jacobi,
                                                                Q{F , G} = {S, {F , G}}

                                              = {{S, F }, G} + (−1)∣S∣∣F ∣ {F , {S, G}}.

            Since ∣S∣ = 1,
                                            Q{F , G} = {QF , G} + (−1)∣F ∣ {F , QG}.

            Therefore
                                                                   (p, Q, {−, −}neck )                  ​




            is a dg Lie algebra.

            8. Open observables on an interval
            For an interval I ⊂ R, define
                                                                    pI = Ω∙c (I)⊗p.
                                                                      ​                ​        ​




            For homogeneous elements
                                                                  α ⊗ F,                   β ⊗ G,

            define the differential
                                           dpI (α ⊗ F ) = ddR α ⊗ F + (−1)∣α∣ α ⊗ QF .
                                                ​
                                                    ​                             ​




            Define the bracket
                                      [α ⊗ F , β ⊗ G] = (−1)∣F ∣∣β∣ (α ∧ β) ⊗ {F , G}neck .                                  ​




            Then dp is a derivation of the bracket:
                        I   ​
                                ​




                                           dpI [X, Y ] = [dpI X , Y ] + (−1)∣X∣ [X, dpI Y ].
                                                        ​
                                                            ​

                                                                          ​
                                                                              ​

                                                                                                                     ​
                                                                                                                         ​




https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa84c-a760-83ea-ae70-183a06ecd2d3                                                                       489/510
30/04/2026, 00:40                                                                                         Whitepaper Critical Analysis

            The proof is the tensor-product Leibniz rule: ddR is a derivation of ∧, and Q is a
            derivation of the necklace bracket.
                                                                                                                                   ​




            Therefore
                                                                                              pI               ​           ​




            is a dg Lie algebra.
            Define
                                                             Obsopen (I) = S (pI ).       ​                                                        ​           ​




            Its differential is the derivation induced by dp . Its P0-bracket is the biderivation
            extending the dg Lie bracket on pI .
                                                                                                           I
                                                                                                                       ​                       ​


                                                                                                                   ​




                                                                  ​




            Thus, on generators,
                                                   {α ⊗ F , β ⊗ G}P0 = [α ⊗ F , β ⊗ G].       ​
                                                                                                      ​




            9. Factorization structure
            If
                                                                                  j:I↪J

            is an inclusion of intervals, extension by zero gives
                                                                 j! : Ω∙c (I) → Ω∙c (J).
                                                                      ​               ​                                                    ​




            Therefore
                                                                              j! : pI → pJ ,
                                                                                  ​               ​                                    ​




                                                      j! : Obsopen (I) → Obsopen (J).
                                                         ​                            ​                                                                    ​




            For disjoint intervals
                                                                          I1 , … , In ⊂ J,
                                                                              ​                                                ​




            define
                              μI1 ,…,In ;J : Obsopen (I1 ) ⊗ ⋯ ⊗ Obsopen (In ) → Obsopen (J)
                                   ​       ​
                                               ​             ​            ​                                                                            ​           ​   ​




https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa84c-a760-83ea-ae70-183a06ecd2d3                                                                                                                 490/510
30/04/2026, 00:40                                                                                            Whitepaper Critical Analysis

            by
                                                   μ(F1 ⊗ ⋯ ⊗ Fn ) = j1,! F1 ⋯ jn,! Fn .
                                                             ​                                           ​                                   ​               ​       ​       ​




            If Ir ∩ Is = ∅, then the supports of the corresponding compactly supported forms are
            disjoint, so
                    ​     ​




                                                                             αr ∧ αs = 0.        ​                   ​




            Hence
                                                                   {jr,! X, js,! Y }P0 = 0.
                                                                             ​                               ​

                                                                                                                                 ​
                                                                                                                                     ​




            Thus the P0-bracket is support-local.
                              ​




            Therefore
                                                                           I ↦ Obsopen (I)                                   ​




            is a locally constant P0-factorization algebra.
                                               ​




            10. Universal cotangent CE/PV theorem
            Let l be a dg Lie algebra with basis {ea}. Write                             ​




                                                                                 dl ea = dba eb ,
                                                                                             ​       ​                   ​                   ​




                                                                                         c
                                                                           [ea , eb ] = Cab
                                                                                     ​      ec .     ​                                   ​               ​




            Let
                                                                  L = T ∗ [1]l = l ⋉ l∨ [1].

            Let ca be the CE coordinate dual to ea. Let ua be the CE coordinate dual to the shifted
            cotangent generator ea[1].
                                                                                 ​                               ​




            Then
                                                                  ∙
                                                                 CCE (L) = S (ua )⊗Λ(ca ).
                                                                       ​                                                 ​                           ​




            The CE differential is
                                                                          1 c a b
                                                        dCE cc = −dca ca − Cab c c.
                                                                          2
                                                                   ​                                                                             ​               ​       ​

30/04/2026, 00:40                                                                                                                  Whitepaper Critical Analysis

            The first term is the dual of dl. The second term is the dual of the bracket.
                                                             ​




            The cotangent-coordinate differential is
                                                                 dCE ua = dba ub + Caj
                                                                                  ​
                                                                                    b
                                                                                       ub c j .
                                                                                              ​                                ​           ​                                    ​                   ​               ​




            The first term is the dual internal differential. The second term is the coadjoint action.
            Now let
                                                                                                      Al = S (l).
                                                                                                               ​




            Let Oa be the linear coordinate corresponding to ea, and let
                      ​                                                                                                                                                     ​




                                                                                                                                    ∂
                                                                                                      θa =                             .
                                                                                                                                   ∂Oa
                                                                                                                                                                ​




                                                                                                                                                            ​




            Then
                                                                 PV(Al ) = S (Oa )⊗Λ(θa ).        ​                                                 ​                   ​




            The differential on polyvectors is the sum of:
             1. the linear vector field induced by dl;
             2. the Lichnerowicz differential induced by the linear Poisson bracket.
                                                                                                       ​




            It satisfies
                                                                            1 c a b
                                                          dPV θc = −dca θa − Cab θ θ,
                                                                            2
                                                                      ​                                                    ​                                        ​                       ​                                   ​




                                                           dPV Oa = dba Ob + Caj
                                                                              b
                                                                              ​
                                                                                 Ob θ j . ​                            ​                       ​                                        ​               ​               ​




            Define
                                                         Φl (ca ) = θa ,
                                                             ​
                                                                                                                                                   Φl (ua ) = Oa .
                                                                                                                                                        ​                           ​                                       ​       ​




            Then
                                                         1 c a b
                                 Φl (dCE cc ) = −dca θa − Cab θ θ = dPV θc = dPV Φl (cc ),
                                                         2
                                     ​       ​                            ​                                ​                       ​                                                                        ​                               ​       ​




            and
                                  Φl (dCE ua ) = dba Ob + Caj
                                         ​       ​   ​
                                                           b
                                                              Ob θj = dPV Oa = dPV Φl (ua ).
                                                                  ​                   ​                            ​                   ​                                                        ​               ​                       ​       ​       ​

            Since both differentials are derivations,
                                                                               Φl dCE = dPV Φl
                                                                                 ​        ​                               ​                   ​




            on the entire algebra.
            The cotangent bracket on the CE side is
                                                                           {ca , ub }cot = δba ,
                                                                                              ​               ​                           ​

            Therefore
                                                              ∙

            as dg P0-algebras.

            This is the universal cotangent CE/PV identity already used in the manuscript in the
            finite-dimensional and coordinate-completed Hamiltonian setting.

            11. The full unreduced theorem
            Apply the universal theorem to
                                                                                     l = pI .                     ​




            Set
                                                         GI = T ∗ [1]pI = pI ⋉ p∨I [1].

            the universal theorem gives

                                                           ∙
                                                          CCE (GI ) ≅ PV(Obsopen (I)).
                                                                    ​         ​                                                    ​                           ​




            For a free P0-factorization algebra, the derived P0-factorization center is computed by
            the Poisson cochain complex:
                                ​                                                                                      ​




                                                P0
                                               Zfact (Obsopen )(I) ≃ PV(Obsopen (I)).

            On generators,
                                                               cα ↦ θ α ,                                  uα ↦ O α .

            Here eα ∈ pI is a primitive cyclic open BV generator, cα is dual to eα , uα is dual to the

            coordinate, and θα is the corresponding constant polyvector field.

            12. Normal ordering to the Hamiltonian sector
            Let
                                                                                          pH ⊂ p
                                                                                              ​




            be the subspace spanned by cyclic words in x, y only.
            For a word w = w(x, y), define
                                                              #x (w) = number of x in w,

                                                              #y (w) = number of y in w.
                                                                ​




            Define the normal-ordering projection
                                                                        N : pH → C[z1 , z2 ]
                                                                                          ​                                ​               ​




            by
                                                                                                  # (w) #y (w)
                                                                N ([w]) = z1 x                         z2      .
                                                                                                                               ​
                                                                                                           ​




                                                                                                                   ​                           ​

            Then
                                                                               N ([xa y b ]) = z1a z2b .                               ​   ​




            Compose with the quotient by constants:
                                                     C[z1 , z2 ] → Aˉ = C[z1 , z2 ]/C ⋅ 1.

            13. N is a Lie algebra map
            Take
                                                        F = [xa y b ],                                                      G = [xc y d ].

            Compute
                                                                                               a−1
                                                                     ∂x F = ∑ xa−1−i y b xi ,
                                                                               ​                                ​




                                                                                               i=0

                                                                                               b−1
                                                                     ∂y F = ∑ y b−1−j xa y j .
                                                                           ​                            ​




                                                                                               j=0

            Similarly,
                                                                                               c−1
                                                                 ∂x G = ∑ xc−1−r y d xr ,
                                                                       ​                                ​




                                                                                               r=0

                                                                                               d−1
                                                                     ∂y G = ∑ y d−1−s xc y s .
                                                                           ​                                ​




                                                                                               s=0

            The necklace bracket is
                                               {F , G}neck = [∂x F ∂y G − ∂y F ∂x G]cyc .
                                                                                   ​            ​                   ​                                  ​   ​   ​




            Apply N . Each term in
                                                                                               ∂x F ∂y G
                                                                                                    ​                   ​




https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa84c-a760-83ea-ae70-183a06ecd2d3                                                                                                         495/510
30/04/2026, 00:40                                                                                       Whitepaper Critical Analysis

            has
                                                                                      (a − 1) + c

            copies of x, and
                                                                                      b + (d − 1)

            copies of y. There are ad such terms. Hence
                                                    N (∂x F ∂y G) = ad z1a+c−1 z2b+d−1 .
                                                             ​                ​                                                    ​               ​




            Similarly,
                                                     N (∂y F ∂x G) = bc z1a+c−1 z2b+d−1 .
                                                                                  ​                                            ​               ​




            Therefore
                                               N {F , G}neck = (ad − bc)z1a+c−1 z2b+d−1 .
                                                                          ​                                                                ​                   ​




            But
                                               {z1a z2b , z1c z2d } = (ad − bc)z1a+c−1 z2b+d−1 .
                                                     ​   ​       ​    ​                                                                ​                   ​




            Thus
                                                     N {F , G}neck = {N (F ), N (G)}.   ​                                                              ​




            By linearity,
                                                                     N : pH → C[z1 , z2 ]       ​                  ​




            is a Lie algebra map.
            After quotienting constants, it gives
                                                                          Nred : pH → h.    ​                  ​       ​




            14. The bubble-sort homotopy
            Define a section
                                                                     i : C[z1 , z2 ] → pH           ​      ​               ​




https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa84c-a760-83ea-ae70-183a06ecd2d3                                                                                                         496/510
30/04/2026, 00:40                                                                                                     Whitepaper Critical Analysis

            by
                                                                      i(z1a z2b ) = [xa y b ].
                                                                                          ​                   ​




            Then
                                                                                          N i = id.

            For a word w = w(x, y), choose the deterministic bubble-sort path
                                                           w = w 0 , w 1 , … , w r = xa y b ,
                                                                              ​                           ​                                 ​




            where each step replaces one occurrence of yx by xy.
            At step s, write
                                                                          ws = As y xBs ,
                                                                                      ​                                 ​                           ​




                                                                      ws+1 = As xyBs .        ​                             ​                           ​




            Define
                                                                                                                       r−1
                                                             H([w]) = − ∑[ψBs As ].                                             ​                           ​       ​       ​




                                                                                                                       s=0


            Since the gauge part of Q is cyclically inner, it vanishes on cyclic classes in this
            calculation. The Koszul part gives
                                                       Q[ψBs As ] = [(xy − yx)Bs As ].
                                                                  ​               ​                                                                                     ​       ​




            Therefore
                                                     Q[ψBs As ] = [As xyBs ] − [As yxBs ].
                                                              ​       ​                                           ​                     ​                           ​                   ​




            Thus
                                 −Q[ψBs As ] = [As yxBs ] − [As xyBs ] = [ws ] − [ws+1 ].
                                                 ​     ​                                          ​                                 ​                           ​                       ​       ​




            Summing over s,
                                         QH([w]) = ∑([ws ] − [ws+1 ]) = [w0 ] − [wr ].
                                                                          ​                           ​                                         ​                                   ​       ​




                                                                  s

            Hence
https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa84c-a760-83ea-ae70-183a06ecd2d3                                                                                                                                          497/510
30/04/2026, 00:40                                                                     Whitepaper Critical Analysis

                                                             QH([w]) = [w] − [xa y b ].

            Since
                                                                iN ([w]) = [xa y b ],

            we obtain
                                                                     QH = id − iN                    ​




            on pH .  ​




            Because Q = 0 on pH , this is also   ​




                                                             QH + HQ = id − iN .                            ​




            This is the explicit chain contraction from noncommutative cyclic words to commutative
            Hamiltonians.

            15. Product centrality homotopy
            Let
                                                     f ∈ C[z1 , z2 ],
                                                                 ​       ​               f = i(f ) ∈ pH .
                                                                                            ​                        ​




            Let F ∈ Obsopen(I). Define the normal-ordered multiplication operation
                                       ​




                                                              Mf (F ) = i(f N (F )).
                                                                     ​




            Here N is extended multiplicatively from primitive cyclic generators to the completed
            symmetric algebra.
            The raw product by f is          ​




                                                                                 f F.
                                                                                  ​




            Since
                                                               N (f F ) = f N (F ),

            we have
                                                               iN (f F ) = Mf (F ).
                                                                             ​                  ​




https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa84c-a760-83ea-ae70-183a06ecd2d3                                                               498/510
30/04/2026, 00:40                                                                                   Whitepaper Critical Analysis

            Therefore
                                                     f F − Mf (F ) = (id − iN )(f F ).
                                                          ​                        ​                                               ​




            Using
                                                                  id − iN = QH + HQ,

            we obtain
                                              f F − Mf (F ) = QH(f F ) + HQ(f F ).
                                                 ​                 ​                                       ​                           ​




            Define
                                                                           Hfprod
                                                                              ,F = H(f F ).
                                                                                        ​                              ​       ​




            Then
                                                     f F − Mf (F ) = QHfprod
                                                      ​
                                                                               prod
                                                                         ,F + Hf ,QF .
                                                                               ​                                           ​               ​   ​




            This is the product-centrality homotopy.
            It is concrete: it bubble-sorts every noncommutative word appearing in f F to its
            normal-ordered representative, and records every adjacent swap as a Q-boundary with
                                                                                                                                                   ​




            one inserted ψ.

            16. Bracket centrality homotopy
            Let
                                                                                       {−, −}P0                ​
                                                                                                                   ​




            be the P0-bracket on Obsopen.
                           ​                                  ​




            Define the reduced Hamiltonian vector-field operation
                                                              Xf (F ) = i({f , N (F )}).
                                                                       ​




            Since N is a Lie algebra map,
                                                              N {f , F }P0 = {f , N (F )}.
                                                                           ​

                                                                                            ​
                                                                                                ​




            Therefore
https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa84c-a760-83ea-ae70-183a06ecd2d3                                                                                             499/510
30/04/2026, 00:40                                                                                     Whitepaper Critical Analysis

                                                                          iN {f , F }P0 = Xf (F ).
                                                                                              ​

                                                                                                       ​
                                                                                                           ​           ​




            Hence
                                             {f , F }P0 − Xf (F ) = (id − iN ){f , F }P0 .
                                                  ​


                                                                  ​
                                                                      ​                   ​                                                     ​


                                                                                                                                                            ​
                                                                                                                                                                ​




            Using
                                                                          id − iN = QH + HQ,

            we get
                                    {f , F }P0 − Xf (F ) = QH{f , F }P0 + HQ{f , F }P0 .
                                         ​

                                                      ​
                                                          ​               ​                                    ​

                                                                                                                           ​
                                                                                                                               ​                        ​

                                                                                                                                                                            ​
                                                                                                                                                                                ​




            Define
                                                                          Hfbr,F = H({f , F }P0 ).
                                                                                      ​                            ​

                                                                                                                               ​
                                                                                                                                   ​        ​




            Then
                                               {f , F }P0 − Xf (F ) = QHfbr,F + Hfbr,QF .
                                                              ​


                                                                              ​
                                                                                  ​               ​                                    ​            ​               ​




            This is the P0-bracket centrality homotopy.
            It says that the unreduced bracket of a boundary Hamiltonian representative with any
            open observable is homotopic to the Hamiltonian vector-field operation prescribed by
            the reduced CE/PV theorem.

            17. Compatibility with factorization
            All maps
                                             N,                   i,                      H,                   Q,                          {−, −}neck                   ​




            act on the cyclic-word factor. They do not alter the compactly supported form α ∈
            Ω∙c (I).
                ​




            Therefore, for an interval inclusion
                                                                                                  j : I ↪ J,

            extension by zero satisfies
https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa84c-a760-83ea-ae70-183a06ecd2d3                                                                                                                          500/510
30/04/2026, 00:40                                                                                                              Whitepaper Critical Analysis

                                                                                       j! N = N j! ,
                                                                                               ​                                                    ​




                                                                                           j! i = ij! ,        ​                            ​




                                                                                       j! H = Hj! ,
                                                                                               ​                                                    ​




                                                                                       j! Q = Qj! ,​                                            ​




                                                             j! {F , G}P0 = {j! F , j! G}P0 .
                                                              ​


                                                                                                           ​
                                                                                                                   ​                    ​                       ​


                                                                                                                                                                        ​
                                                                                                                                                                            ​




            For disjoint intervals I1, … , In ⊂ J ,
                                               ​                  ​




                                                                      n
                                H(F1 ⋯ Fn ) = ∑(−1)∣F1 ∣+⋯+∣Fr−1 ∣ F1 ⋯ H(Fr ) ⋯ Fn .
                                          ​          ​                        ​
                                                                                                                           ​                                ​




                                                                                                                                                                    ​               ​   ​




                                                                  r=1

            Therefore H prod and H br are compatible with disjoint factorization products.
            This proves the centrality homotopies at the factorization-algebra level, not merely at a
            single stalk.

                                                                 ​                               ​               ​                       ​                   ​




https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa84c-a760-83ea-ae70-183a06ecd2d3                                                                                                                   502/510
30/04/2026, 00:40                                                                                       Whitepaper Critical Analysis

                                                    ​        ​     ​                            ​                         ​                                   ​       ​       ​

                                                                       m = a,                                 n = b.

                                                                 ⟨ρa,b , z1m z2n ⟩ = δa,m δb,n .

                                                                   ρ0,0 = z1−1 z2−1 dz1 dz2
                                                                            ​                           ​     ​               ​           ​

                                                    P = Annres (C ⋅ 1) = ⨁ Cρa,b .  ​                                                         ​                   ​       ​

            20. Coadjoint action on principal parts
            Define the coadjoint action by
                                                                 ⟨f ⋅ ρ, g⟩ = −⟨ρ, {f , g}⟩.

            Let
                                                                            p q
                                                             f = z1 z2 ,        ​       ​                   g = z1m z2n .             ​               ​




            Then
https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa84c-a760-83ea-ae70-183a06ecd2d3                                                                                                                        503/510
30/04/2026, 00:40                                                                            Whitepaper Critical Analysis

                                                     {f , g} = (pn − qm)z1p+m−1 z2q+n−1 .                      ​                ​




            Pair with ρa,b :    ​




                                        −⟨ρa,b , {f , g}⟩ = −(pn − qm)δa,p+m−1 δb,q+n−1 .
                                                     ​                                                                      ​           ​




            The residue is nonzero exactly when
                                                     m = a − p + 1,                                n = b − q + 1.

            Substitute:
                                      −(pn − qm) = −(p(b − q + 1) − q(a − p + 1)).

            Expand:
             p(b − q + 1) − q(a − p + 1) = pb − pq + p − qa + pq − q = pb − qa + p − q.

            Therefore
                                         z1p z2q ⋅ ρa,b = −(pb − qa + p − q)ρa−p+1,b−q+1 .
                                             ​   ​           ​                                                                      ​       ​




            Terms with negative indices are zero.
            For z1, set p = 1, q = 0:
                      ​




                                                                 z1 ⋅ ρa,b = −(b + 1)ρa,b+1 .
                                                                   ​         ​                                         ​




            For z2, set p = 0, q = 1:
                      ​




                                                                 z2 ⋅ ρa,b = (a + 1)ρa+1,b .
                                                                       ​             ​                             ​




            These formulas agree with the residue coadjoint computation in the manuscript.
                    main

                                                                  a(bB) = (ab)B,

            22. Bracket of Hamiltonian currents with principal parts

            Let
                                                             Bf (a) = a(t)dt ⊗ i(f ).
                                                                 ​




            Let
                                                                     Θρ (B ) = θB⊗ρ .
                                                                          ​                                ​
                                                                                                           ​




            In the cotangent CE/PV theorem, a function coordinate brackets with a constant vector
            field by evaluation. The Hamiltonian action appears through the Schouten differential:
                                                                         d π O f = Xf .
                                                                                  ​   ​                ​




            For principal parts, the induced bracket is
                                                     {Bf (a), Θρ (B)}P0 ≃ Θf ⋅ρ (aB).
                                                             ​                ​


                                                                                               ​
                                                                                                   ​                   ​       ​




            To verify this, pair both sides with a Hamiltonian test current
                                                                         b(t)dt ⊗ g.

            The left side acts by first applying the Hamiltonian bracket and then the functional ρ:
                                                                 ⟨ρ, {f , g}⟩ B(ab).

            By the coadjoint definition,
                                                             ⟨f ⋅ ρ, g⟩ = −⟨ρ, {f , g}⟩.

            The sign is exactly the sign appearing in the shifted cotangent convention. Therefore the
            right side pairs as
                                                 ⟨f ⋅ ρ, g⟩ (aB)(b) = ⟨f ⋅ ρ, g⟩ B(ab).

            Thus the equality is the coadjoint identity transported to brane currents.
            Also,
                                                             {Θρ (B), Θσ (C)}P0 = 0
                                                                     ​                     ​

                                                                                                               ​
                                                                                                                   ​       ​




            because constant polyvector fields commute.
            This recovers the reduced principal-part current bracket

                                                       {Bf (a), Θρ (B)} = Θf ⋅ρ (aB),
                                                             ​               ​                            ​




                                                             {Θρ (B), Θσ (C)} = 0,
                                                                    ​                      ​




            but now as the Hamiltonian sector of the full cyclic cotangent-center theorem.

            23. The full theorem in final form
            Theorem. Full unreduced stable cyclic BV closed-open center theorem.
            Let
                                                         E = Cx ⊕ Cy ⊕ Cγ ⊕ Cψ

            with
                                            ∣x∣ = ∣y∣ = 0,                           ∣γ∣ = 1,                     ∣ψ∣ = −1.

            Let
                                                                  p = Cyc(T (E))≥1                            ​




            with necklace bracket determined by
                                                        {x, y} = 1,                            {γ, ψ} = 1,

            and differential
                                                                            Qγ = γ 2 ,

                                                                   Qx = γx − xγ,

                                                                   Qy = γy − yγ,

                                                         Qψ = xy − yx + γψ + ψγ.

            Then (p, Q, {−, −}neck ) is a dg Lie algebra.
                                                  ​




            For each interval I ⊂ R, define
                                                                   pI = Ω∙c (I)⊗p,
                                                                        ​              ​          ​




                                                                 Obsopen (I) = S (pI ),
                                                                                 ​                    ​




            and
https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa84c-a760-83ea-ae70-183a06ecd2d3                                                                    507/510
30/04/2026, 00:40                                                                                           Whitepaper Critical Analysis

                                                                           GI = pI ⋉ p∨I [1].
                                                                                       ​                    ​                                    ​




            Then there is a natural isomorphism of locally constant P0-factorization algebras                                                                                        ​




                                                        ∙           P0
                                                       CCE (GI ) ≅ Zfact
                                                              ​          (Obsopen )(I).
                                                                                   ​
                                                                                                                ​




                                                                                                                        ​                                    ​                                       ​




            On generators,
                                                             cα ↦ θ α ,                                                         uα ↦ O α .   ​                               ​           ​




            The reduced Hamiltonian BF source
                                                                          gHam,I = hI ⋉ PI [1]  ​                           ​                        ​




            maps into this full center by:
                                                 ua(t)dt⊗f ⟼ Bf (a) = a(t)dt ⊗ i(f ),
                                                                      ​                                 ​




                                                       cB⊗ρ ⟼ Θρ (B) = θB⊗(ρ∘Nred ) .
                                                                  ​                                 ​


                                                                                                                                                                                             ​
                                                                                                                                                                                                 ​




            The required centrality homotopies are explicit:
                                                                          Hfprod
                                                                             ,F = H(i(f )F ),
                                                                                            ​                                                                            ​




                                                             Hfbr,F = H({i(f ), F }P0 ),
                                                                               ​


                                                                                                                                                                 ​
                                                                                                                                                                     ​                   ​




            where H is the deterministic bubble-sort homotopy
                                                             H([w]) = − ∑[ψBs As ]                                               ​                       ​                       ​




                                                                                                                            s

            satisfying
                                                             QH + HQ = id − iN .

            The Matlis residue identification gives
                                                                                           P ≅ h∨cont ,                                  ​




            with basis
                                             ρa,b = z1−a−1 z2−b−1 dz1 dz2 ,
                                                   ​                       ​                        ​               ​                ​                       a + b > 0,

            and action

30/04/2026, 00:40                                                                   Whitepaper Critical Analysis

                                         z1p z2q ⋅ ρa,b = −(pb − qa + p − q)ρa−p+1,b−q+1 .
                                             ​   ​           ​                                                                ​




            Consequently,
                                                     {Bf (a), Θρ (B)}P0 ≃ Θf ⋅ρ (aB),
                                                                 ​          ​

                                                                                          ​
                                                                                              ​               ​           ​




            and

            24. Why the construction works
            The construction works because every layer is the algebraic shadow of a simple
            operation.
            The variables x, y are noncommutative position coordinates of the brane stack. Their
            necklace bracket is the stable trace version of the canonical matrix bracket. The ghost γ
            encodes infinitesimal conjugation. The variable ψ imposes the moment-map equation
            xy − yx = 0. The differential

                                                         Qψ = xy − yx + γψ + ψγ

            therefore says: impose the constraint and transform it equivariantly.
            Normal ordering
                                                                                        #x(w) #y(w)
                                                                     N ([w]) = z1            z2   ​               ​




            forgets noncommutative ordering and remembers only the commutative Hamiltonian.
            Bubble sorting records exactly how much information was forgotten. Every swap
                                                                        AyxB ⇝ AxyB

            is a Q-boundary because
                                                      Q[ψBA] = [AxyB] − [AyxB].

            Thus the reduced Hamiltonian theorem is not an unexplained quotient. It is a chain
            contraction:
                                                                                id ≃ iN .

https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa84c-a760-83ea-ae70-183a06ecd2d3                                                                        509/510
30/04/2026, 00:40                                                                        Whitepaper Critical Analysis

            The cotangent CE/PV theorem works because T ∗[1]l is the universal Lie algebra whose
            CE coordinates are precisely functions and vector fields on l∨ :
                                                             ua ↦ O a ,
                                                               ​                ​              ca ↦ θ a .

            The Matlis module appears because the continuous dual of the formal disk is not
            polynomial. The dual basis to Taylor monomials is extracted by residue currents:
                                                             ⟨ρa,b , z1m z2n ⟩ = δa,m δb,n .
                                                                    ​       ​        ​                ​      ​




            That is why principal parts are the cotangent modes and why the polynomial Ψa,b
            classes cannot replace them. The manuscript’s separation rule already records that Ψa,b
            are PBW special-fibre labels while ρa,b are Matlis principal-part coadjoint data.
                                                                                                                              ​




                                                                        ​




            The full theorem is therefore a single mechanism:
             cyclic BV primitive algebra                           ⇒                shifted cotangent CE algebra        ⇒   polyvecto

            This supplies the missing unreduced central operations and their explicit homotopies.
